# Changelog

All notable changes to the OpenCLAW-AKOS project are documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## Policy

Release lane definitions, SemVer judgment, `[Unreleased]` working-line discipline, and the customer-invisible versioning posture live at [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Tech/System Owner/canonicals/SOP-RELEASE_TAXONOMY_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Tech/System%20Owner/canonicals/SOP-RELEASE_TAXONOMY_001.md). Three release lanes are independent (methodology `major.minor` in [`LOGIC_CHANGE_LOG.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/canonicals/LOGIC_CHANGE_LOG.md) + `D-IH-*` rows; HLK vault folder path `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/`; openclaw-akos SemVer + this file + git tag); bumping one lane does not imply bumping any other. See `SOP-RELEASE_TAXONOMY_001` §1 + §5 for the lane definitions and cross-lane non-implication rules, §2 + §3 for tag criteria and SemVer judgment, and §6 for the customer-invisible versioning posture (load-bearing per operator intent).

---

- **I95 fast CI hardening (2026-06-08)**: `pre-commit-fast` installs `requirements.txt` + `pip install -e .`; `/prompts/assemble` uses `sys.executable` instead of Windows-only `py` (fixes API smoke on Linux GHA).

- **I95 two-plane data integrity remediation (2026-06-08; D-IH-95-G; DATA-02/DATA-05)**: Holistic guard stack so Plane-1 (`validate_hlk` CSV+Pydantic) cannot green while Plane-2 (Supabase mirror DDL + row counts) drifts silently. NEW [`validate_csv_column_alignment.py`](scripts/validate_csv_column_alignment.py) (every data row = `len(FIELDNAMES)` + DATE cols + required `org_uuid` on baseline roles), [`validate_pydantic_mirror_enum_ssot.py`](scripts/validate_pydantic_mirror_enum_ssot.py) (Pydantic enum ⊆ migration CHECK; parses `IN (...)` + `ANY ARRAY` forms), [`validate_mirror_emit_contract.py`](scripts/validate_mirror_emit_contract.py) (CSV emit-eligible row count = INSERT count + workflow wiring), [`emit_mirror_delete_reconcile.py`](scripts/emit_mirror_delete_reconcile.py) (orphan shrink; default ON in CI). Wired into [`validate_hlk.py`](scripts/validate_hlk.py) + live DATA-02/DATA-05 probes in [`dataops_quality_check.py`](scripts/dataops_quality_check.py). Migration [`20260608211500_i95_mirror_enum_parity_design_pattern_class.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260608211500_i95_mirror_enum_parity_design_pattern_class.sql). **Data remediation surfaced by guards**: quoted unescaped commas in `PEOPLE_DESIGN_PATTERN_REGISTRY` (`pattern_dual_register_internal_external`, `pattern_area_buildout`); minted missing `org_uuid` for RevOps Manager / RevOps Analyst / CRO (`d4e5f6a7-7070-4bbb-d005-*`). **OPS-95-1 closed** after full-sync verified SSOT counts. Report: [`two-plane-data-integrity-remediation-2026-06-08.md`](docs/wip/planning/95-canonical-articulation-model/reports/two-plane-data-integrity-remediation-2026-06-08.md).

- **I95 process-design singularity milestone — capability + process de-densification (2026-06-08; D-IH-95-I; BT-09)**: The capability + process layers were a 1:1 seed-shadow; de-densified **both** in lockstep. **`CAPABILITY_REGISTRY` 1,119 → 93** stable bearer-agnostic capabilities (area-by-area, cross-area reconciled; `capability_tier` + `l1_domain` + `definition` added, `bearer_class` removed; code-symbols → component / tools → substrate / subjects → topics) + `CAPABILITY_CONFIDENCE_REGISTRY` synced (93). **`process_list` 1,207 → 496** curated processes; **583** task-grain rows **demoted not deleted** into a NEW [`BUILDOUT_BACKLOG.csv`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/dimensions/BUILDOUT_BACKLOG.csv) registry ([`akos/hlk_buildout_backlog_csv.py`](akos/hlk_buildout_backlog_csv.py) + [`scripts/validate_buildout_backlog_registry.py`](scripts/validate_buildout_backlog_registry.py); `validate_capability_registry` now accepts `process_list ∪ backlog`). Six area collapse maps + synthesis + process-list inventory under [`l2-collapse-maps/`](docs/wip/intelligence/canonical-articulation-model-2026-06-05/l2-collapse-maps/). Downstream FK repair: `USE_CASE_ARCHIVE`, `AIC_CAPABILITY_IMPLEMENTATION_MATRIX`, `ADVISER_ENGAGEMENT_DISCIPLINES`, 2 capability realizations. KM Officer seat activated; `TOPIC_REGISTRY` facets + 19 governed rows + `topic_class` retired. `validate_hlk` OVERALL PASS throughout (commits `c6d0160`/`2302392`/`ee5fa4b`). The quality ceiling the de-densify sweeps target; full **v3.2** reserved for the I95 graph cutover + 8-area articulation sweep.

- **Brand-domain + naming governance harmonized into one composed path (2026-05-31; D-IH-86-FK)**: Closes the gap where deciding a brand name or a domain fell *between* three areas that each owned a piece but never composed them — brand architecture (Marketing), trademark clearance (Legal), domain topology (Tech). The diagnosis (operator-confirmed) was a **harmonization** gap, not missing capability. Four fixes, operator-approved at the canonical-CSV/SOP/doctrine gate and executed via the active I86 coordinator ahead of the I92 charter (so the ratifying decision is **D-IH-86-FK**, not the placeholder D-IH-92-F — the decision validator FK-checks the initiating initiative and I92 is not yet chartered): (1) NEW composition SOP [`SOP-BRAND_DOMAIN_NAMING_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/SOP-BRAND_DOMAIN_NAMING_001.md) + paired process row `tbi_mkt_prc_brand_domain_naming_001` — a CMO-accountable decision flow (brand fit → trademark clearance → domain availability/topology → make/buy/broker) that *orchestrates* the three silos' own SOPs/registries instead of duplicating them. (2) Deduped the duplicate Legal capability rows in [`CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.csv`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/dimensions/CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.csv) — legacy `CAP-THI-LEGAL-DTP-303` is now `deprecated`, canonical `CAP-HOL-LGL-PRC-TRADEMARK-NAMING-GOVERNANCE-001` annotated. (3) Added a **make/buy/outsource frame** (the operator's "do we have it, build it, or buy it?" question, mechanized) to [`HOLISTIKA_CAPABILITY_DOCTRINE.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/canonicals/HOLISTIKA_CAPABILITY_DOCTRINE.md) §8.5 (coordinated with I82; purely additive). (4) NEW [`brand-naming-craft`](.cursor/skills/brand-naming-craft/SKILL.md) agent skill composing the three silos + the make/buy frame + the 2026 naming bar. **RACI ratified**: Accountable = **CMO** (area-director level, *not* escalated to the Founder — establishing the reusable principle that a thin area keeps accountability at its director level rather than bloating sibling areas); Founder retains the new-mark trademark-filing gate. The separate domain-*strategy* CAPABILITY_REGISTRY row is deferred to the Capability Curator via I82's own flow (real rating, not a seed). **Mechanical evidence**: `validate_hlk.py` OVERALL PASS (process items 1187; DECISION_REGISTER + CAPABILITY_REGISTRY PASS; one advisory pairing WARN expected — the SOP's automation is the existing validators, not a new runbook). **Operator-gated follow-ups**: commit + mirror parity (`process_list_mirror` + `decision_register_mirror` upserts after commit; CAPABILITY_REGISTRY has no mirror). Worked memo: [`brand-domain-options-and-governance-gap.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/brand-domain-naming-2026-05-31/brand-domain-options-and-governance-gap.md).

- **I86 Wave R+5 C6 — Research area LOGIC CHANGE: the CORPINT lifecycle becomes the area's spine (2026-05-29)**: Founder-ratified novel framing (**D-IH-75-G**) that reorganizes the Research area around *how information actually flows* — **acquire → process → store ⇄ recall → share, guarded by protect** — instead of around a flat list of disciplines. In plain terms: the six lifecycle stages are now the backbone; the four disciplines (Methodology / Intelligence / Diagnosis / Validation) are the **crafts** that act on each stage; and the points where the flow crosses into another area (Store→Tech, Share→Marketing/Brand, Recall→Tech, Protect→People/Ethics+Compliance) are now first-class **named hand-offs** — because Holistika is the sum of its areas. **What it fixes**: three lifecycle stages (dissemination, retrieval, protection) previously had no owner and fell through the cracks; they now do. **PROTECT** (counter-intelligence — guarding our sources + crown-jewel knowledge) is established as a first-class stage with a cross-area ownership triad (**D-IH-75-H**); a full Counter-Intelligence discipline is forward-chartered for the People co-owners to ratify. **What was built** (production-grade — no empty folders): NEW [`RESEARCH_LIFECYCLE_DOCTRINE.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Research/canonicals/RESEARCH_LIFECYCLE_DOCTRINE.md) (the area's operating logic, externally grounded in the intelligence cycle + knowledge-management lifecycle + competitive/counter-intelligence — evidence base §8 + paired source ledger [`research-lifecycle-doctrine-2026-05-29/source-ledger.csv`](docs/wip/intelligence/research-lifecycle-doctrine-2026-05-29/source-ledger.csv)); 4 discipline-root README indexes + 6 capability-seeded technique sub-area indexes (`Research/Methodology/{Pillars,Techniques,Deep-Research}/` + `Research/Intelligence/{HUMINT,OSINT,Matrix}/`); the **6 empty technique husks** under `Admin/O5-1/Research/` (placeholder `.gitkeep` folders since the 2026-04-01 scaffold) **deleted** and rebuilt under the disciplines from the real capability-registry rows; upgraded area [`README.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Research/README.md) + area [`RESEARCH_AREA_CHARTER.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Research/canonicals/RESEARCH_AREA_CHARTER.md) (§4 husk-migration superseded) + all 4 discipline charters (sub-folders realized + lifecycle ownership added). Governance: +2 [`DECISION_REGISTER.csv`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/DECISION_REGISTER.csv) rows (D-IH-75-G/H); [`PRECEDENCE.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/PRECEDENCE.md) row for the doctrine; Wave R+5 plan +C6 row + §5.5 thin-stages closed; legacy migration proposal rewritten (technique-folder rows resolved-by-rebuild; SOP/register moves remain gated). **Single-ownership preserved**: Research documents *its side* of each cross-area join; reciprocal edits to Marketing/Tech/People canonicals are governed follow-ups per KB-integrity I81, not imposed this commit. **Still gated (separate follow-up)**: physically moving the legacy SOPs + IntelligenceOps register CSV into the new tree (the SSOT-path change ripples into PRECEDENCE + validators + cursor-rule globs). **Mechanical evidence**: `validate_research_action.py --source-ledger` PASS (6 rows); `validate_hlk.py` OVERALL PASS; `validate_research_radar.py --self-test` PASS.

- **I86 Wave R+2 Doctrine Rewrite Commit 2 (Pydantic chassis 4-base + 1-overlay rewrite; sha `57b9d24`; 3 files / +814 / -85; chassis-only, validator + runbook follow at Commit 3)**: Second commit of the 7-commit Wave R+2 doctrine-rewrite tranche. Closes the chassis-loop opened at Commit 1 (5cd9793 doctrine rewrite). Files in scope (3): `akos/hlk_collaborator_share.py` rewritten — **VALID_SHARE_PATTERNS** frozenset switched from 3-shape enum (`deep_partner_65_35` / `orchestration_broker_thin_margin` / `custom`) to **4-base enum** (`bd_intro_only` / `joint_venture_aventure` / `consulting_direct` / `deep_partner_65_35`); **VALID_SHARE_OVERLAYS** frozenset NEW (`bd_commission_overlay` as the only initial overlay, stackable on `bd_intro_only` OR `consulting_direct` base only per CS-09); **VALID_METHODOLOGY_READINESS** frozenset NEW (`methodology_trained` / `methodology_in_progress` / `methodology_naive` / `methodology_not_applicable`); **VALID_OVERRIDE_KINDS** extended `+overlay_pct_deviation` (3rd override class for overlay percentage excursions); **VALID_OVERLAY_BASE_PAIRINGS** lookup NEW (lookup dict encoding the legal overlay-base combinations for CS-09 audit); **COLLABORATOR_SHARE_REGISTRY_FIELDNAMES** extended 17 → 20 cols (`+share_overlay` + `+methodology_readiness` + `+parallel_invoice_stream_indicator`); Pydantic `CollaboratorShareRegistryRow` model updated to enforce the new 4-base enum + optional `share_overlay` Literal + required `methodology_readiness` Literal + required `parallel_invoice_stream_indicator` bool + `overlay_pct_deviation` accepted as 3rd `override_kind` value in `CollaboratorRateOverrideRow`. The `parallel_invoice_stream_indicator` field (ratified at D-IH-86-EK) encodes the operator's *"some engagements bill the partner-collaborator separately on a parallel invoice stream"* framing — a boolean signal that the engagement's revenue does NOT flow through Holistika's invoice to the customer (the partner invoices the customer directly), changing the TRUE-MARGIN denominator for share computation. `methodology_readiness` (D-IH-86-EL) encodes the operator's *"i'd never accept 65/35 unless the collaborator is methodology-trained"* gating — drives CS-09's coherence audit. `tests/test_hlk_collaborator_share.py` extended 65 → 106 tests (+41 new): coverage for the 4-base enum validation + `bd_commission_overlay` overlay validation + `methodology_readiness` validation + `parallel_invoice_stream_indicator` validation + `overlay_pct_deviation` override-kind validation + `VALID_OVERLAY_BASE_PAIRINGS` lookup correctness + frozenset immutability invariants. `files-modified.csv` +3 rows tracking the chassis-loop commit's scope. Mechanical evidence: `py -m pytest tests/test_hlk_collaborator_share.py` → 106/106 PASS; ReadLints → 0 errors across both in-scope files. Validator self-test failure observed at this commit IS the expected pre-existing ImportError from Commit 1 (validator still imports the pre-rewrite 3-shape enum symbols) — deferred to Commit 3 per tranche charter §"Closing-loop ordering" (the validator rewrite is Commit 3's atomic scope; the chassis-only nature of Commit 2 means the validator gap stays open by design until Commit 3 closes the loop). Decision lineage: D-IH-86-EK (parallel_invoice_stream_indicator ratified at this commit) + D-IH-86-EL (methodology_readiness ratified at this commit) reference D-IH-86-EJ parent (Commit 1 4-base + 1-overlay model ratification).

- **I86 Wave R+2 Doctrine Rewrite Commit 1 (COLLABORATOR_SHARE_DOCTRINE.md full rewrite: 3-shape enum → 4-base + 1-overlay model + methodology-readiness axis + CS-09 overlay-base coherence + supersede chain D-IH-86-DE/DF/EG → D-IH-86-EJ/EK/EL; status: charter reset; doctrine-only commit, chassis + validator + governance authoring + registries follow in Commits 2-7)**: Full rewrite of the 13th Quality Fabric specialty doctrine prompted by the 13/05 SUEZ customer-meeting transcript surfacing the operator's actual commercial philosophy as **4 base patterns + 1 stackable overlay**, NOT the prior 3-shape enum. The pre-rewrite `share_pattern = orchestration_broker_thin_margin` was an architectural invention that mis-encoded the SUEZ deal as Holistika-takes-6%; the operator's verbatim 2026-05-22 framing — *"i'd never accept to take over the operations of a business for less than 65% and every collaborator even tentatives ones even Websitz knows it"* + *"what about 15% of business development that i give to people when they don't work but just come and give us a project and work as business developer/account mgr at least for the beginning, getting their share over the margin so that they have an incentive to bring good deals?"* + *"we need to get paid from our own project like any consultancy does"* — names the SUEZ shape as `consulting_direct + bd_commission_overlay` (Holistika gets 100% of project revenue minus 15% BD commission overlay routed to Aïsha on the BENEFITS pool per the TRUE-MARGIN formula). The `custom` pattern is ALSO removed (doctrinal escape hatch that prevented mechanical validation); two new patterns are added — `bd_intro_only` (pure 85/15 BD/intro-only collaborator) and `joint_venture_aventure` (peer-equivalent 50/50 co-delivery aventure for founder-peer partnerships). Methodology-readiness axis adds a new gating dimension that prevents the "35% compromise" failure mode the operator named verbatim (*"that's also there what's also there is me compromising for that 35% and i don't lie ... we lose value if we don't have things ready because in reality A was correct"*) — when a collaborator's `methodology_readiness` is `methodology_naive`, `deep_partner_65_35` becomes structurally inappropriate and the correct shape is `consulting_direct + bd_commission_overlay`. Doctrine `status:` RESET to `charter` per `D-IH-86-EM`; the pre-rewrite Stage-1 active-promotion (D-IH-86-DF) is superseded; re-promotion gates require ≥ 2 of 4 base patterns + ≥ 1 overlay value exercised cleanly in lived practice (D-IH-86-EO forward-allocated). **Files (1 modified; 8 surgical patches)**: (1) **MODIFIED `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/canonicals/COLLABORATOR_SHARE_DOCTRINE.md`** — 8 surgical patches preserving overall structure while replacing the load-bearing sections: **Patch 1** frontmatter (`last_review: 2026-05-26` + `last_review_decision_id: D-IH-86-EJ` + `methodology_version_at_review: v3.2` + `ratifying_decisions:` extended with `D-IH-86-EJ/EK/EL/EM/EN`). **Patch 2** opening epigraph (added 4-base + 1-overlay framing + operator's extending framing quote + rewrite description). **Patch 3** rewrite of **§2.3** "Three economic shapes → Four base patterns + one stackable overlay" — full enum replacement: `deep_partner_65_35` preserved (65/35 default; deep-partner anchor); `bd_intro_only` NEW (85/15 default; pure BD/intro-only collaborator); `joint_venture_aventure` NEW (50/50 default; peer-equivalent co-delivery aventure for founder-peer partnerships); `consulting_direct` NEW (100/0 default; pure consultancy with no collaborator OR can stack with `bd_commission_overlay` for the SUEZ shape); plus 1 stackable overlay `bd_commission_overlay` (15% default; applied to base pattern's BENEFITS pool per TRUE-MARGIN formula). Removes `orchestration_broker_thin_margin` + `custom` patterns explicitly (supersede rationale documented inline; per-row migration via Commit 5 SUPERSEDE SQL). **Patch 4** insertion of NEW **§2.4 Methodology-readiness axis** — 4-value enum (`methodology_trained` / `methodology_in_progress` / `methodology_naive` / `methodology_not_applicable`) gating which `share_pattern` values are appropriate per collaborator; coherence violations fail CS-04 at WARN AND require explicit `bd_commission_overlay` retrofit OR pattern downgrade BEFORE engagement charters. **Patch 5** rewrite of **§3.2** "orchestration_broker SUEZ projection → consulting_direct + bd_commission_overlay SUEZ corrected" — full per-row math illustrating 15% BD commission overlay on the BENEFITS pool with corrected SUEZ values (Holistika 85% × BENEFITS = principal share; Aïsha 15% × BENEFITS = BD overlay share); plus NEW **§3.3** `bd_intro_only` worked example (operator 22/05 framing); NEW **§3.4** `joint_venture_aventure` worked example (forward-charter; first instantiation TBD); NEW **§3.5** "The removed `custom` shape — what replaces it" (reclassification + doctrine amendment routes). **Patch 6** rewrite of **§6** validator check table — CS-03 + CS-04 4-way branching on `share_pattern` × `share_overlay`; NEW **CS-09 overlay-base coherence audit** check (overlay value membership + sibling base row existence + overlay+base pairing matrix per `VALID_OVERLAY_BASE_PAIRINGS`); rewritten CS-08 enum-validity check (3→4 values; `orchestration_broker_thin_margin` + `custom` no longer valid; FAIL ramp); INFO ramp reset to charter at rewrite per D-IH-86-EM with re-promotion gates documented. **Patch 7** rewrite of **§11 Cross-references** decision lineage — DA/DB/DC/DD preserved (DB formula reused with adjusted default-anchor per pattern; DC clause table unchanged); DE superseded by EJ at 2026-05-27; DF superseded by EK; EG superseded by EL; EH + EI preserved (commercial-pattern-recoding unaffected); NEW EJ (doctrine FULL REWRITE) + EK (re-active-promotion reserved) + EL (SUEZ recommercialization reserved) + EM (validator INFO ramp reset) + EN (methodology-readiness axis gating) + EO (Stage-1 re-active-promotion gate reserved). **Patch 8** rewrite of **§9 Promotion criteria** + **§10 Self-discipline rules for agents** — §9 charter→active gates require ≥ 2 of 4 base patterns + ≥ 1 overlay exercised cleanly + methodology-readiness gate compliance; §10 expanded from 6 rules to 8 rules (NEW Rule 1: resolve `share_pattern` × `share_overlay` × `methodology_readiness` triple BEFORE drafting; NEW Rule 2: default per pattern NOT per row with the 4 per-pattern anchors documented; NEW Rule 7: never compromise to bridge a methodology gap citing the 22/05 operator framing as the load-bearing reference). **Mechanical evidence (pre-commit)**: `py scripts/validate_collaborator_share.py --self-test` PASS (chassis still on 3-value enum; intentional — Commit 2 syncs chassis to new 4+1 enum); `py scripts/synthesis_before_tranche_check.py --check-charter <tranche-charter>` PASS=5/WARN=1 (SYN-07 multi-commit; dispositioned `scope-extend` at tranche-charter authoring time)/FAIL=0/INFO=1; (b) Two SUEZ engagement_ids per CS-03 single-pattern-per-engagement validator constraint surfaced at this commit: `ENG-SUEZ-WEBUY-2026` carrying `orchestration_broker_thin_margin` interim splits 6/47/47 across `HOL-CORP` (broker margin 6%) + `POI-FOUNDER-MARK-I-2026` (Founder Mark-I senior FR founder-principal mid-band 120 EUR/h) + `GOI-PRT-EFA-2026` (Creacion Et Joie SL / EFA Académie organisation senior FR partner-business-development mid-band 100 EUR/h); `ENG-SUEZ-WEBUY-2026-AISHA-CONT` carrying `deep_partner_65_35` 65/35 Holistika/Aïsha continuity slice (POI-PRT-EFA-LEAD-2026; partner-operator-continuity mid-band 60 EUR/h). All 4 SHARE_REGISTRY rows at `status=draft` (reversible until SUEZ commercial-close + Aïsha sub-contract sign). (c) 10 HOLISTIKA_VENDOR_SERVICES_BILLED rows seeded (one per service class per doctrine §2.2 defaults; VBILL-SUEZ-DATAOPS-001 carries `bill_mode_decision_id=D-IH-86-EI` flipping `dataops_engineering` from `in_kind` default to `billed` per primary-deliverable rule codified at this commit — dataops IS the SUEZ POC value-creating scope, not cross-cutting infra; 560h × 80 EUR = 44 800 EUR draft per commercial-schedule-c Variant C estimate). (d) 3 COLLABORATOR_MARKET_RATE_REFERENCE seed rows for CS-06 band audit: `rate_fr_partner_operator_continuity_mid` + `rate_fr_founder_principal_senior` + `rate_fr_partner_business_development_senior`. (e) Cobranded FR cover mail at `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/01-operator-pack/cover-email-2026-05-27.fr.md` addressing SUEZ rep by name + naming Aïsha continuity role with AL-restricted access explicit. (f) 2-page architecture addendum FR PDF source at `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/02-customer-pack/architecture-addendum.fr.md` covering Phase 1/2/3 + Aïsha-led continuity + SUEZ CTO office replicability + ERP-engagement-governance UX 3-surface citation; `scripts/render_suez_engagement_pdfs.py` extended with `architecture_addendum` surface entry for downstream WeasyPrint render. (g) 5 SUEZ-specific operational `CAPABILITY_REGISTRY.csv` rows authored per D-IH-82-V surface convention: `CAP-HOL-DATAOPS-SUEZ-LIBELLE-GENERATOR-001` (customer_dashboard; Phase 1 Excel + Power Query libellé generator per 5-component naming rule × 6 categories); `CAP-HOL-DATAOPS-SUEZ-CATEGORY-ACCOUNT-MAPPING-001` (customer_dashboard + erp_workflow_join; catégorie ↔ compte comptable mapping engine bridging PR libellé to SAP); `CAP-HOL-MADEIRA-SUEZ-EMAIL-EXTRACTION-001` (customer_dashboard + erp_workflow_join; lifecycle=planned; Phase 2 email-to-structured-PR extraction lightweight web app); `CAP-HOL-DATAOPS-SUEZ-DISPUTE-REGISTER-001` (customer_dashboard + operator_dashboard; litige prevention + dispute register module); `CAP-HOL-DATAOPS-SUEZ-USAGE-DASHBOARD-001` (customer_dashboard; auto-generated monthly Power BI usage dashboard). Total CAPABILITY_REGISTRY: 1097 → 1102 rows. (2) **NEW `scripts/synthesis_before_tranche_check.py`** (~730 lines) — paired per-tranche sweep runbook implementing the doctrine's `compose_SYNTHESIS()` function. Module structure: minimal YAML frontmatter parser `_parse_yaml_frontmatter` (no external deps; supports key:value + inline-list `[a,b,c]` + bullet-list under `key:` shape + quoted strings + booleans; delimited by `---`/`---`) + `_charter_from_dict` coercion to SynthesisTrancheCharter Pydantic model with sensible defaults + **10 dimension probes** (`_probe_audience` FAIL when empty / `_probe_channel` INFO when empty acceptable for J-OP-only / `_probe_scenario` INFO when empty acceptable for internal-governance / `_probe_brand_register` WARN when unset / `_probe_governance` FAIL when ratifying_decisions empty / `_probe_erp_surface` WARN for engagement+external N/A elsewhere when empty / `_probe_atomicity` WARN on multi-commit / `_probe_reversibility` FAIL when rationale missing / `_probe_closing_loop` FAIL when test missing / `_probe_recipient_fallback` WARN for external_deliverable N/A elsewhere when empty) wired into `_DIMENSION_PROBE_DISPATCH` dict (exact 10-key coverage of VALID_DIMENSION_CODES; runbook self-test verifies coverage + orphan-free) + `sweep_tranche(charter)` iterates fire-set + dispatches probes + assembles findings + summary with PASS/WARN/FAIL/INFO/N/A counts + `synthesis_complete = (fail_count == 0)` boolean + `inline_ratify_gates_open = fail_count + warn_count` + `render_report_markdown(charter, findings, summary)` emits §3-mechanical-evidence-shaped Markdown body (YAML frontmatter with `intellectual_kind: synthesis_before_tranche_report` + Tranche charter section + Summary section + Per-dimension findings table with pipe-escape + 5-option Disposition workflow section). CLI shape: `--self-test` (always-on chassis check) / `--check-charter <path>` (read frontmatter from markdown file + sweep + optionally emit report) / `--tranche-id`+`--tranche-class` (inline CLI mode for ad-hoc sweeps) / `--emit-report` + `--reports-dir` (write report to disk at `synthesis-check-<slug>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md`). Default mode (no args, no charter) routes to self-test. Per `akos-executable-process-catalog.mdc` Rule 1: this runbook is the AC-AUTOMATION half of the SOP+runbook pair; SOP at Commit 2c-a carries the AC-HUMAN walkthrough. Reports default to `docs/wip/planning/86-initiative-cluster-execution-coordinator/reports/` for governance-class sweeps; engagement-class sweeps redirect via `--reports-dir` per `akos-planning-traceability.mdc`. Codifies the verbatim economic model: *"65% for us, 35% for Aïsha out of the margin and that means we need to act as consultants and partners but we at holistika we treat our partners as B2B, hence we propose a plethora of services we take for granted we'll do and delegate whatever process we may have agreed with the partner to them ... websitz per example was asked to support no mktops as they were a mkt agency themselves on top of bringing us customers to the table."* **Files (9 net-new + 1 modified; all atomic)**: (1) **NEW `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/canonicals/COLLABORATOR_SHARE_DOCTRINE.md`** (~370 lines; 11 sections — Purpose / Economic architecture (TRUE-MARGIN formula + 5-CSV chassis + default `bill_mode` per Holistika service class table) / Worked example with 65/35 math on €100k engagement / External research grounding (OECD DEMPE TPG 2022 + Goldscheider 25% Rule 2002 + Hennart 1988 + Yan & Gray 1994) / Internal precedent grounding (D-IH-73-J + Bâtard 2020 + Websitz + Aïsha POI-PRT-EFA-LEAD-2026) / Drift gate behaviour (7-check validator CS-01..07 with INFO→FAIL ramp) / Cadence / Anti-patterns A-F / Promotion criteria charter→active→FAIL / Self-discipline rules / Cross-references). - **I86 Wave R+1 P2 Commit 3-c — Wave R closure UAT v2 amendment as PWF_GOVERNANCE worked-example birth artifact + governance quartet completion (D-IH-86-CZ; D-IH-86-CW/CX/CY ratified; OPS-86-22 closed + OPS-86-23 amended + OPS-86-24/25 minted)**: Third + final commit of the I86 Wave R+1 P2 governance trio (Commit 1 = Wave R+1 P1 UAT_DISCIPLINE promotion at sha `d0880c6` via D-IH-86-CW; Commit 2 = Wave R+1 P2 Commit 3-a/3-b at sha `<3a-sha>`/`<3b-sha>` via D-IH-86-CX/CY) carries the **closing-loop pattern realisation** as the load-bearing structural claim: the `scripts/validate_uat_report.py` validator minted at Commit 1 emitted finding code `UAT-FM-11-PWF-WITHOUT-RATIONALE` against the Wave R closure UAT report on first run — a literal field-test signal that the new validator catches the exact gap it was designed to catch (the same Wave R close that established the 11-section UAT bar was authored before the structured rationale shape existed). Commit 3-c brings Wave R into compliance and closes the validator-field-test-closing-loop in the same wave-close window, demonstrating the pattern future agents inherit (validator catches gap on first run + amend in same window + cite finding as field-test signal). **Wave R UAT v2 amendment** (`docs/wip/planning/86-initiative-cluster-execution-coordinator/reports/uat-wave-r-closure-2026-05-24.md`): (a) `last_review:` `2026-05-24` -> `2026-05-25`; (b) `verdict_history` block added (v1 legacy-string-rationale entry + v2 structured `monitoring-obligation` PWFFollowupRationale entry); (c) `verdict_followup_rationale` block inserted per `PWFFollowupRationale` Pydantic shape (`followup_class: monitoring-obligation` + `closure_target: Wave R+2 close` + `owner: System Owner` + `closure_decision_id_target: D-IH-86-CZ` + multi-line notes covering OPS-81-22 Stripe MCP audit + OPS-86-22 partial-close + OPS-86-23 backlog carve); (d) `ratifying_decisions:` extended with `D-IH-86-CW`, `D-IH-86-CX`, `D-IH-86-CY`, `D-IH-86-CZ`; (e) `linked_canonicals:` extended with `UAT_DISCIPLINE.md` + `PASS_WITH_FOLLOWUP_GOVERNANCE_DISCIPLINE.md`; (f) `linked_runbooks:` extended with `scripts/validate_uat_report.py` + `scripts/validate_pwf_governance.py`; (g) §12 *Amendment log v2* subsection added (v1 -> v2 mechanical delta + why-no-verdict-flip + field-test-signal-closure-loop + forward-charters into Wave R+1 closure + I88 Bundle C). No body content change in §1-§11 (verdict + closure-criteria + mechanical-evidence + per-dimension findings + D-IH-86-D 4-signal cross-check + SOP+runbook pair + risk-closures + decision-close-outs + registry-edits + verdict-checklist all preserved as authored at Wave R close). **Why `monitoring-obligation` class**: PWF taxonomy distinguishes deferred-work-with-tracker (requires `_trackers/<slug>.md` file) from monitoring-obligation (OPS_REGISTER rows serve as tracker); Wave R's 3 open OPS rows (OPS-81-22 + OPS-86-22 + OPS-86-23) fit monitoring-obligation naturally without creating empty `_trackers/` files. **OPS_REGISTER 4-row delta in same commit**: `OPS-86-22` `status: open -> closed` `closed_at: 2026-05-25` (3-of-4 UAT artifacts closed via D-IH-86-CW; 1-of-4 MKTOPS-side `mktops_campaign_quality_check.py` carved to `OPS-86-25`); `OPS-86-23` amended (DIM-06 10 findings carved to `OPS-86-24` per migration posture; DIM-10 4 sub-findings closed via D-IH-86-CY; remaining 16 findings = DIM-04 8 + DIM-05 8); `OPS-86-24` minted (10 closed-initiative UAT-class-completeness findings; forward-only per migration posture; quarterly review cadence); `OPS-86-25` minted (MKTOPS forward-charter; closes when `I-NN-MKTOPS-OPERATIONALISATION` activates). **DECISION_REGISTER**: `D-IH-86-CZ` appended (Wave R UAT amendment ratification + PWF_GOVERNANCE worked-example #1); decisions table post-commit = 420 total (418 active + 2 superseded) per `validate_decision_register.py` PASS. **Why no D-IH-86-CU/CV slot mints**: pre-commit plan considered minting CU (regression-sweep ratification) + CV (index-sweep ratification) as parallel quartet shells; rejected because (1) no sweep-discipline ratification needed in Wave R+1 (regression-sweep + index-sweep doctrines minted at Wave M+N respectively; not amended); (2) D-IH-86-CY already covers the only sweep change (DIM-10 probe-correctness fix); (3) Wave R+1 has not closed yet — wave-close sweeps fire at wave-close not mid-wave. Trio + amendment (CW + CX + CY + CZ) carries all required governance load without inflating decision register with empty governance shells. **Decision-log narrative** (`docs/wip/planning/86-initiative-cluster-execution-coordinator/decision-log.md`): Round 7 (D-IH-86-CW UAT_DISCIPLINE promotion + Bundle B Strand 2 closure note) + Round 8 (D-IH-86-CX PWF_GOVERNANCE mint + D-IH-86-CY DIM-10 fix + D-IH-86-CZ Wave R v2 amendment + why-no-CU/CV justification + cross-cluster note on second compounding cycle + closing-loop pattern reservation as `pattern_validator_field_test_closing_loop` after third confirmed instantiation per `akos-people-discipline-of-disciplines.mdc` RULE 1). **All gates PASS pre-commit**: `scripts/validate_uat_report.py` on Wave R amended report PASS (UAT-FM-11 cleared) — **required a parser bug-fix** during validation: the hand-rolled flat-only YAML parser at `scripts/validate_uat_report.py:_parse_frontmatter` could not read the structured `verdict_followup_rationale:` PWF block (it stored the key as an empty list when the value was a nested dict, causing `not rationale` to evaluate True and UAT-FM-11 to fire). Upgraded the parser to try PyYAML first (nested-dict capable; aligns parse-result with sister validator `scripts/validate_pwf_governance.py` per D-IH-86-CX) + fall back to the hand-rolled parser when PyYAML unavailable (preserves pre_commit import-light posture). This IS the closing-loop pattern realised at the deepest level — the validator caught the gap on first run + the parser bug surfaced during attempted closure + the fix shipped in the same wave window per operator HYBRID directive (*"we must fix any bugs — CICD directive, no bug tolerance"* from dim10-disposition ratification). `scripts/validate_pwf_governance.py --report uat-wave-r-closure-2026-05-24.md` PASS (PWF-FM-01..05 clean; FIRST clean PWF worked-example); `validate_uat_report.py --self-test` PASS; `validate_pwf_governance.py --self-test` PASS (8/8 sub-tests); `scripts/validate_ops_register.py` PASS (130 rows; OPS-86-22 closed + 23 amended + 24+25 minted); `scripts/validate_decision_register.py` PASS (418 active + 2 superseded = 420 total; D-IH-86-CZ FK-resolves to OPS-86-22 + OPS-86-24 + OPS-86-25 now-existing); `scripts/validate_hlk.py` umbrella OVERALL PASS. **Closing-loop reservation**: future agents inherit the pattern (validator catches gap on first run + amend in same window + cite finding as field-test signal); 2 confirmed instantiations after this (Wave M/N specialty mints + Wave R+1 PWF mint); 1 more instantiation required to trigger transferable-mint per People DoD RULE 1.
- **I86 Wave R+1 P2 Commit 3-b — Wave R DIM-10 deploy-evidence-completeness probe-correctness fix + reality-reflecting backfill supplement (D-IH-86-CY; OPS-86-23 sub-finding closed; HYBRID disposition per operator dim10-disposition ratification)**: Operator-ratified HYBRID path (option C; *"we must fix any bugs (make this a CICD directive, no bug tolerance). But we must backfill to reflect reality as we go because we'll have better processes and metadata (also CICD for data integrity)."*) resolves 4 DIM-10 false-positive findings from Wave R close (`reports/regression-sweep-2026-05-24-wave-r-close.md`) flagging I68/I71/I72/I73 `files-modified.csv` for missing deploy/READY/HTTP-200 evidence tokens. **Root cause** identified during execution: `_probe_dimension_10_deploy_evidence_completeness` in `scripts/inter_wave_regression_sweep.py` used a **global `sibling_rows` accumulator** across all per-initiative `files-modified.csv` files, then ran reports-directory + UAT-presence + deploy-token checks **per CSV regardless of whether that specific CSV carried any sibling-repo rows**. I68/I71/I72/I73 had ZERO sibling-repo rows in their own `files-modified.csv` (every row was `repo=openclaw-akos`), yet the probe still demanded their `reports/` carry deploy evidence — a structural false positive ratifying ghost sibling-repo presence. **Scope (Commit 3-b atomic; 9 surfaces)**: (1) **MODIFIED `scripts/inter_wave_regression_sweep.py`** (~34 insertions / 19 deletions) — replaced global `sibling_rows` with **per-CSV `this_csv_sibling_rows`** counter that short-circuits the reports-dir/UAT/deploy-token checks when 0 (preserving global `total_sibling_rows` for the summary verdict); added `_safe_relpath()` helper wrapping `Path.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)` with `try/except ValueError → fallback to as_posix()` for pytest `tmp_path` robustness (same defensive pattern as the PWF validator from Commit 3-a). (2) **MODIFIED `tests/test_inter_wave_regression.py`** (+213 lines) — new `TestDimension10PerCsvScoping` class with 5 tests + `_build_planning_fixture` helper synthesising temp planning layouts: `test_zero_sibling_zero_findings` (initiatives with ZERO sibling-repo rows produce NO DIM-10 findings; this is the bug-fix regression test) + `test_sibling_with_complete_evidence_zero_findings` (true PASS path) + `test_sibling_without_uat_does_flag` (true gap detection: sibling-bearing CSV with no `uat-*.md` IS flagged) + `test_sibling_with_uat_missing_evidence_does_flag` (true gap detection: sibling-bearing CSV with `uat-*.md` but missing deploy tokens IS flagged) + `test_total_sibling_rows_counted_globally` (clean-verdict summary stays accurate). **35/35 PASS** including all 5 new tests + 30 pre-existing tests; no regressions. (3) **NEW `docs/wip/planning/86-initiative-cluster-execution-coordinator/reports/uat-dim10-backfill-supplement-2026-05-25.md`** (~150 lines; `verdict: PASS`) — closure UAT supplement applying the I85/I87 11-section bar (`akos-planning-traceability.mdc` §"UAT quality bar"): §1 closure summary + §2 closure-criteria verification with reproducible commands + §3 mechanical evidence (probe-fix diff + 5-test PASS table + post-fix sweep clean=1/gap=0) + §4 per-dimension findings (DIM-10 specifically) + §5 D-IH-86-D mechanical cross-check + §6 SOP+runbook pair (paired `SOP-PEOPLE_INTER_WAVE_REGRESSION_001` AC-HUMAN + AC-AUTOMATION) + §7 risk-register closure (R-IH-86-N/A: no new risks; this is regression-test-locked) + §8 decision close-outs (D-IH-86-CY) + §9 closure-registry edits (DECISION_REGISTER 420→421 active + OPS-86-23 amended) + §10 verdict + 7-item operator sign-off + §11 cross-references. Explicitly documents the **reality-reflecting backfill** principle: NO synthetic deploy tokens injected into I68/I71/I72/I73 `files-modified.csv` (their TRUE sibling-repo touch count is zero; injecting tokens would be data-integrity drift, the opposite of what the operator's "CICD for data integrity" directive demands); instead the probe is corrected to match reality. (4) **NEW `docs/wip/planning/86-initiative-cluster-execution-coordinator/reports/regression-sweep-2026-05-25-commit-3-b-dim10.md`** (~80 lines) — post-fix targeted DIM-10 sweep showing `clean: 1 / gap: 0` (vs Wave R's `clean: 0 / gap: 4`); narrative explains the 45 sibling-repo rows present globally (across initiatives that legitimately bear sibling-repo touches) all have their owning CSV's `reports/` carry UAT with deploy/READY/HTTP-200 evidence. (5) **NEW `artifacts/regression-sweep-2026-05-25.json`** (sidecar mechanical evidence per the `INTER_WAVE_REGRESSION_DISCIPLINE.md` §3.3 dual-output contract). (6) **APPENDED `DECISION_REGISTER.csv`** D-IH-86-CY (420→421 active + 2 superseded; governance class; reversibility high; rationale carries operator verbatim quote + 5-line probe-fix summary + 5-test enumeration + reality-reflecting backfill principle articulation + lineage to D-IH-86-CR/CS for the original Wave R disposition gate). (7) **MODIFIED `OPS_REGISTER.csv`** OPS-86-23 amended: title updated (30→26 remaining findings); long-form notes amended to mark DIM-10 sub-finding RESOLVED 2026-05-25 with cross-reference to the supplement; `decision_ids` extended `D-IH-86-CS;D-IH-86-CR;D-IH-86-CY`; weight tightened 3.0→2.5 reflecting reduced backlog. (8) **MODIFIED `CHANGELOG.md`** (this entry). (9) **APPENDED `I86 files-modified.csv`** +9 rows tracking Commit 3-b deliverables (all `akos-pending` SHA pre-commit; hygiene SHA backfill follows). **Validation**: `validate_decision_register.py` PASS (421 active + 2 superseded); `validate_ops_register.py` PASS; `validate_hlk.py` OVERALL PASS (all 50+ validators green); targeted `pytest tests/test_inter_wave_regression.py -v` 35/35 PASS; targeted `inter_wave_regression_sweep.py --wave-closing 'R+1' --dimensions DIM-10` clean=1/gap=0. **Why this matters (CICD-as-doctrine codification)**: the operator's framing *"this must be a CICD directive — no bug tolerance"* + *"backfill to reflect reality as we go (CICD for data integrity)"* is now operationalised as a two-prong rule pattern for any future probe-vs-reality drift: (a) **probe correctness** is the load-bearing fix because the probe is the policy-as-code (5 regression tests lock in the corrected scoping invariant; no future refactor can silently re-introduce the global-accumulator bug); (b) **reality-reflecting backfill** refuses to fabricate evidence to make a misbehaving probe go green (which would produce data-integrity drift — the audit-trail anti-pattern this very discipline exists to prevent). The HYBRID is not a compromise; it is the only correct disposition when the probe is wrong AND the data is right. Future regression-sweep findings whose root cause is probe drift inherit this pattern: fix the probe + co-mint regression tests + author the reality-reflecting backfill supplement; never inject synthetic evidence into upstream CSVs. **Reversibility high**: probe-fix is a pure refactor preserving the gate's intent; tests are net-additions; supplement + sweep report are dated artifacts; D-IH-86-CY governance class with high reversibility; OPS-86-23 amendment revertible (single-row revert restores Wave R close state). **Forward state**: Commit 3-b LANDED; Commit 3-c PENDING (DECISION_REGISTER follow-up D-IH-86-CU/CV completion + Wave R UAT amendment v2 + DIM-06 followup). I86 Wave R+1 P2 substrand COMPLETE on the DIM-10 axis.
- **I86 Wave R+1 P1 Commit 3-a — PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUP Governance Discipline mint (12th Quality Fabric specialty content axis paired with UAT_DISCIPLINE classification axis; D-IH-86-CX; OPS-86-22 + OPS-86-23 advance)**: Third of three atomic commits per `meta6-b` ratification. **Composes multiplicatively** with UAT_DISCIPLINE (Commit 1; D-IH-86-CW) per `HOLISTIKA_QUALITY_FABRIC.md` §3 — UAT_DISCIPLINE classifies the **shape** of a UAT (7 axes); PWF Governance classifies the **content** of a `verdict: PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUP` (5-class followup taxonomy + structured `verdict_followup_rationale` block). **5-class taxonomy** (operator-ratified at `meta6-b`): `monitoring-obligation` (signal must keep clearing; e.g., Sentry error rate stays ≤ 5/hour) / `deferred-work-with-tracker` (work intentionally not done in this gate; tracker mandatory) / `convention-class-followup` (cross-cutting cleanup deferred to a Wave-X consolidation) / `mechanical-recovery-with-eta` (transient gap with named recovery window) / `escalation-to-blocker-tracker` (closure ratified despite a blocker on a sibling; blocker-tracker mandatory). **Scope (Commit 3-a atomic; 16 surfaces)**: (1) **NEW `akos/hlk_pwf_governance.py`** (~330 lines) — `PWFFollowupRationale` + `PWFGovernanceFinding` + `PWFGovernanceReport` Pydantic frozen models + `VALID_PWF_FOLLOWUP_CLASSES` frozenset (5 values) + `VALID_PWF_FINDING_CODES` frozenset (5 codes: `PWF-FM-01-CLASS-MISSING` / `PWF-FM-02-CLOSURE-TARGET-MISSING` / `PWF-FM-03-OWNER-MISSING` / `PWF-FM-04-TRACKER-PATH-MISSING-OR-INVALID` / `PWF-FM-05-CONVENTION-CLASS-INCOMPLETE`) + fixture constructors. (2) **NEW `scripts/validate_pwf_governance.py`** (~530 lines) — `--self-test` (8 in-process checks ~2s) + `--report <path>` (single-file sweep) + `--all` (all in-scope UAT reports glob `docs/wip/planning/**/reports/uat-*.md`) + `--strict` (FAIL on forward-only by 2026-05-19 watershed) + PyYAML-based nested rationale parser handling 4 input shapes + 5 finding-code-emitting `_check_*` functions + `pathlib.Path.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)` with `ValueError` fallback for pytest tmp paths. (3) **NEW `tests/test_validate_pwf_governance.py`** (35 tests; 35/35 PASS) — Pydantic fixture round-trips + 5 finding-code coverage tests + parser 4-shape input coverage + integration tests against synthetic UAT reports + edge cases (empty rationale + invalid class + invalid tracker_path + pre-watershed INFO-only). (4) **NEW `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/canonicals/PASS_WITH_FOLLOWUP_GOVERNANCE_DISCIPLINE.md`** (~430 lines; status `charter`) — 5-class enum + structural rationale block schema + 5-finding-code reference + compose_PWF + INFO→FAIL ramp (§4.1: gated on Wave T at earliest + 3 consecutive clean wave-close sweeps + operator-explicit decision row) + per-class worked examples + anti-patterns + cross-references to UAT_DISCIPLINE classification axis + external research grounding (World Quality Report 2024 + Standish CHAOS 2023 + ITIL 4 Continual Improvement Register + Six Sigma DMAIC Control phase) + §6 "Demotion procedure" + cross-walk to akos-conflict-surfacing-and-blocker-trackers Option-5 enum. - **I86 Wave R Bundle B-2b EXECUTION — FINOPS writer executable layer: 2 NEW Edge Functions + dispatch-pattern refactor of stripe-webhook-handler + 5 shared TS modules + 7 Deno tests + 2 Python runbooks + 1 pgmq RPC wrappers migration + 46/46 pytest + 2 release-gate INFO advisories (D-IH-81-W; SECOND of R5-triple commits; B-2c canonical CSV writes + governance close still PENDING)**: Operator ratified two questions cleanly on 2026-05-23: `b2b-test-b` (inline Deno test scaffolding for every Edge Function + shared module — chose higher upfront test-coverage cost over `b2b-test-a` post-deploy testing for insurance against silent worker-logic regression) + `b2b-wh-b` (refactor `stripe-webhook-handler` from 270-line monolith into dispatch pattern — chose long-term clean architecture over `b2b-wh-a` minimal inline addition for stable FINOPS-vs-Kirbe-vs-Holistika concern isolation). This commit lands the SECOND of the three R5-triple commits: the **executable layer** (Edge Functions + dispatch modules + Deno tests + Python runbooks + pgmq RPC wrappers migration + release-gate INFO wiring). Canonical CSV writes (`ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY.csv` +2 rows + new `counterparty_resolution_strategy` column) + INFO→FAIL strict-mode promotion + UAT + first live Stripe `charge_succeeded` proof-of-life round-trip evidence land at **B-2c** (closure decision `D-IH-81-X`). **Scope (Bundle B-2b atomic commit; 24-file delta + 4 governance writes = 28 files)**: (1) **Supabase migration** `supabase/migrations/20260524100000_i81_p2_b2b_pgmq_rpc_wrappers.sql` (~157 lines) — creates 5 `SECURITY DEFINER` RPC wrappers in the `public` schema (`pgmq_send_finops_writer` + `pgmq_read_finops_writer` + `pgmq_delete_finops_writer` + `pgmq_archive_finops_writer` + `pgmq_read_finops_dlq`) with `EXECUTE` granted to `service_role`. Works around the PostgREST limitation that schema-qualified `pgmq.send` cannot be called directly via the `supabase-js .rpc()` client method — wrappers provide a `public`-schema interface while keeping `pgmq.*` invocations confined to the wrapper definitions. DBA tranche; `npx supabase db push` after merge. (2) **Six shared TypeScript modules** at `supabase/functions/_shared/finops/`: `types.ts` (Pydantic enum frozensets mirrored as TS `ReadonlySet<string>` × 8 enums + 4 interfaces) + `counterparty_resolver.ts` (R1-a 4-strategy ladder mirroring `akos.hlk_finops_ledger.resolve_counterparty_id`) + `fx_snapshot.ts` (R2-a ECB cache + 0.5%% Stripe divergence detector mirroring `akos.hlk_fx_rate.compute_fx_snapshot`) + `ops_register_emit.ts` (R4-a 24-col OPS row builder + RICE auto-score + `compliance.ops_register_mirror` insert mirroring `akos.hlk_ops_register_emit`) + `stripe_event_logger.ts` (R3-a Layer 1 idempotency: `logStripeEvent` + `enqueueFinopsEvent` + `markStripeEventProcessed` + `incrementStripeEventAttempts` using the pgmq RPC wrappers). (3) **Two NEW Edge Functions**: `supabase/functions/fx-rate-cache-refresh/index.ts` (~244 lines; Supabase scheduled function cron daily 06:00 UTC; fetches ECB SDMX daily reference rates for USD/GBP/CHF; performs X/EUR currency inversion; upserts to `holistika_ops.fx_rate_cache`; tolerates ECB fetch failures gracefully per R2-a 4-tier fallback ladder) + `supabase/functions/finops-writer-worker/index.ts` (~470 lines; Supabase scheduled function cron every 1m; consumes `pgmq.finops_writer_queue` via RPC wrapper; reads raw event from `holistika_ops.stripe_events`; applies `counterparty_resolver` + `fx_snapshot`; constructs `finops.registered_fact` row with idempotency Layer 3 `ON CONFLICT (stripe_event_id) DO NOTHING`; retries with exponential backoff 30s/60s/120s/240s + archives to `pgmq.finops_writer_dlq` after 4 failures + emits OPS row with `severity=high` + `topic=finops_writer_dlq_archive`; DLQ depth alert on every invocation). (4) **One REFACTORED Edge Function** (`b2b-wh-b` operator ratification): `supabase/functions/stripe-webhook-handler/index.ts` extracted from 270-line monolith into thin orchestrator (signature verification + dispatch fanout) + 2 dispatch modules at `supabase/functions/stripe-webhook-handler/dispatch/`: `finops_dispatch.ts` (~117 lines; MANDATORY; logs raw event to `holistika_ops.stripe_events` + enqueues to `pgmq.finops_writer_queue` for FINOPS-scope event types `charge.*`/`invoice.*`/`customer.subscription.*`/`payment_intent.*`; never throws; gates Stripe 200 OK per R3-a webhook-200-immediately doctrine) + `kirbe_holistika_dispatch.ts` (~241 lines; BEST-EFFORT; preserves original Kirbe + Holistika billing-plane routing logic VERBATIM via "extract without modify" refactor-safety doctrine; failures isolated from FINOPS path via try/catch wrapper in orchestrator; logged but never propagate to Stripe 200 OK decision). (5) **Seven inline Deno test files** (per `b2b-test-b` ratification — Deno-native unit-test coverage on worker logic before B-2c go-live): `_shared/finops/test_types.ts` + `test_counterparty_resolver.ts` + `test_fx_snapshot.ts` + `test_ops_register_emit.ts` + `test_stripe_event_logger.ts` cover the 5 shared modules + `dispatch/test_finops_dispatch.ts` + `dispatch/test_kirbe_holistika_dispatch.ts` cover the orchestrator dispatch branches (signature happy-path + FINOPS-failure-propagation + Kirbe-failure-isolation). Runnable locally via `deno test --allow-net --allow-env supabase/functions/_shared/finops/ supabase/functions/stripe-webhook-handler/dispatch/`. (6) **Two Python runbooks** per `akos-executable-process-catalog.mdc` Rule 1 AC-HUMAN + AC-AUTOMATION pairing: `scripts/finops_dlq_drain.py` (~463 lines; operator DLQ drain tool with `DlqEntry`/`DrainOperation`/`DrainSummary` frozen Pydantic models + 4 CLI subcommands `--self-test`/`--inspect`/`--requeue --message-id`/`--acknowledge --message-id --reason`; talks to Supabase via `service_role` + pgmq RPC wrappers) + `scripts/stripe_audit_metadata.py` (~603 lines; operator pre-flight Stripe metadata audit with `StripeMetadataFinding`/`StripeAuditReport` frozen Pydantic models + `classify_customer` + `classify_subscription` pure predicate functions + 5 CLI subcommands `--self-test`/`--audit-customers`/`--audit-subscriptions`/`--output-json <path>`/`--output-csv <path>`; talks to Stripe via official `stripe` SDK read-only). (7) **Paired pytest**: `tests/test_finops_dlq_drain.py` (~296 lines; 28 tests covering Pydantic immutability + RPC name registry + env-shortcircuit + CLI smoke + `--self-test` round-trip) + `tests/test_stripe_audit_metadata.py` (~385 lines; 18 tests covering Pydantic models + `classify_*` predicate edge cases + JSON/CSV output round-trip + CLI smoke). 46 new tests; B-2a's 57 tests preserved; 103 FINOPS Python tests total. (8) **Release-gate INFO advisory wiring**: `config/verification-profiles.json` `pre_commit` profile gains `finops_dlq_drain_self_test` + `stripe_audit_metadata_self_test` steps as INFO (default mode never blocks gate; `--strict` not invoked in pre_commit; CI cost ~1s each) + `scripts/release-gate.py` adds `run_finops_dlq_drain_self_test()` + `run_stripe_audit_metadata_self_test()` functions wired into `main()` results table as INFO rows. Both stay INFO until B-2c proof-of-life round-trip + operator-explicit FAIL promotion at `D-IH-81-X` closure. **Governance writes (this commit)**: `DECISION_REGISTER.csv` +`D-IH-81-W` row (architecture-class active medium reversibility; 410 active + 2 superseded post-append); I81 `decision-log.md` +full narrative section for D-IH-81-W (8 deliverables + rationale for dispatch refactor + rationale for inline Deno scaffolding + mechanical evidence + production deployment workflow + forward state + research grounding); I81 + I86 `files-modified.csv` +28 rows each; I86 operator-scratchpad drain entry. **No canonical CSV mutations** — `ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY.csv` + `OPS_REGISTER.csv` + `FINOPS_COUNTERPARTY_REGISTER.csv` all untouched at B-2b; those are B-2c scope per phased schema introduction discipline (Pydantic + runtime fallback covers the missing column lookup until B-2c canonical-CSV mint). **Mechanical evidence**: `py scripts/finops_dlq_drain.py --self-test` PASS; `py scripts/stripe_audit_metadata.py --self-test` PASS; `py -m pytest tests/test_finops_dlq_drain.py tests/test_stripe_audit_metadata.py -q` 46/46 PASS; `py -m pytest tests/test_validate_finops_ledger.py tests/test_hlk_fx_rate.py tests/test_resolve_counterparty_id.py -q` 57/57 PASS (B-2a regression baseline preserved → 103 FINOPS tests total); `py scripts/validate_hlk.py` umbrella OVERALL PASS; `py scripts/validate_decision_register.py` PASS (410 active + 2 superseded post-D-IH-81-W append); `py -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('scripts/release-gate.py').read())"` syntax OK; `py -c "import json; json.load(open('config/verification-profiles.json'))"` parses OK. **Reversibility medium**: executable layer (Edge Functions + dispatch modules + runbooks + pgmq RPC wrappers migration + release-gate INFO advisory wiring) rolls back via `supabase functions delete` + `DROP FUNCTION pgmq_*` migration + git revert of TS/Python files. Dispatch-pattern refactor is moderate-blast-radius: Kirbe/Holistika billing-plane logic preserved verbatim with Deno smoke-test coverage; refactor risk mitigated by best-effort try/catch wrapper around `dispatchKirbeHolistika` in orchestrator (FINOPS path never blocked by Kirbe/Holistika failure). Idempotency invariants survive rollback: `holistika_ops.stripe_events` PK=`stripe_event_id` + `finops.registered_fact` ON CONFLICT skip via composite unique constraint ensures no double-writes on retry storms. Strict-mode promotion gated at D-IH-81-X (B-2c closure + first live Stripe round-trip evidence). **Production deployment workflow (out-of-band; not part of this commit)**: 1) `npx supabase db push` (applies pgmq RPC wrappers migration). 2) `npx supabase functions deploy fx-rate-cache-refresh`. 3) `npx supabase functions deploy finops-writer-worker`. 4) `npx supabase functions deploy stripe-webhook-handler` (re-deploys with dispatch refactor; same endpoint URL). 5) Cron schedule `fx-rate-cache-refresh` daily 06:00 UTC. 6) Cron schedule `finops-writer-worker` every 1m. 7) Run `py scripts/stripe_audit_metadata.py --audit-customers --audit-subscriptions --output-json artifacts/stripe-audit-pre-go-live.json` to capture baseline. 8) Trigger first AT Stripe `charge_succeeded` event; verify worker writes to `finops.registered_fact` with resolved `counterparty_id` (proof-of-life criterion for B-2c closure under D-IH-81-X). **Forward state**: Bundle B-2b CLOSED at this commit (executable layer landed; full pipeline end-to-end-runnable in dev); **Bundle B-2c PENDING** (`D-IH-81-X`; canonical CSV writes + INFO→FAIL promotion + ARCHITECTURE.md/USER_GUIDE.md sync + UAT + proof-of-life evidence); Bundle B Strand 2 ambiguous-per-row inline-ratify still pending (3-4 batches per `b1-s2-a`); Quality Fabric 12th specialty mint (SYNTHESIS_BEFORE_TRANCHE; PRIORITY-5) still pending — B-2a + B-2b + B-2c becomes third worked precedent when specialty mints; drain7 cursor-rule-skill-pairing subagent proposal still pending; A2 cross-area Ops-wiring gate at FINOPS area executable operational coverage (full coverage gated at B-2c proof-of-life).

- **I86 Wave R Bundle B-2a EXECUTION — FINOPS writer substrate: DDL + Pydantic SSOT + helpers + validator + 57/57 tests + release-gate INFO wiring (D-IH-81-V; first of R5-triple commits; B-2b executable + B-2c data/governance still PENDING)**: Operator ratified the full R1..R5 batch on 2026-05-23 (`r1-a` engagement-model router + `r2-a` ECB FX cache + `r3-a` pgmq DLQ + `r4-a` HLK-ERP OPS-row convergence + `r5-triple` three-commit split). This commit lands the FIRST of the three triple-split commits: the **substrate-only** layer (DDL + Pydantic SSOT + helpers + validator + tests + release-gate INFO wiring). Executable Edge Functions + worker land at **B-2b** (`D-IH-81-W`). Canonical CSV writes (`ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY.csv` +2 rows + new `counterparty_resolution_strategy` column) + governance close + UAT land at **B-2c** (closure decision under `D-IH-81-W`). **Scope (Bundle B-2a atomic commit; substrate-only)**: (1) **Supabase migration** `supabase/migrations/20260524000000_i81_p2_b2_finops_writer_substrate.sql` (~91 lines) — enables `pgmq` extension + creates 2 queues (`finops_writer_queue` + `finops_writer_dlq`) + creates `holistika_ops.stripe_events` raw-event idempotency log table (PK=`stripe_event_id`) + creates `holistika_ops.fx_rate_cache` ECB daily-rate cache table + extends `finops.registered_fact` with 4 FX columns (`amount_minor_eur` + `fx_rate_ecb` + `fx_rate_stripe` + `fx_source`) + grants `service_role` permissions on `compliance.ops_register_mirror`. DDL only; apply via `npx supabase db push` after merge. (2) **Pydantic SSOT** `akos/hlk_finops_ledger.py` (~318 lines) — mints `RegisteredFactRow` model + 14-col `REGISTERED_FACT_FIELDNAMES` tuple + 4 enum frozensets (`VALID_FACT_TYPES` + `VALID_FX_SOURCES` + `VALID_RESOLUTION_STRATEGIES` + `VALID_CONFIDENCE_LEVELS`) + `resolve_counterparty_id()` 4-strategy ladder (`metadata_billing_plane` medium / `stripe_customer_link_lookup` low / `engagement_model_router` N/A-defers-to-B-2c / `manual_review` unresolved) + `compute_fx_snapshot()` ECB 4-tier fallback (`identity_eur` / `ecb_daily` / `ecb_previous_day_fallback` / `manual_override`) + 0.5% Stripe-vs-ECB divergence flag. Per R1-a engagement-model router + R2-a ECB FX cache designs. (3) **Helper module** `akos/hlk_fx_rate.py` (~165 lines) — ECB XML parser + EUR-base inversion + Holistika-pair conversion (EUR↔USD↔GBP↔CHF) + 4-tier fallback ladder + 0.5% Stripe-vs-ECB divergence detector. (4) **Helper module** `akos/hlk_ops_register_emit.py` (~115 lines) — 24-col `OPS_REGISTER` row contract + RICE auto-score for programmatic OPS row emission. R4-a HLK-ERP convergence implementation: reuses existing `render_operator_inbox.py` auto-render chain; no new dashboard infrastructure. (5) **Validator** `scripts/validate_finops_ledger.py` (~139 lines) — exercises Pydantic + resolution + FX + OPS-emit round-trip against 4 synthetic Stripe-event facts (`finops_stripe` + `finops_openai` + `finops_at_pymes` + an unresolved `manual_review` case) that FK-resolve to `FINOPS_COUNTERPARTY_REGISTER.csv` `finops_*` slugs. Default mode runs at INFO advisory (never blocks gate); `--strict` mode FAILS on synthetic errors. (6) **Test bundle** 57/57 PASS: `tests/test_validate_finops_ledger.py` (28 tests — SSOT constants + Pydantic row validation valid/invalid pairs + CLI smoke default + strict modes) + `tests/test_hlk_fx_rate.py` (17 tests — ECB XML + EUR-base inversion + Holistika-pair conversion + fallback ladder + divergence detector) + `tests/test_resolve_counterparty_id.py` (12 tests — 4-strategy resolution + `compute_fx_snapshot` scenarios + OPS payload emit). (7) **Release-gate INFO wiring**: `config/verification-profiles.json` `validate_finops_ledger_self_test` step added to `pre_commit` profile + `scripts/release-gate.py` `run_finops_ledger_validation()` function + INFO advisory row in main() results table. INFO ramps to FAIL at `D-IH-81-W` (B-2c closure + first live Stripe `charge_succeeded` round-trip success — proof-of-life criterion: resolved `counterparty_id` + computed `amount_minor_eur` via ECB cache hit). (8) **Governance writes (this commit)**: `DECISION_REGISTER.csv` +`D-IH-81-V` row (architecture-class active medium reversibility; 410 active + 2 superseded post-append); I81 `decision-log.md` +full narrative section for D-IH-81-V (substrate scope + 7 deliverables + rationale + mechanical evidence + forward state + research grounding); I81 + I86 `files-modified.csv` +13 rows each; I86 operator-scratchpad drain entry; `supabase/migrations/README.md` parity table entry. **No canonical CSV mutations** — `ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY.csv` + `OPS_REGISTER.csv` + `FINOPS_COUNTERPARTY_REGISTER.csv` all untouched at B-2a; those are B-2c scope per phased schema introduction discipline (Pydantic `counterparty_resolution_strategy` column lookup defers to B-2c canonical-CSV mint with runtime fallback). **No Edge Function code** — all 3 functions (2 NEW + 1 MODIFIED stripe-webhook-handler FINOPS branch extension) + 2 runbooks (`finops_dlq_drain.py` + `stripe_audit_metadata.py`) are B-2b scope. **Mechanical evidence**: `py scripts/validate_finops_ledger.py` PASS (4 synthetic facts round-trip clean); `py -m pytest tests/test_validate_finops_ledger.py tests/test_hlk_fx_rate.py tests/test_resolve_counterparty_id.py -q` 57/57 PASS; `py scripts/validate_hlk.py` umbrella OVERALL PASS; `py scripts/validate_decision_register.py` PASS (410 active + 2 superseded post-D-IH-81-V append). **Reversibility medium**: substrate (DDL migrations + Pydantic modules + tests + INFO advisory wiring) rolls back via `supabase migration repair` + module removal + verification-profiles entry deletion if B-2b/B-2c execution surfaces architectural regressions. - **I86 Wave R Bundle B-2 ARCHITECTURE SYNTHESIS — research-grounded craft for 4 decisions (D-IH-81-V; read-only synthesis-before-tranche; awaiting R1..R5 ratify before execution)**: Operator partial-ratify at the Bundle B-2 7-decision batch (2026-05-23) cleanly ratified 3 decisions (A=`a1-per-event` write granularity, D=`d1-finops-ledger` Pydantic SSOT location, F=`f1-env-per-env` signing secrets) and requested research-grounded crafting of the remaining 4 (B=counterparty resolution, C=currency strategy, E=retry posture, G=observability). This commit lands the synthesis-before-tranche architecture report responding to that craft request. **Scope (read-only synthesis only; no code/CSV/migration changes)**: (1) **Architecture report minted** at `docs/wip/planning/81-vault-integrity-layout-milestones-retrofit/reports/p2-bundle-b2-architecture-2026-05-23.md` (~520 lines) carrying TL;DR + verbatim operator framing + internal research sweep (`ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY.csv` 8-model inventory; `OPS_REGISTER.csv` 124-row state; `render_operator_inbox.py` + `OPERATOR_INBOX.md` auto-render surface; `stripe-webhook-handler/index.ts` 270-line gap analysis; `holistika_ops.stripe_customer_link` + `finops.registered_fact` Supabase state; `sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py` insert pattern) + external research sweep (Stripe FX Quotes API; ECB daily reference rates; Supabase pgmq extension; industry webhook DLQ + idempotency patterns). (2) **Correction of recon §3 error**: Recon report erroneously claimed `Charge.exchange_rate` field exists on Stripe Charge object; external research confirms it does NOT — FX must come from a separate source (ECB-authoritative recommended; `Charge.balance_transactions[].exchange_rate` available as cross-check). (3) **Refined recommendations for the 4 craft decisions**: **B (counterparty resolution)** → engagement-model-aware router via `counterparty_resolution_strategy` column on `ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY.csv` + 2 new model rows (`eng_model_saas_subscription` + `eng_model_rpp_vendor`); router in `akos/finops_writer.py` looks up customer's engagement_model_id → applies strategy. Adding new business model = 1 CSV row, no code change. **C (FX strategy)** → ECB-authoritative + Stripe-cross-check; NEW `holistika_ops.fx_rate_cache` table + NEW `fx-rate-cache-refresh` Edge Function (daily 06:00 UTC); writes immutable; >2% divergence surfaces OPS row. **E (retry posture)** → industry-standard 3-layer pattern: webhook returns 200 immediately + writes raw event to `holistika_ops.stripe_events` (PK=`stripe_event_id` for idempotency) → `finops-writer-worker` Edge Function consumes from `pgmq.finops_writer_queue` with exponential backoff (1m/5m/30m/2h) → 4th retry fail lands in `pgmq.finops_writer_dlq` + emits OPS row. Aligned with Stripe's published retry guidance + GitHub Webhooks DLQ. **G (observability)** → HLK-ERP convergence via `OPS_REGISTER.csv` → `OPERATOR_INBOX.md` auto-render (no new surface; reuses existing `render_operator_inbox.py`); worker emits OPS rows for DLQ alerts + FX divergence + provisional-row review + AT-vs-live shape drift. (4) **End-to-end mermaid** of Bundle B-2 architecture (Stripe webhook → idempotency log → pgmq → worker → router → registered_fact + OPS signals + Inbox auto-render). (5) **File-by-file deliverable inventory** for B-2 execution: ~30 files across 11 categories (3 Supabase migrations + 3 Pydantic SSOT + 3 helper modules + 2 Edge Functions + 4 validators + 2 runbooks + 4 canonical CSV writes + 6 test files + optional cursor-rule + docs sync + governance writes). Effort estimate: 1 focused push window or 2 split windows. (6) **R1..R5 refined ratify batch surfaced** for operator covering engagement-model router + ECB FX cache + pgmq queue substrate + HLK-ERP convergence + commit-shape (single vs split). (7) **Updated risk register** (R-B2-1..R-B2-8; +R-B2-8 = pgmq-not-yet-enabled). (8) **Closure decision labels proposed**: `D-IH-81-V` (this architecture report; read-only) + `D-IH-81-W` (Bundle B-2 execution; appended at write commit). U→V contiguous. **No canonical files mutated; no Pydantic; no validator; no migration; no webhook handler change**. All those are Bundle B-2 execution scope pending R1..R5 ratification. **Mechanical evidence**: `validate_hlk_vault_links.py` PASS; `validate_hlk.py` umbrella OVERALL PASS; pure read-only synthesis report + governance cascade (operator-scratchpad drain + files-modified.csv backfill + CHANGELOG entry). **Forward state**: Bundle B-1ext + B-2-arch CLOSED (both read-only); Bundle B-2 execution PENDING R1..R5 ratify; Bundle B Strand 2 ambiguous-per-row still pending; QF 12th specialty mint still PRIORITY-5; drain7 subagent landing still pending.

- **I86 Wave M.5 hotfix — Doctrine-wins SSOT reconciliation of inter-wave regression dimension codes (D-IH-86-BW = 1 decision)** — Atomic hotfix on top of Wave M atomic commit `e82a0ae` + closure commit `07c5a10`. SSOT collision identified during Wave M closure self-review: `VALID_DIMENSION_CODES` in [`akos/hlk_inter_wave_regression.py`](akos/hlk_inter_wave_regression.py) (DIM-01-CLOSING-WAVE-SURFACES through DIM-12-CANONICAL-CSV-MIRROR-PARITY) described entirely different probes than the canonical [`INTER_WAVE_REGRESSION_DISCIPLINE.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/canonicals/INTER_WAVE_REGRESSION_DISCIPLINE.md) §2 dimensions table + the always-applied [`akos-inter-wave-regression.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-inter-wave-regression.mdc) cursor rule (DIM-01-DECISION-LINEAGE through DIM-12-OPERATOR-SCRATCHPAD-CONTINUITY). Two AskQuestion options surfaced for direction: (A) code-wins reformat canonical + cursor rule to match implementation; (B) doctrine-wins rewrite code to match canonical + cursor rule. Operator-ratified posture: **Option B doctrine-wins-engrave-properly** (same architectural pattern as D-IH-66-O canon-aligned hardening + operator I72 Cluster B engrave-properly override — whenever code + doctrine collide the canonical is authoritative because external agents read cursor rule + canonical first; code is operationalisation). Execution: P1 rewrote [`akos/hlk_inter_wave_regression.py`](akos/hlk_inter_wave_regression.py) (`VALID_DIMENSION_CODES` + `BASELINE_DIMENSION_CODES` + `CONDITIONAL_DIMENSION_CODES` + Literal type all updated to 12 doctrine-aligned names per canonical §3 compose_REGRESSION baseline/conditional split); P2 rewrote [`scripts/inter_wave_regression_sweep.py`](scripts/inter_wave_regression_sweep.py) (12 probe functions implementing doctrine heuristics literally + `PROBE_REGISTRY` mapping + `WAVE_AWARE_DIMENSIONS` dispatch contract + CLI help text + module docstring); P3 rewrote [`tests/test_inter_wave_regression.py`](tests/test_inter_wave_regression.py) (49 tests covering new dimension names + baseline-vs-conditional split invariants + probe-registry SSOT-equality assertions + smoke per dimension + emit functions + CLI modes); P4 verified clean (validate_design_pattern_registry PASS / validate_hlk PASS / pytest 49/49 PASS / regression sweep regenerated against Wave-L close producing 63 findings 3 clean + 7 drift + 53 gap = real signal from doctrine-aligned probes). Self-discipline lesson crystallised: *"when an always-applied cursor rule + canonical table + code Literal enum collide, always pick doctrine + rewrite code; reading cursor rule first is the codified agent default; misleading the agent at that surface costs orders of magnitude more in downstream confusion than rewriting an enum + 12 probe functions"* — lands in I80 Round 2 codify-via-existing-rules queue (precedent already encoded across `akos-governance-remediation.mdc` canonical-wins-on-drift + `akos-inline-ratification.mdc` Principle 6 novel framings + `akos-planning-traceability.mdc` UAT quality bar verdict_history amendment shape).

INFO→FAIL ramp at Wave M+3 per D-IH-86-BN. Process closes the operator's standing concern: *"how do we stop the regression-noticing pattern from being one-off per agent + one-off per chat and make it a wave-close discipline that future agents inherit by default."* **P2 first-sweep against Wave-L close — 72 findings** discovered via lived-experience execution of the new discipline. **P3 cluster-collapse umbrella (D-IH-86-BR/BS = 2 decisions)** — heavy-always Q2 disposition (cluster all 72 + post single AskQuestion batch per inline-ratify-craft Principle 5). **P4 sub-cluster ratifies (D-IH-86-BT/BU/BV = 3 decisions)**: Cluster A (BT) — 58/72 traced to probe-noise root cause (over-narrow `valid_statuses` frozenset); fix extends to 11 values; post-fix sweep 85→29 findings. Cluster B (BU) — 4/4 gap findings surfaced DATAOPS/MKTOPS/TECHOPS/UX_DISCIPLINE.md absent from `HOLISTIKA_QUALITY_FABRIC.md` §6 (forward-chartered but never minted); operator-ratified **engrave-properly OVERRIDE**: mint 4 full comprehensive canonicals at status:charter with paired cursor rules + full compose_X rule definitions + 7-dimension quality bars + cadence + integration with sister disciplines + cross-references + research grounding. Operator quote: *"As we sweep we clean and mint properly. That's why I expected each of these expensive runs to pay off by improving the overall integrity of my knowledge base."* Cluster C (BV) — 15/72 traced to self-referential class (probe scanned Wave M-pending untracked files that are wave-mint output itself); ratified rework-now-via-P7-atomic-commit (drifts auto-clear when commit lands). Forward-cadence: future Wave N+ regression sweeps run AFTER wave commit lands (post-commit cadence) eliminating self-referential class entirely. **P5/P6/P7 atomic commit (Wave M atomic commit `e82a0ae` + closure commit `07c5a10`)**: 8 new canonicals + 5 cursor rules + 2 paired Python modules + 1 paired runbook + 1 validator + 49 tests + process_list row + 5 design-pattern rows + DECISION_REGISTER appends + scratchpad updates + UAT report + files-modified backfill. The lived-experience execution of the new discipline proved its own value within the same wave that minted it.

(5) **Cursor rule** at [`.cursor/rules/akos-index-integrity.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-index-integrity.mdc) (5 RULES + when-applies + cross-references; mechanical companion to the canonical).
