- **I73 P0 charter — People Operations + Engagement Models + Methodology IP (mega-initiative; 2026-05-15)** — Activates `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-73` from candidate to active with `gated_on: charter_satisfies_gate (D-IH-73-B)` per bootstrapping-reality reframe (operator + Madeira AI O5-1 + ad-hoc collaborators; founder's own paid employment per [`FOUNDER_TRAJECTORY_INTERNAL.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/canonicals/FOUNDER_TRAJECTORY_INTERNAL.md) §2 funds Holistika's bootstrap; designing the 7-class engagement-model taxonomy IS the unblock, not a traditional hire). Round-2 amendment to candidate scaffold (4 strands → 8 strands; engagement-as-unit reframe). **8 strands across 11 phases (P0..P11)**: Strand A Learning charter + Holistik Researcher curriculum; Strand B Ethics+Learning inseparability (quarterly co-review SOP + brand-voice "we become unethical when we unlearn" refresh); Strand C 4 Engagement-lifecycle SOPs (hiring + onboarding + payroll + offboarding) parameterized by `engagement_model_id`; Strand D People Compliance/Ethics boundary + process_list orphan reassignments; Strand E `ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY` sibling dimension at People Operations (7-class taxonomy: hourly / milestone / percentage / apprentice / investor / outsourced / operator_self); Strand F Historical case-law (Bâtard 2020 / Mark-II / Alias V / RCD Legal / L'Oréal arrangement; `access_level=5 register=internal`); Strand G KB human-readability (4 persona views mapped 1:1 to engagement classes; hlk-erp panel filter routes); Strand H Methodology IP minting path (brand-vs-name decision matrix deferred to filing time per `D-IH-73-F`). **7 charter-time decisions ratified**: `D-IH-73-A` mega scope (`operator_inline_explicit_via_askquestion`) + `D-IH-73-B` charter-satisfies-gate (`operator_inline_default_accepted_via_skip`) + `D-IH-73-C` ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY home at People Operations sibling dimension (`operator_inline_explicit_via_askquestion`) + `D-IH-73-D` 7-class taxonomy (`operator_inline_explicit_via_askquestion`) + `D-IH-73-E` outsourced helper separate class with extra SOC (`operator_inline_default_accepted_via_skip`) + `D-IH-73-F` IP brand-vs-name decision-deferred-with-criteria-matrix (`operator_inline_default_accepted_via_skip`) + `D-IH-73-G` 4 KB-readability personas mapped 1:1 to engagement classes (`operator_inline_default_accepted_via_skip`). Gate 1 batched AskQuestion 2026-05-15. **10 OPS-73-* rows minted** (`OPS-73-1` ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY mint + `OPS-73-2` Learning charter + `OPS-73-3` Engagement-lifecycle SOPs + `OPS-73-4` Historical case-law + `OPS-73-5` Ethics+Learning quarterly review + `OPS-73-6` Compliance/Ethics boundary + `OPS-73-7` KB human-readability + `OPS-73-8` Methodology IP minting + `OPS-73-9` first-engagement UAT + `OPS-73-10` integration verification). **8 conundrums** deferred to per-phase inline-ratify (`C-73-1..C-73-8`). **PAUSE POINTS** at P1 (canonical-CSV gate: ENGAGEMENT_MODEL_REGISTRY + ENGAGEMENT_REGISTRY 17-col extension + ~6-8 `tbi_peopl_dtp_engagement_*` process_list rows + Supabase mirror), P6 (process_list orphan reassignments), P8 (brand-vs-name decision matrix). Workspace folder [`docs/wip/planning/73-people-operations-and-learning-curriculum/`](docs/wip/planning/73-people-operations-and-learning-curriculum/) ships with `master-roadmap.md` + `reports/p0-charter-report.md` (12-row plan-quality bar self-critique gate PASS) + `decision-log.md` (D-IH-73-A..G with full rationale + decision_source) + `risk-register.md` (R-IH-73-1..10 with mitigation + owner + close-out phase) + `files-modified.csv` (18-col schema). Authoritative Cursor plan at `~/.cursor/plans/i73-people-ops-engagement-models-methodology-ip-c9d4e7f3.plan.md` with 3 mermaid diagrams (architecture / module / phase-dep) + 11 per-phase deep sections (SCOPE / PREREQUISITES / DELIVERABLES / VERIFICATION / pause-point class / self-checkpoint count / cursor-rules adherence each) + 12 external research sources (Lattice + Gallup + ATD + ISO 30414 + Holberton/Lambda/Multiverse + Topcoder/Toptal/Upwork + YC/a16z + Maven/Reforge/On Deck/Section + IDEO + Strategyzer + Atlassian/Notion/Linear + Holistika v1.x–v2.7 archive). `INITIATIVE_DEPENDENCIES.md` promoted (I73 candidate → active in mermaid + blocker table + hold-gate section all MET); `_templates/README.md` per-initiative state table updated; `PLANNING_COMPENDIUM.md` §11.4 sub-section rewritten from candidate to active state with decisions + conundrums + cross-links + Cursor plan / workspace mirror anchors; `_candidates/i73-people-operations-and-learning-curriculum.md` slimmed to 10-line redirect stub (`status: superseded`, `superseded_by:` pointer).

authored 3 missing SOPs (`SOP-REVENUE_ROLLUP_001.md` + `SOP-PERSONA_AUDIT_001.md` + `SOP-ENGAGEMENT_SCAFFOLDING_001.md`); resolved 4 stale `TODO[I72-...]` pointers in `REVOPS_PROCESS_CATALOG.yaml`. **R-C `dd86b22`**: RevOps role slim (`D-IH-72-AM`) — deleted 4 forward-charter specialist rows (RevOps Systems Specialist + Process Architect + Enablement Lead + Data Engineer) from `baseline_organisation.csv` and absorbed disciplines into RevOps Manager's headcount expansion pattern; baseline 82→78. **R-D `ccbf87d`**: Marketing sub-role selective collapse (`D-IH-72-AN`) — deleted 6 sub-role rows (Demand Gen + Paid Media → Reach Manager; Community → Resonance Manager; Thought Leadership Editor + Corporate Marketing → Storytelling Manager; Growth Hacker → Experimentation Manager); retained Account Management Manager + PR Manager + Marketing Analytics Manager (distinct external mandates); baseline 78→72; 17 `process_list.csv` role_owner cells rerouted; - **release-gate.py — missing `import subprocess` (2026-05-15)** — Pre-existing bug from I72 P9 commit `297d6b7` where [`scripts/release-gate.py`](scripts/release-gate.py) calls `subprocess.run(...)` at lines 575 + 585 + 595 + 605 without importing the `subprocess` module. Caused `NameError: name 'subprocess' is not defined` on every release-gate invocation, blocking the strict matrix gate from completing past the RevOps spine row. One-line fix: add `import subprocess` to the standard library imports block alongside `argparse` / `logging` / `os` / `shutil` / `sys`. Surfaced during I73 P0 validator matrix run; fixed in-place per `akos-governance-remediation.mdc` Design-for-Invariance (one-line obvious fix unblocks the gate without changing any other behavior). Post-fix release-gate runs to completion; remaining FAILs (Test suite + Browser smoke + sibling-repo `boilerplate/i18n/messages/*.json` BRAND voice register hits + Vale exit=2) are all pre-existing carry-overs per the 2026-05-14 CHANGELOG note ("Pre-existing browser-smoke + Test suite release-gate FAILs unchanged (carry-overs since P13.6; separate cleanup work)") + post-I72 brand drift on sibling-repo i18n strings — not caused by I73 P0.

Operator UAT bands A-D self-verified PASS per blanket-trust posture; `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-71` closed (`closure_decision_id: D-IH-71-CLOSURE`; `closed_at: 2026-05-14`); `OPS-71-1` closed (validator-pack productization discharged; 3 of 3 I71 OPS rows now closed); annotated `v3.1.0` tag cut at this commit per `C-71-3` HOLD-for-P6-closure verdict ratified at P3 via [`D-IH-71-P`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/DECISION_REGISTER.csv) + SOP-RELEASE_TAXONOMY_001 §2 tag criteria (coherent externally-visible repo cut tied to a meaningful event = I71 closure). ### Changed

- **Release-gate hygiene — RCA + 3 pre-existing FAILs fixed + schema-drift prevention layer (2026-05-11)** — Operator-requested follow-up to the P13.6 carry-over backlog. The three FAILs documented in [`p13-6-closure-2026-05-11.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/p13-6-closure-2026-05-11.md) §4 were all instances of one structural pattern: **a canonical artifact mutated (CSV column appended / decision ID minted / row count grew), but a hardcoded downstream (script tuple / mirror DDL / test fixture / validator regex) was not updated in lockstep**. Fixed all three plus four additional cascading test fragilities surfaced en route, then added the prevention layer that catches the bug class going forward. **RCA finding** — `baseline_organisation.csv` was the only canonical compliance CSV without a sibling `akos/hlk_<csv>_csv.py` SSOT module (every other CSV follows that pattern); the missing module forced `sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py` to hardcode a 12-column tuple inline, which silently went stale when the `role_hourly_*_eur` rate trio was added. Additionally, the decision-id regex in `validate_decision_register.py` only accepted single-letter suffixes (`[A-Z]`), but I66 minted decisions through D-IH-66-AD (double-letter), and 11 of those decisions never propagated to `DECISION_REGISTER.csv`. **Fix #1 — decision-register backfill + regex widen**: appended D-IH-66-U..AD (11 rows: trademark filing operator handoff, trademark naming governance SOP, legal templates suite, SOP-META cross-references, files-modified.csv schemas v1+v2, strict-mode jargon promotion, Vercel build-failure patterns canon, recurrent CICD smoke discipline, direct-access service+method pages) to [`DECISION_REGISTER.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/DECISION_REGISTER.csv); widened [`scripts/validate_decision_register.py`](scripts/validate_decision_register.py) `DECISION_ID_STANDARD_RE` from `[A-Z]` to `[A-Z]{1,2}` (backwards-compatible — single-letter IDs still match). **Fix #2 — BASELINE_FIELDNAMES SSOT extraction + Supabase migration + test refresh**: created [`akos/hlk_baseline_org_csv.py`](akos/hlk_baseline_org_csv.py) declaring the canonical 15-column `BASELINE_ORGANISATION_FIELDNAMES` tuple, matching the akos/hlk_*_csv.py pattern used by every other dimension CSV; rewrote [`scripts/sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py`](scripts/sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py) to import from there instead of hardcoding; added [`supabase/migrations/20260511030000_release_gate_hygiene_baseline_rates.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260511030000_release_gate_hygiene_baseline_rates.sql) `ALTER TABLE compliance.baseline_organisation_mirror ADD COLUMN role_hourly_min_eur / role_hourly_par_eur / role_hourly_max_eur TEXT`. **Fix #3 — compute-from-canonical pattern for row-count fixtures**: refactored brittle hardcoded counts across [`tests/test_hlk.py`](tests/test_hlk.py) (`test_eleven_projects` → `test_canonical_project_count_matches_csv`; `test_project_summary`; `test_hlk_projects`), [`tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py`](tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py) (`_COUNTED_CSVS` map + `_csv_row_count()` helper drives 13 counter assertions + the persona-scenario INSERT count), [`tests/test_validate_hlk_dispatcher.py`](tests/test_validate_hlk_dispatcher.py) (`test_json_report_baseline_row_counts_match_i31_uat` → `test_json_report_baseline_row_counts_match_csv`), [`tests/test_localisation_frontmatter.py`](tests/test_localisation_frontmatter.py) (`test_brand_french_patterns_stub_exists` → `test_brand_french_patterns_exists` after the file was promoted from stub to active in I66 P1), [`tests/test_holistik_ops_axis_graph.py`](tests/test_holistik_ops_axis_graph.py) (`test_existing_role_process_program_topic_unchanged` → `test_graph_label_counts_match_registry`), and [`tests/test_company_deck.py`](tests/test_company_deck.py) (`test_slide_11_pillar_1_quotes_governance_metrics` now reads canonical CSV counts). Updated content artifacts: [`GOVERNANCE_MOAT.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/GOVERNANCE_MOAT.md) role count 65→66 (P13.4 trainee row) + process count 1.103→1.125 (I66 + P13.4 growth); [`deck_slides.yaml`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_company_dossier/deck_slides.yaml) slide 11 pillar 1 mirrors the same counts. **Prevention layer — `validate_compliance_schema_drift.py` + cursor-rule sync rows**: net-new [`scripts/validate_compliance_schema_drift.py`](scripts/validate_compliance_schema_drift.py) generalizes the existing single-CSV `check_process_list_header.py` to all 22 canonical compliance CSVs by walking a `(csv_path, akos_module, fieldnames_attr)` registry and comparing each CSV header to its SSOT tuple (catches additions / removals / order-drift / renames); wired into [`scripts/release-gate.py`](scripts/release-gate.py) as a strict gate via `run_compliance_schema_drift_check()`; added 3 new doc-sync rows to [`.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) codifying (a) the canonical-CSV → SSOT tuple + mirror DDL + sync script + test fixture lockstep contract, (b) the "new canonical CSV ships → schema-drift registry + sync script + Supabase migration + PRECEDENCE.md must all update in the same commit" cascade, (c) the "decision ID minted in CHANGELOG / files-modified / commit message → must append to DECISION_REGISTER.csv in the same commit" discipline (closes the markdown-only-reference gap the validator can't catch). Verification: [`release-gate.py`](scripts/release-gate.py) reports 22 PASS gates including the new compliance schema drift gate (PASS — 22 canonical CSVs aligned with akos.* SSOT tuples); test suite 2087/2088 pass (the one remaining is browser-smoke which has been pre-existing for environmental reasons since before P13 — Playwright browser workers cannot launch on this Windows host without a separate install pass; documented as carry-over in P13.6 §4). Pre-existing carry-over items 1, 2, 3 from the P13.6 closure are now CLOSED.

- **Track customer-deliverable PDFs in `_exports/` (policy reversal; 2026-05-11)** — Reverses the `_exports/` gitignore policy for `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think Big/Clients/*/_exports/` so branded PDFs and `render-manifest.json` are now **tracked in git**. The repo is shared with non-technical readers via git + Google Drive sync, and most stakeholders consume PDFs, not markdown; ignoring rendered PDFs left the engagement folder unreadable for collaborators without local Python tooling. New `.gitignore` rule allowlists `*.pdf` + `render-manifest.json` while still ignoring the render-time markdown sidecars (which duplicate the canonical sources in `01-operator-pack/` + `02-customer-pack/` and would create drift risk if tracked). [`_exports/README.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/_exports/README.md) added to orient readers; engagement README + render-script docstring updated to match. The `render-manifest.json` sha256 entries are the audit anchor — markdown edits without matching PDF re-render show as a manifest diff. Initial commit ships all 7 SUEZ surfaces (cdc-feasibility-shape / discovery-questionnaire / proposal / deck-suez-webuy + customer pack proposal / tarification / deck). Untracks stray `_remaining.txt` phase-walk helper that slipped into the I12 P12 commit.

### Added

- **I71 P5 — Pack A4 render-ownership coverage + Strand B AIOps hardening (2026-05-14):** Closes the I71 validator-pack quartet (A1 voice register + A2 Gantt confidence + A3 multilingual + A4 render ownership) and operationalises Strand B AIOps observability as advisory CI infrastructure. Authors [`scripts/validate_render_ownership.py`](scripts/validate_render_ownership.py) thin CLI walking `Think Big/Clients/*` + `Think Big/Advisers/*` engagement folders (skipping `_`-prefixed templates); parses [`WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT_HOLISTIKA.md` §16](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/canonicals/WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT_HOLISTIKA.md) canonical 9-row matrix (deck / proposal / tarification / Gantt / dossier / counterparty_brief / objections / press / advisor_email); checks per-deliverable `role_owner:` frontmatter against canonical expected role (Copywriter for deck + counterparty_brief; PMO for proposal + tarification + dossier + advisor_email; UX Designer for gantt; Account Manager for objections; Storytelling Manager for press). Surfaces 3 detection classes: `render-ownership-mismatch` (warning default; per-rule severity override via `render-ownership-pack.yml`; promotable to error via `AKOS_RENDER_OWNERSHIP_STRICT=1` env), `render-ownership-undeclared` (info), and `transition-trigger-pmo-to-revops` (info; fires when ≥ 3 active engagements ship PMO-owned deliverables per §16.3 PMO → RevOps drift signal; ratified threshold per `D-IH-72-B` 3+-engagement RevOps activation trigger). Chassis additions at [`akos/brand_voice_register.py`](akos/brand_voice_register.py): `RenderOwnershipRule` + `RenderOwnershipPack` Pydantic models (frozen; default_severity warning per discipline); `parse_render_ownership_rules` + `parse_render_ownership_pack_yaml` parser helpers; `DeliverableKind` Literal; `STANDARD_DELIVERABLE_KINDS` constant; ~150 LOC added (chassis grew 1193 → ~1340 LOC; comfortably under 1800 LOC re-evaluation threshold per the chassis-location decision criterion). **Chassis-extension verdict** (not sibling spinout) preserves the established additive-only chassis contract (P1 28-case regression suite locks signatures; P2 +7 BaseModels + 7 parsers; P5 +2 BaseModels + 2 parsers); 28-case P1 chassis regression PASS post-P5. Operator override at [`canonicals/_validators/render-ownership-pack.yml`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/canonicals/_validators/render-ownership-pack.yml) v0.1.0 (sibling shape to `gantt-pack.yml` + `multilingual-pack.yml`; not in `CANONICAL_REGISTRY` per the operator-pack-not-canonical-track precedent). **Strand B hardening** authors [`scripts/check_observability_mcps.py`](scripts/check_observability_mcps.py) (Option C — filesystem-only smoke check at `~/.cursor/projects/<workspace>/mcps/` for `user-sentry` + `user-langfuse` folders + `tools/` subdir descriptors; host-portable + offline-friendly + SOC-clean per [`akos-holistika-operations.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-holistika-operations.mdc) SOC posture — no live API probe; no secret values logged; no env values logged); wired into [`scripts/release-gate.py`](scripts/release-gate.py) as INFO row (advisory only; never blocks). [`WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT_HOLISTIKA.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/canonicals/WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT_HOLISTIKA.md) §18 expanded per **C-71-5 every-CI-gate-its-own-row default** ratified via `D-IH-71-T` (operator blanket-trust signal at P5 kickoff pre-approved the default; no AskQuestion surfaced — lower-friction option): §18.1 retains the high-level signal-class routing table (6 rows); new §18.2 authors 8 per-CI-gate routing rows (Pack A1 voice register + Pack A2 Gantt confidence + Pack A3 multilingual + Pack A4 render ownership + Tier 1 Vale sibling + review-stamp validator + observability MCP smoke + `release-gate.py` meta-gate) each carrying gate-name + owner-role + first-hop-channel + default-severity + playbook-link + SOC-redacted dashboard cross-link. Severity ladder ratified at §18.2: **strict (FAIL)** for Pack A1 + A2 + A3 (block CI); **warning** for Vale (warning-level hits FAIL the row but emergency-toggleable); **advisory INFO (never blocks)** for Pack A4 + review-stamp + MCP smoke + meta-gate. **SOC posture**: Sentry + Langfuse dashboard URLs surface through Cursor MCP session role-based-access (operator MCPs `user-sentry` + `user-langfuse` handle the URL → role-checked-access flow); CI logs surface only the reachability bit (yes/partial/no) per `check_observability_mcps.py`. No new SaaS tenants provisioned at P5 (Sentry + Langfuse remain operator-MCP-routed). 41-case test suite at [`tests/test_validate_render_ownership.py`](tests/test_validate_render_ownership.py) PASS in 0.75s; `pytest -m brand` regression PASS (226 + 1 skip). Real-vault smoke: 5 warning + 5 info advisories surface (SUEZ + Asesoría role_owner gaps + PMO→RevOps transition trigger at 7 engagements ≥ 3 threshold); 0 error hits. Strand B MCP reachability: 2/2 MCPs reachable on operator host (user-sentry 22 tools + user-langfuse 37 tools). 2 D-IH-71 rows minted: `D-IH-71-S` (Pack A4 ratification) + `D-IH-71-T` (Strand B observability cardinality + C-71-5 every-CI-gate-its-own-row verdict). `OPS-71-1` status stays `open` until P6 closes the validator-pack strand. `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-71` notes appended (P5 SHIPPED). Phase report at [`p5-pack-a4-strand-b-2026-05-14.md`](docs/wip/planning/71-cicd-discipline-and-aiops-baseline-maturity/reports/p5-pack-a4-strand-b-2026-05-14.md). Sibling commit `bf4e3ac`/`b0895075` (W3-authored P4-followup review-stamp expansion to 17 mirrors + Artifact standalone-table; `D-IH-71-R`) inadvertently scooped `D-IH-71-S` + `D-IH-71-T` registry rows + `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-71` P5 SHIPPED note into its commit; this P5 commit ships the remaining P5 chassis + validator + tests + WORKSPACE §18 + docs sync + phase report + CHANGELOG entry on top. Forward: P6 closing UAT + `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-71` closure + optional `v3.1.0` annotated tag per `C-71-3` HOLD verdict.

- **I71 P4 follow-up — review-stamp expansion to 17 additional `compliance.*` mirrors + Artifact subject class via `compliance.review_stamps_standalone` (2026-05-14):** Round-2 follow-up to P4 commit `bb04f08` ratified in one decision row [`D-IH-71-R`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/DECISION_REGISTER.csv) (135 active decisions; 134 prior + 1 new). Two combined deliverables: **(A)** extends the 4-column review-stamp shape (`last_review_at` DATE / `last_review_by` TEXT / `last_review_decision_id` TEXT / `methodology_version_at_review` TEXT) authored at P4 from the 4 governance-trio mirrors to the **17 remaining mirrored `compliance.*` canonicals** — `baseline_organisation_mirror` + `finops_counterparty_register_mirror` + `goipoi_register_mirror` + `adviser_engagement_disciplines_mirror` + `adviser_open_questions_mirror` + `founder_filed_instruments_mirror` + `program_registry_mirror` + `topic_registry_mirror` + `persona_registry_mirror` + `persona_scenario_registry_mirror` + `channel_touchpoint_registry_mirror` + `sourcing_register_mirror` + `skill_registry_mirror` + `touchpoint_kit_cell_mirror` + `policy_register_mirror` + `repo_health_snapshot_mirror` + `repository_registry_mirror` (column-extension); **(B)** mints `compliance.review_stamps_standalone` table (10 columns: `id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY` + `subject_kind TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (subject_kind IN ('canonical_md', 'standalone_csv', 'sop_md'))` + `subject_path TEXT NOT NULL` + optional `subject_id TEXT` FK-by-convention to `CANONICAL_REGISTRY.canonical_id` + 4 stamp columns + audit timestamps; `UNIQUE (subject_kind, subject_path)`) for the **Artifact subject class** (canonical `.md` files referenced via `CANONICAL_REGISTRY.csv`); RLS service_role-only ALL policy (deny anon + authenticated) per [`akos-holistika-operations.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-holistika-operations.mdc) §"Schema responsibilities". The standalone-table path was deferred at P4 per the C-71-4 column-extension-where-mirror-exists default; this follow-up lands it because the Artifact subject class has no mirror. Migration [`supabase/migrations/20260514202912_i71_p4_followup_review_stamp_expansion.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260514202912_i71_p4_followup_review_stamp_expansion.sql) applied via Supabase MCP `apply_migration` (68 `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` + 1 `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` + 1 `ALTER TABLE ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` + 1 `CREATE POLICY` + 17 documented no-op `UPDATE` statements + 1 multi-row `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` backfill of 66 rows from `CANONICAL_REGISTRY.csv` `last_review`; one transaction; idempotent). Same-commit lockstep per [`akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) "Any canonical compliance CSV header change" contract: 17 canonical CSV headers extended (4 columns appended at end with empty trailing values for all existing rows) + 17 `akos/hlk_*_csv.py` SSOT tuples extended in lockstep; [`scripts/validate_compliance_schema_drift.py`](scripts/validate_compliance_schema_drift.py) PASS (22 canonicals tuple-aligned). Extends [`scripts/validate_review_stamps.py`](scripts/validate_review_stamps.py) `_REGISTRY` from 4 to 21 entries (4 P4 baseline + 17 follow-up) + adds new `_scan_canonical_md_artifacts()` helper that walks `CANONICAL_REGISTRY.csv` and treats its existing `last_review` column as the stamp date for the Artifact scan path; total 22 reports per validator run (21 column-extension surfaces + 1 Artifact scan). 28-case test extension at [`tests/test_validate_review_stamps.py`](tests/test_validate_review_stamps.py) PASS in 1.17s (`TestRegistryExpansion` + `TestColumnExtensionMigrationExpanded` × 17 + `TestArtifactScan` × 5 + `TestComplianceSchemaDriftRegression` + `TestRealCanonicalsSmokeExpanded` × 3); 57 total cases now in the test module; P1 chassis regression suite remains green. SQL audit trail at [`reports/sql-proposal-p4-followup-2026-05-14.md`](docs/wip/planning/71-cicd-discipline-and-aiops-baseline-maturity/reports/sql-proposal-p4-followup-2026-05-14.md) (Operator SQL Gate per `akos-holistika-operations.mdc` discover-propose-execute pattern; operator blanket-trust pre-approval at I71 P4 kickoff); design doc at [`reports/p4-followup-design-2026-05-14.md`](docs/wip/planning/71-cicd-discipline-and-aiops-baseline-maturity/reports/p4-followup-design-2026-05-14.md) (mirror enumeration table + per-mirror disposition + standalone-table architecture + validator extension plan + 7-mirror exclusion rationale: 4 taxonomy lookups + 3 operational facts; CYCLE_REGISTER deferred). Real-vault smoke results: 1716 info / 0 warning / 0 error advisory hits across 2025 rows (180-day window; 30-day grace from authored-date proxy); Artifact scan: 92 rows stamped (no advisories; all within 180-day window). Empty-by-default per row stamp columns; operator backfills incrementally via [`REVIEW_STAMP_INBOX.md`](docs/wip/planning/REVIEW_STAMP_INBOX.md) workflow. RLS posture: 17 mirrors retain existing `service_role`-only deny-anon/-authenticated; new standalone table gets matching `service_role`-only ALL policy; Supabase MCP `get_advisors` returned 0 follow-up-attributable issues. PII: none of the new columns or table carry PII. `OPS-71-3` stays CLOSED at follow-up with `closure_decision_id: D-IH-71-Q` (no state change at this rolling chore); `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-71` notes appended (P4-followup SHIPPED). - **I71 P4 — Strand C2 review-stamp dimension (column-extension on mirrored canonicals; 2026-05-14):** Closes `OPS-71-3` with `D-IH-71-Q` (134 active decisions; 133 prior + 1 new). Authors [`supabase/migrations/20260514193709_i71_p4_review_stamp.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260514193709_i71_p4_review_stamp.sql) adding 4 review-stamp columns (`last_review_at` DATE / `last_review_by` TEXT / `last_review_decision_id` TEXT / `methodology_version_at_review` TEXT) to `compliance.*` mirrors of `process_list_mirror` + `decision_register_mirror` + `initiative_registry_mirror` + `ops_register_mirror` per **C-71-4 column-extension verdict** (default applied: mirrored tables get column-extension; unmirrored canonicals get separate-table — only the mirrored path lands at P4; `CANONICAL_REGISTRY` Artifact-class standalone-table path deferred to follow-up commit when first unmirrored canonical needs a stamp). Pattern follows I70 P8.2 baseline `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` precedent (additive nullable; idempotent; no `NOT VALID + VALIDATE CONSTRAINT` since no CHECK constraints). Same-commit lockstep per [`akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) "Any canonical compliance CSV header change" contract: 4 canonical CSV headers extended (process_list.csv 23→27 cols / DECISION_REGISTER.csv 15→19 cols / INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv 21→25 cols / OPS_REGISTER.csv 20→24 cols) with empty trailing values for all existing rows; 4 [`akos/hlk_*_csv.py`](akos/) SSOT tuples extended in lockstep; [`scripts/validate_compliance_schema_drift.py`](scripts/validate_compliance_schema_drift.py) PASS (22 canonicals tuple-aligned). CLI surface: `--threshold-days N`, `--strict`, `--json-log`, `--inbox-path`, `--no-inbox`, `--today`. Wired into [`scripts/release-gate.py`](scripts/release-gate.py) as INFO row "Review-stamp freshness (...; advisory only; ...)" — never blocks. Stale + missing rows surface to NEW sidecar [`docs/wip/planning/REVIEW_STAMP_INBOX.md`](docs/wip/planning/REVIEW_STAMP_INBOX.md) (companion to [`OPERATOR_INBOX.md`](docs/wip/planning/OPERATOR_INBOX.md) which is auto-rendered from `OPS_REGISTER.csv`); idempotent dated-section between `<!-- BEGIN REVIEW-STAMP-AUTO -->` / `<!-- END REVIEW-STAMP-AUTO -->` markers; first run surfaces 1212 info advisories / 0 warnings / 0 errors across 1364 rows. Reserves `/operator/governance/freshness-dashboard/` panel slot in [`HLK_ERP_ARCHITECTURE.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/canonicals/HLK_ERP_ARCHITECTURE.md) §4 (implementation deferred to I72+ RevOps activation per master-roadmap §P4 → §P5 chain). 29-case test suite at [`tests/test_validate_review_stamps.py`](tests/test_validate_review_stamps.py): TestValidStamps (4) + TestStaleRows (4) + TestThresholdEdgeCases (2) + TestMissingStamps (4) + TestInvalidDecisionRef (3) + TestEmptyStampTolerance (3) + TestColumnExtensionMigration (5) + TestInboxSurfacing (3) + TestRealCanonicalsSmoke (1) — all PASS. - **I71 P3 — Strand C1 release-taxonomy SOP authored (2026-05-14):** Closes `OPS-71-2` with `D-IH-71-P`. Authors [`SOP-RELEASE_TAXONOMY_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Tech/System%20Owner/canonicals/SOP-RELEASE_TAXONOMY_001.md) at `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Tech/System Owner/canonicals/` (~180 lines; 8 sections). §1 codifies the three release lanes ratified at I71 P0 via `D-IH-71-D` (methodology `major.minor` carried by [`LOGIC_CHANGE_LOG.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/canonicals/LOGIC_CHANGE_LOG.md) + `D-IH-*` rows; HLK vault folder path `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/`; openclaw-akos SemVer + [`CHANGELOG.md`](CHANGELOG.md) + `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` annotated git tags). §2 tag criteria distinguishes legitimate triggers (initiative-closure cut for substantive scope; public deploy event; MAJOR methodology bump) from non-triggers (every `D-IH-*` row; every commit; every small initiative closure). §3 SemVer judgment table maps PATCH/MINOR/MAJOR with worked examples + MINOR-default heuristic. §4 `[Unreleased]` working-line discipline per Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0; tag ratification renames the heading; Policy header pointer added at the top of this file pointing back to the SOP. §5 cross-lane non-implication rules (a methodology bump does NOT imply folder rename or tag; a folder rename is its own initiative; tags lag methodology by intent; common confusions enumerated). §6 (load-bearing per operator intent verbatim *"intuitive clever versioning — do not let the customer know it's a new version"*) codifies five customer-invisible versioning invariants: (1) customer-facing engagement deliverables carry NO version stamp in the rendered PDF cover/footer/headers/metadata; (2) `LOGIC_CHANGE_LOG.md` + `D-IH-*` rows carry methodology version churn operator-internal; (3) git tag developer-internal; (4) vault folder path structural operator-internal; (5) PDF metadata (Producer / Author / Title / Subject / Keywords) optionally carries build-info for operator triage but no human-readable version number. Customer-visible revision name-bumping (e.g., `proposal.customer.fr.v2.pdf`) is operator-curated only, never automatic, only when customer explicitly asks. §6.4 anti-patterns table extends the SUEZ-style brand-failure leak posture closed by I71 P1+P2 validator chassis to the versioning-leak failure mode. §6.5 enumerates the five rendering surfaces (cover-page / footer-running-header / PDF metadata / filename / cover-strip carriage) with per-surface owner-coverage gate per WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT §16.2. §7 cross-references; §8 maintenance + change control (SOP's own SemVer; review trigger; validator hooks; forward-looking notes including the future `validate_pdf_metadata_no_version_leak.py` reserved candidate). [`CANONICAL_REGISTRY.csv`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/CANONICAL_REGISTRY.csv) +1 row `sop_release_taxonomy_001` (111 rows total; was 110). [`PRECEDENCE.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/PRECEDENCE.md) Canonical assets table extended with new Release-taxonomy-SOP row. `OPS-71-2` closes with `closure_decision_id: D-IH-71-P` + `closed_at: 2026-05-14`. `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-71` notes appended (P3 SHIPPED). Companion Policy header above `[Unreleased]` cross-links readers to the SOP in one click. C-71-3 inline-ratify gate (tag-now-vs-hold for I71 P6 closure; default = hold) remains pending coordinator AskQuestion post-execution. Phase report at [`p3-release-taxonomy-2026-05-14.md`](docs/wip/planning/71-cicd-discipline-and-aiops-baseline-maturity/reports/p3-release-taxonomy-2026-05-14.md).

- **I71 P2 — Packs A2 + A3 + Addition 11 + Tier 1 Vale sibling (2026-05-14):** Closes I71 P2 with four atomic deliverables on the Pack A1 chassis. **Pack A2** ships [`scripts/validate_brand_gantt_confidence.py`](scripts/validate_brand_gantt_confidence.py) thin CLI walking `gantt.*.md` artifacts under `Think Big/Clients/*` + `Think Big/Advisers/*` engagement folders; detects three classes of violation per [`BRAND_GANTT_DISCIPLINE.md` §2 + §4](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/UX%20Designer/canonicals/BRAND_GANTT_DISCIPLINE.md): confidence-band-validity, variant-quadrant-consistency, confidence-inflation. Chassis surfaces `GanttConfidenceRule` + `AudienceQuadrantRule` + `BrandGanttPack` (additive); operator override at [`canonicals/_validators/gantt-pack.yml`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/canonicals/_validators/gantt-pack.yml); 37-case test suite at [`tests/test_validate_brand_gantt_confidence.py`](tests/test_validate_brand_gantt_confidence.py). **Pack A3** ships [`scripts/validate_brand_multilingual.py`](scripts/validate_brand_multilingual.py) thin CLI walking engagement folders for the 3-file pattern (`README.md` 5-line pointer + `README.fr.md` + `README.en.md`) + per-locale frontmatter cohesion per [`BRAND_MULTILINGUAL_CONTRACT.md` §2 + D-IH-70-P](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/canonicals/BRAND_MULTILINGUAL_CONTRACT.md). Chassis surfaces `LocaleSuffixRule` + `ReadmeTriadRule` + `BrandMultilingualPack`; operator override at [`canonicals/_validators/multilingual-pack.yml`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/canonicals/_validators/multilingual-pack.yml) with `default_triad_severity` knob operationalising C-71-2 inline-ratify (default warn-until-2-bilingual; coordinator handles inline-ratify); 28-case test suite at [`tests/test_validate_brand_multilingual.py`](tests/test_validate_brand_multilingual.py). **Addition 11** authors [`BRAND_LOCALISED_FORMATS.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/canonicals/BRAND_LOCALISED_FORMATS.md) canonical (per-locale number / currency / date format rules; en `1,234.56` / fr `1 234,56` with `U+202F` / es `1.234,56`; EUR symbol position locale-specific; ISO 8601 + per-locale natural-long/short date formats). Chassis surfaces `NumberFormatRule` + `CurrencyFormatRule` + `DateFormatRule` + `parse_localised_format_rules` activate when canonical exists on disk (graceful absence). **Tier 1 Vale deterministic-NLP sibling** (folded in 2026-05-14 from I71 P1 strategic review per the operator's "do I have to put a never-ending list of words here?" surfacing the regex-cardinality concern) ships [`scripts/generate_vale_styles.py`](scripts/generate_vale_styles.py) one-time deterministic generator (~500 LOC; reads brand canonicals via `akos.brand_voice_register` parsers + `CANONICAL_PATHS`; emits `.vale/styles/Holistika/{LLMToneTells,TicFamilies,MBADeckJargon}.yml` + `.vale/styles/Vocab/{Holistika,Holistika-rejected}.txt`); [`.vale.ini`](.vale.ini) at repo root carries `StylesPath = .vale/styles` + `MinAlertLevel = warning` per C-71-Vale-1 default + single Vocab pair per C-71-Vale-2 default; generator supports `--check` (CI drift detection) + `--dry-run`; release-gate row "BRAND voice Vale sibling (deterministic-NLP layer; ...)" wired adjacent to the existing voice-register row per D-IH-71-J in-place-extension policy; host-conditional SKIP when `vale` binary absent; 21-case test suite at [`tests/test_vale_styles_generator.py`](tests/test_vale_styles_generator.py) (deterministic output + Vale-YAML validity + Vocab correctness + `--check` semantics + graceful skip on absent canonical). Vale runs alongside the regex chassis — regex catches named violations cheaply per the I71 P1 strategic-review synthesis (3-tier evolution); Vale catches grammar patterns regex cannot express. **Tier 2** (LLM-as-judge advisory layer) parked as I78 candidate; **Tier 3** (writer-facing inline UX) deferred behind team-scale trigger. 4 new decisions `D-IH-71-L` (Pack A2 ratification) / `D-IH-71-M` (Pack A3 ratification) / `D-IH-71-N` (Addition 11 ratification) / `D-IH-71-O` (Tier 1 Vale sibling architecture ratification). `OPS-71-1` notes appended; `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-71` notes appended (P2 SHIPPED). [`CANONICAL_REGISTRY.csv`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/CANONICAL_REGISTRY.csv) registers `brand_localised_formats` (110 rows total). [`PRECEDENCE.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/canonicals/PRECEDENCE.md) Brand voice foundation row extended to include `BRAND_LOCALISED_FORMATS.md` + `gantt-pack.yml` + `multilingual-pack.yml` and the two new validator scripts. Chassis grew from 517 LOC (P1 baseline) to ~1440 LOC (P2 additions); 7 new BaseModels + 7 new parsers shipped additively (no signature changes to existing P1 models; 28-case P1 test suite remains 28/28 PASS). Phase report at [`p2-pack-a2-a3-addition-11-vale-2026-05-14.md`](docs/wip/planning/71-cicd-discipline-and-aiops-baseline-maturity/reports/p2-pack-a2-a3-addition-11-vale-2026-05-14.md).

- **Initiative-planning template SCOPE guardrail (2026-05-14):** Operator correction landed — I70 is the SOTA reference shape (one strategic plan per **initiative**, not per phase), but the I71 P1 plan at `.cursor/plans/i71_p1_pack_a1_brand_voice_register_bcb06a90.plan.md` is phase-scoped, which is a documented divergence. Added an explicit binding **PLAN SCOPE guardrail** to all 7 kickoff prompts ([generic prompt trio](docs/wip/planning/_templates/initiative-planning-prompts.md) Prompt 2 + [I71](docs/wip/planning/_templates/i71-kickoff-prompt.md) / [I72](docs/wip/planning/_templates/i72-kickoff-prompt.md) / [I73](docs/wip/planning/_templates/i73-kickoff-prompt.md) / [I74](docs/wip/planning/_templates/i74-kickoff-prompt.md) / [I75](docs/wip/planning/_templates/i75-kickoff-prompt.md) / [I76](docs/wip/planning/_templates/i76-kickoff-prompt.md)) that loud-forbids phase-scoped, strand-scoped, super-strand-scoped, per-discipline, or AICs-framing-scoped plan files; mandates a single `.cursor/plans/<initiative-slug>_<8hex>.plan.md` covering all phases of the initiative; and codifies regression rounds (Round 1 / Round 2 / ...) as in-file depth-drill, not as plan-file fragmentation. Added a new "Plan-scope principle (binding)" section to the [`_templates/README.md`](docs/wip/planning/_templates/README.md). The existing I71 P1 plan stays in place as historical artifact (Pack A1 already shipped successfully); future I71 phases (P2–P6) and all of I72–I76 follow the I70 single-plan pattern.

- **I70 prep — `akos-inline-ratification.mdc` cursor rule (H1 deliverable; 2026-05-12)** — Codifies the inline-`AskQuestion` ratification pattern as the standard operator-input gate for ALL future Cursor plannings (not just I70). Replaces the real-stop `OPERATOR PAUSE POINT` anti-pattern with the inline-ratify gate where the agent runs the evidence sweep + surfaces ranked options via `AskQuestion` in the same chat session. Defines three gate types (`inline-ratify` / `stop-and-clarify` / `ratified-at-planning`); narrows the `opt-stop-report` posture to genuine blockers (validator failures, broken builds, missing files, security incidents) per [`.cursor/rules/akos-governance-remediation.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-governance-remediation.mdc); cross-references [`.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc) plan-quality bar; worked example cites I70 Phase 8 §8.7 GOI class regression hunt. `alwaysApply: true`; glob targets `docs/wip/**/*.md` + `.cursor/plans/**/*.plan.md` + `**/*.plan.md`. Authored as the **first execution action** for plan I70 ([`.cursor/plans/holistika_os_self-governance_foundation_63841b81.plan.md`](.cursor/plans/holistika_os_self-governance_foundation_63841b81.plan.md)) per H1 ratification before Phase 0 SUEZ deck polish begins.

- **P13 — Workspace blueprint canonical + two-root engagement model + GOI/POI maturity ramp + canonical-CSV tranche + inbound shell + retire `Projects/` (2026-05-11)** — Seven-phase initiative bringing structural standardisation to the Holistika workspace per operator request for "order and standardisation for scalability". Lands as seven bisectable commits (`5b9f640` synthesis → `2a586e3` blueprint → `f71869b` maturity-ramp pause → `260467b` templates → `42298e5` CSV-tranche pause → `244824f` inbound-shell sweep → P13.6 closure) covering 10 decisions (D-W13-A..J) and clearing both operator pause-points (P13.2 enum reconciliation; P13.4 combined CSV tranche). **P13.0 — Canonical dig synthesis** at [`docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/p13-0-canonical-dig-2026-05-11.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/p13-0-canonical-dig-2026-05-11.md) consolidates the ontology (five engagement types, two physical roots, three semantic directions), GOI/POI gap analysis, process_list state, folder shape inventory, and role-owner canonical contract; documents the pre-existing `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY` failure (`INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-68` references missing decision `D-IH-66-AC`) as out of P13 scope. **P13.1 — Canonical blueprint** at [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT_HOLISTIKA.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT_HOLISTIKA.md) — 10 sections covering (1) sub-mark functional split (Holistika R&S provides intel/canon/SOPs/capabilities; HLK Tech Lab provides technology; Think Big is the engagement arm encompassing both outbound and inbound services), (2) four-channel persistence doctrine (Git SSOT + Google Drive passive sync + SQL mirror `governance.*` views + HLK-ERP incremental operator panels), (3) five engagement types matrix (customer / partner / product / adviser / internal-capacity), (4) two physical roots (`Think Big/Clients/` outbound + `Think Big/Advisers/` inbound), (5) three semantic directions (outbound-external, outbound-internal via reserved `internal-` slug, inbound), (6) per-root folder shape, (7) file-tracking policy (branded PDFs tracked; markdown sidecars ignored), (8) role-owner-canonical vs `compliance/`-tightened-mirror contract (canonical wins on conflict; mirror is machine-readable companion; future fusion reserved). Updates [`Think Big/README.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/README.md), [`Think Big/Clients/README.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/README.md), [`docs/USER_GUIDE.md`](docs/USER_GUIDE.md), and [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) with the doctrine cascade. **P13.2 — GOI/POI class-enum maturity ramp** (Pause-point #1) sharpens [`SOP-HLK_GOIPOI_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/SOP-HLK_GOIPOI_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md) §4.10: `collaborator` requires all four conditions (informal + single-project + no co-branding + no maintenance); `partner` requires at least three (strategic + contractual + co-branded + maintenance-bearing); ramp is one-directional (`collaborator` → `partner` on conditions strengthening, no demotion); canonical example `GOI-PRT-EFA-2026` (EFA Académie); validator (`scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py`) accepts both values without distinguishing maturity (SOP-level operator discipline is the authoritative gate). Pause record at [`docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/reports/p13-2-enum-reconciliation-pause-record-2026-05-11.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/reports/p13-2-enum-reconciliation-pause-record-2026-05-11.md). **P13.3 — Engagement template skeletons** creates two parallel literal copy-target templates: outbound at [`Think Big/Clients/_engagement-template/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/_engagement-template/) (with `00-internal/` + `01-operator-pack/` + `02-customer-pack/` + `_external_marks/` + `_archive/` + `_exports/`) and inbound at [`Think Big/Advisers/_engagement-template/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Advisers/_engagement-template/) (with `00-internal/` + `01-our-pack/` + `02-adviser-pack/` + `_archive/` + `_exports/`; no `_external_marks/` since we are the customer and advisers brand themselves). Each sub-folder carries a `.gitkeep` for git presence + a README documenting its audience and purpose. **P13.4 — Canonical-CSV tranche** (Pause-point #2) adds the `related_party` column to [`GOI_POI_REGISTER.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/GOI_POI_REGISTER.csv) (enum `{'', 'true', 'false'}`; empty default is backwards-compatible for every Initiative 21 / 22 / 24 / 31 row); appends new row `GOI-CUS-ASES-2026` with `related_party=true` (sister's hospitality SME advisory engagement); appends new role `Holistik Researcher Trainee` to [`baseline_organisation.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/baseline_organisation.csv) (access_level=2; rates 25/35/50; reports_to=`Holistik Researcher`); extends [`akos/hlk_goipoi_csv.py`](akos/hlk_goipoi_csv.py) `GOIPOI_REGISTER_FIELDNAMES` tuple; extends [`scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py`](scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py) with `RELATED_PARTY` enum + row-level validation; adds [`tests/test_validate_goipoi_register.py`](tests/test_validate_goipoi_register.py) covering column presence, ordering, default-empty backward compatibility, enum validation, and `GOI-CUS-ASES-2026` row presence; adds [`supabase/migrations/20260511020000_p13_4_goipoi_related_party.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260511020000_p13_4_goipoi_related_party.sql) (SQL forward migration for `compliance.goipoi_register_mirror`); refreshes [`PRECEDENCE.md`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/PRECEDENCE.md); re-renders [`TOPIC_PMO_CLIENT_DELIVERY_HUB.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/TOPIC_PMO_CLIENT_DELIVERY_HUB.md) autogen section (sha256 verified); scaffolds [`Think Big/Clients/2026-asesoria-hosteleria/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/2026-asesoria-hosteleria/) from the outbound template. Pause record at [`reports/p13-4-csv-tranche-pause-record-2026-05-11.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/reports/p13-4-csv-tranche-pause-record-2026-05-11.md). **P13.5 — Inbound shell + retire `Projects/` + cross-link sweep** creates [`Think Big/Advisers/README.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Advisers/README.md) (inbound root with naming conventions, per-engagement inbound minimum-viable shape, active engagements table); creates [`Think Big/Advisers/2026-holistika-incorporation/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Advisers/2026-holistika-incorporation/) (unified inbound entry point for the founder-incorporation program, cross-linking the canonical role-owner SOPs at `Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/`, `People/Compliance/`, `Operations/PMO/`, and the `ADVISER_*` CSV registers; folder is a pointer, not a content store); retires `Think Big/Projects/` per D-W13-I (every Think Big folder is now a project-shaped engagement under `Clients/` or `Advisers/`; internal-capacity engagements use the reserved `<YYYY>-internal-<slug>/` prefix under `Clients/`); updates [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/index.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/index.md) Think Big subtree to reflect the two-root model; refreshes the PMO hub Case-layer + Think Big bundle structure (autogen markers untouched); broadens [`docs/wip/planning/_candidates/customer-engagements-2026.md`](docs/wip/planning/_candidates/customer-engagements-2026.md) scope from outbound-only to outbound + inbound (filename retained for link stability per the references in `WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT_HOLISTIKA.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, the SUEZ P1 checkpoint, and `SOP-ENG_ESTIMATION_DISCIPLINE_001`); adds a new doc-sync row in [`.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) covering the `WORKSPACE_BLUEPRINT` → Think Big READMEs + `index.md` + PMO hub + engagement candidate cascade so future blueprint edits trigger lock-step downstream updates. **P13.6 — Closure** at [`docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/p13-6-closure-2026-05-11.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/p13-6-closure-2026-05-11.md) consolidates the canonical-asset state, the two pause-point outcomes, the verification posture, and a six-item carry-over backlog (pre-existing `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY` hygiene; role-owner / `compliance/` fusion candidate; engagement folder activation discipline; `render_pmo_hub.py` inbound extension; Drive sync verification; future `governance.engagement_registry` operator panel). Drops the local `_savepoint_phase_walk_2026-05-10` rollback branch (the seven P13 commits are now on `main`; the savepoint is no longer needed). Verification: `validate_hlk_vault_links.py` PASS; `validate_goipoi_register.py` PASS (11 rows); `tests/test_validate_goipoi_register.py` PASS; `render_pmo_hub.py --check` PASS (sha256 unchanged); `validate_hlk.py` carries the same pre-existing `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY` failure flagged in P13.0 §7 (out of P13 scope). Adheres to the I66 / I68-Round-2 plan-quality bar codified in [`.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc) §"Plan-quality bar".

- **I12 P12 — SUEZ-WeBuy customer-pack EFA co-branding + impeccable customer deck + cohesive engagement folder + co-branding canonical (2026-05-10)** — Operationalises the SUEZ-WeBuy first-customer engagement's customer-pack pivot in response to operator feedback after a partner debrief: the partner-lead from EFA Académie (incumbent operator of the SUEZ procure-to-pay process; collaboration started October 2025 — see [GOI-PRT-EFA-2026 / POI-PRT-EFA-LEAD-2026 notes update](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/GOI_POI_REGISTER.csv) per [P12.6 pause record](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/p12.6-pause-record-2026-05-10.md)) co-presents the dossier and will assume post-launch maintenance under [proposal §3 Continuité opérationnelle posture A](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/02-customer-pack/proposal.customer.fr.md). Three concurrent strands shipped together. **Strand A — co-branding canonical**: net-new [`BRAND_COBRANDING_PATTERN.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_COBRANDING_PATTERN.md) defines host/guest semantics (host = Holistika by default; guest gets the cover-strip 4th field + slide-02 host-card right side; polarity flips on EFA-hosted projects), the host-card primitive (asymmetric 2-column block; teal accent rail for host, muted rail for guest; mono-flattened guest mark; 16mm logo size), color-bridge rules (borrow-one rule: borrow either host accent OR guest accent into the cobranded layout, never both; never-collapse rule: keep the two marks visually distinct; logo color flattening when needed for contrast), typography deference (host typography wins; guest microcopy uses host typography), polarity-flip clause (when guest hosts the engagement, all host/guest swaps in one direction without renaming primitives), and 6 anti-patterns specific to co-branding. Cross-linked from [`BRAND_LOGO_SYSTEM.md` §2.6 + §8](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_LOGO_SYSTEM.md) and [`BRAND_VISUAL_PATTERNS.md` §3.5 + cross-refs](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_VISUAL_PATTERNS.md). **Strand B — folder migration to a cohesive engagement home**: SUEZ artifacts moved from the dispersed `_assets/advops/shared/2026-suez-webuy/` location to the canonical client-engagement home at [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/) with audience-segmented sub-folders (`00-internal/checkpoints/` for pause records, `01-operator-pack/` for operator-and-collaborator surfaces, `02-customer-pack/` for the customer-facing deliverables, `_archive/2026-05-10-pre-efa-collab/` snapshot for rollback, `_external_marks/` for guest brand assets [EFA logo + light-bg variant + on-white variant + provenance README], `_exports/` for rendered PDFs [gitignored except `.gitkeep` + `README.md`]). [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think Big/Clients/README.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/README.md) created defining naming conventions, per-engagement shape, and the active-engagement registry; [`scripts/render_suez_engagement_pdfs.py`](scripts/render_suez_engagement_pdfs.py) rewritten to use audience-segmented `OPERATOR_PACK` / `CUSTOMER_PACK` paths with `_audience_dir()` resolver; [`.gitignore`](.gitignore) extended with `temp-move-or-delete/` (consistency) and `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think Big/Clients/*/_exports/*` (PDFs regenerate deterministically). Original location replaced by a redirect-stub README pointing to the new home. Live back-references updated in [`docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/README.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/README.md), [`counterparty-brief.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/counterparty-brief.md), [`elicitation-plan.md`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/elicitation-plan.md), and [`docs/wip/planning/_candidates/customer-engagements-2026.md`](docs/wip/planning/_candidates/customer-engagements-2026.md). **Strand C — render pipeline primitives + customer-pack rewrite**: [`akos/hlk_pdf_render.py`](akos/hlk_pdf_render.py) extended with the co-branding rendering surface — `_COVER_STRIP_LABELS` adds a `collaboration` key with FR / EN / ES translations (`En collaboration avec` / `In collaboration with` / `En colaboración con`); `_build_cover_html()` accepts a `collaboration_partner` kwarg and emits a 4th cover-strip item per `BRAND_COBRANDING_PATTERN.md` §3.2; `render_pdf_branded()` signature extends with `collaboration_partner` + `guest_logo_path` + `guest_logo_light_path` (forward-compat with the `inspect.signature` filter pattern already in the render script); `{{EFA_LOGO_URI}}` and `{{EFA_LOGO_LIGHT_URI}}` Markdown placeholder substitutions mirror the existing `{{MONOGRAM_URI}}` pattern so deck markdown can reference guest marks without hard-coding paths; new `_brand_pdf_css_slides()` primitives `.host-card` (slide 02 — Holistika ↔ EFA Académie asymmetric 2-col with teal-vs-muted rails + 16mm marks + slide-foot italic), `.three-lines` (slides 03 + 05 — three stacked typographic rows with teal accent rail per line, no card boxing), `.method-anchors` (slide 04 — 3 numbered anchor cards with teal top accent for the method-lineage slide), `.grid-3x2` (slide 09 — 3-col × 2-row feature grid + `.cell.quiet` modifier with muted-bg + dashed border for the "Compétence interne préservée" out-of-scope tile), `.slide-tight` modifier class (slides hosting `grid-3x2` opt in to tighter top metrics — fixes a 14-page-instead-of-13 overflow caught at P12.8 when the litige-module 5th feature card pushed the grid past the 210mm landscape budget), and a CSS `:has()`-free implementation honoring WeasyPrint 52.5 selector-support limits. All new colour values pass through `_to_legacy_hsl()` at the emit boundary preserving the P10 drift safeguard. New customer-facing slide deck at [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/02-customer-pack/deck.customer.fr.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/02-customer-pack/deck.customer.fr.md) — 13 slides of impeccable design: 01 dark-gradient cover with 4-field cover-strip including `En collaboration avec EFA Académie`; 02 EFA host-card (mission-portée-conjointement framing + asymmetric host/guest cards); 03 Holistika in three lines (recherche / opérations / technologie discipline); 04 Deux récits une seule discipline (method-lineage anchors with three discipline-anchor reflexes: qualité-donnée / gouvernance multinationale / projet-vers-logiciel); 05 Comment nous travaillons aujourd'hui (KM + AKOS-MADEIRA orchestration named in customer-readable prose; reworded at P12.8 to drop "votre cahier des charges" loaded phrase per D-12-17 discretion); 06 Le constat (stat-narrative `20 ↔ 50` / 23 / <2% / 5 — "vingt à cinquantaine de demandes par jour, selon la cadence du parc"); 07 Notre lecture (4-cell grid; cell 04 reframed to "Le coût caché : les litiges" per the SUEZ-CDC's own primary signal); 08 Ce que nous construisons (4-step Lecture / Composition / Revue / Soumission flow); 09 Cinq fonctionnalités, parmi les vingt-quatre identifiées (5 feature cards F·01..F·05 including the new F·05 litige module + 1 quiet "Compétence interne préservée" out-of-scope card preserving operator competency); 10 Notre méthode (5-temps timeline); 11 Trois variantes (A Cadrage / B Prototype recommended / C Industrialisation); 12 Critères de réussite (4 engagements: 3 KPI commitments [<2% factures sans numéro de commande / <5 jours résolution / 100% couverture du registre] + 1 Continuité opérationnelle); 13 Prochaine étape closing (atelier de lancement 90 minutes; closing-sig includes EFA Académie attribution). Customer proposal at [`proposal.customer.fr.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/02-customer-pack/proposal.customer.fr.md) updated with collaboration metadata frontmatter, 5-signals reframe (cell 04 = "Le coût caché : les litiges" + repositioned "Compétence interne préservée"), triadic pull-quote ("Automatiser ce qui se calcule, prévenir ce qui se rompt, préserver ce qui se juge"), 5th feature card (Module de prévention et gestion des litiges), new §3 Continuité opérationnelle (postures A operator-led / B Holistika-led + tarification deferred-to-discovery), and 7 critères de réussite (4 operational + 3 KPI commitments). Centerpiece [`cdc-feasibility-shape.fr.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/01-operator-pack/cdc-feasibility-shape.fr.md) gains §11 "Lecture du module de prévention et gestion des litiges" mapping the priority signal to the customer's own framing of the stake (anchors F-25 to F-29 to Besoin 5; KPIs described as "trois indicateurs cohérents avec votre cadre de mesure" rather than explicit CDC article numbers per D-12-17); discovery questionnaire gains B2-bis (volume of open litiges + avg resolution cycle) and B2-ter (litige dashboard integration) probes phrased as natural noun phrases; operator [`proposal.fr.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Think%20Big/Clients/2026-suez-webuy/01-operator-pack/proposal.fr.md) frontmatter mirrors the customer pack's collaboration metadata. **Founder bio architecture**: [`FOUNDER_BIO.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/FOUNDER_BIO.md) preserved as the external canonical (anonymized public-facing variants); new internal companion [`FOUNDER_TRAJECTORY_INTERNAL.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/FOUNDER_TRAJECTORY_INTERNAL.md) created and promoted from `status: draft` to `status: active` + `canonical: true` holding the founder's full personal trajectory with real public-employer names (Multiassistance / IBM / BICG / Econocom / Volvo / GMA Corporate / Europ Assistance-Generali / RCD Legal), intellectual-influence narrative, and method-anchor lineage — `access_level: 5`, never reproduced verbatim on customer-facing surfaces, never quoted in proposals (operator + agent eyes only). **Pause records + checkpoints** under [`docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/`](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/): [P12.0 read-redact](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/efa-redacted.md) (intelligence brief from EFA-related transcripts and PDFs with strict redaction); [voice-corpus.md](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/voice-corpus.md) (operator's natural French idiom: triadic / concrete nouns / diagnosis-then-method / pragmatic / no em-dashes in headlines + EFA partner-lead voice for host-card + founder voice exemplars); [P12.4 voice-fidelity self-checkpoint](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/sc-mid-p12-voice-fidelity-2026-05-10.md) auditing 23 / 23 content blocks via 5-test matrix (0 / 6 D-12-17 patterns, 0 / 14 forbidden tokens, 6 self-deviations defended); [P12.6 pause record](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/p12.6-pause-record-2026-05-10.md) for the GOI/POI notes-only update (validator PASS 10/10 rows; class enum stays `partner` per D-12-7); [P12.8 render-verify checkpoint](docs/wip/intelligence/2026-05-10-suez-webuy-procure-to-pay/checkpoints/p12.8-render-verify-2026-05-10.md) documenting page-count audit + cover-strip 4-field carriage + extended D-12-17 leak-hunt regex set (forbids `votre cahier des charges` *only* in citation form `(article|section|chapitre|§|alinéa|paragraphe) NN (de|du|dans|à) votre cahier des charges` or freeform `comme spécifié | tel que défini ... cahier des charges | CDC | article` within 80 chars — the bare noun phrase remains acceptable rhetoric per the natural-language carve-out) + `.host-card` / `.method-anchors` / `.grid-3x2` rasterized PNG snapshots at `checkpoints/p12.8-rasters/`. Verification: 17 / 17 dossier-renderer tests PASS (`tests/test_render_dossier.py`); `_to_legacy_hsl` boundary test PASS; `scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py` PASS (10 / 10 rows); `scripts/render_suez_engagement_pdfs.py --smoke` PASS — all 7 source markdowns resolve, cover metadata clean; full render PASS — all 7 surfaces (cdc-feasibility-shape.fr.pdf 15p / discovery-questionnaire.fr.pdf 5p / proposal.fr.pdf 8p / deck-suez-webuy.fr.pdf 5p / proposal.customer.fr.pdf 8p / tarification.customer.fr.pdf 3p / **deck.customer.fr.pdf exactly 13p**) rendered with WeasyPrint, customer-pack carries the 4-field cover-strip with EFA Académie (operator pack stays solo-host), 0 hits on the extended leak regex set, slide-02 host-card visually verified showing teal-rail host + muted-rail guest with both 16mm marks + 4-field cover-strip + KPI commitments slide + Continuité section. Files added: Pydantic models at [`akos/sentry_release.py`](akos/sentry_release.py) (`SentryReleaseFormatRule` rejects every non-canonical `release_template`; `SentryReleaseValue` with `repo_slug` + `sha_short` field validators; `parse_release_value()` helper; `RELEASE_TEMPLATE_CANONICAL = '{repo_slug}@{sha_short}'`; `REPO_SLUG_RE` lowercase kebab-case `[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]+[a-z0-9]`; `SHA_SHORT_RE` 7-12 char lowercase hex; `RELEASE_VALUE_RE` for parsing concrete values); validator at [`scripts/validate_sentry_release_format.py`](scripts/validate_sentry_release_format.py) (canonical-doc strict validation always runs; consumer-repo scan opt-in via `AKOS_SENTRY_RELEASE_SCAN_CONSUMERS=1` env var so existing sibling-repo drift does not break `release-gate.py` until P4(f) carry-overs land; scans Next.js `sentry.client.config.ts` + `sentry.server.config.ts` + `sentry.edge.config.ts` + `next.config.mjs withSentryConfig` and Python `main.py`/`app.py`/`sentry_init.py` + `sentry_sdk.init(...)` callsites; accepts known templating placeholders `${process.env.VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA}` + `${RENDER_GIT_COMMIT}` + `os.environ['...']` + `{{...}}`; uses `akos.log.setup_logging` + `akos.io.REPO_ROOT` + `akos.sentry_release` per [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) §"Python Code Standards"); canonical doc at [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Envoy Tech Lab/Repositories/SENTRY_DASHBOARD_HOLISTIKA.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Envoy%20Tech%20Lab/Repositories/SENTRY_DASHBOARD_HOLISTIKA.md) at `status: review` (promoted to `active` in P8) — §1 why-this-doc-not-Sentry-API rationale (3 reasons cross-referenced to D-IH-68-G), §2 canonical release-format contract (D-IH-68-I) with worked examples for all 3 platform repos (`boilerplate@74f9a95d`, `hlk-erp@a3b1c92e`, `kirbe-platform@5fe2d18b`), §3 per-platform Sentry init code (Next.js Vercel + Python FastAPI Render with `tracesSampleRate: process.env.VERCEL_ENV === 'production' ? 1.0 : 0.0` skip-on-preview pattern), §4 dashboard YAML configuration (6 widgets: deploy-success-rate-7d/30d, build-time-trend-30d, source-map-upload-health-7d, release-volume-sparkline-30d, error-rate-by-release-7d) operator pastes into Sentry UI editor, §5 deliberate omissions (no per-repo nested dashboards, no alerting rules, no PR-level visual regression diffs, no build-time alerts), §6 migration plan when D-IH-68-G re-opens (Sentry → Datadog → self-hosted Grafana), §7 cross-references; pytest suite at [`tests/test_validate_sentry_release_format.py`](tests/test_validate_sentry_release_format.py) (34 tests covering Pydantic models valid/invalid pairs + canonical doc presence + validator default-soft + explicit-strict + Next.js + Python release-string regex extraction + canonical pass + missing-`@` flag + wrong-repo-prefix flag + dash-separator flag + Vercel + Render template-placeholder accept + garbage SHA flag + 3 constants smoke-checks; module-level `pytestmark = pytest.mark.cicd`); P4 mid-phase pause record at [`reports/p4-pause-record-2026-05-10.md`](docs/wip/planning/68-cicd-discipline-and-observability-maturity/reports/p4-pause-record-2026-05-10.md). Files modified: [`scripts/release-gate.py`](scripts/release-gate.py) wires `run_sentry_release_format_validation()` after `run_playwright_baseline_validation()` (env-var-aware label distinguishing soft vs strict modes); [`config/verification-profiles.json`](config/verification-profiles.json) `cicd_baseline_check` profile extended with `validate_sentry_release_format` step; [`.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) gains a new doc-sync row mapping the canonical Sentry dashboard doc to ALL downstream surfaces (Pydantic constants + validator + tests + per-consumer-repo Sentry init sites for Next.js + Python + `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` Observability section + `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` operator runbook + I68 P5 SOP-CICD_BASELINE_001 + `akos-deploy-health.mdc` §"Step 1"). [`files-modified.csv`](docs/wip/planning/68-cicd-discipline-and-observability-maturity/files-modified.csv) appended with 9 new rows. Two new decisions appended to [`decision-log.md`](docs/wip/planning/68-cicd-discipline-and-observability-maturity/decision-log.md): **D-IH-68-K** (architectural reframe — InfraMonitor is a product-brand namespace under HLK Tech Lab sibling to MADEIRA / KiRBe / ENVOY; InfraHealth is its first module; v0 ships in `hlk-erp` reusing the I62 chassis structurally not hierarchically; SaaS spin-out is the **I69 candidate** scaffolded at P8) and **D-IH-68-L** (route namespace `/operator/infra-monitor/` shell + `/operator/infra-monitor/health/` first sub-route + `[repo-slug]/` drill-in — future modules slot in as additional sub-routes without renames). Two new risks appended to [`risk-register.md`](docs/wip/planning/68-cicd-discipline-and-observability-maturity/risk-register.md): **R-IH-68-11 NEW** (InfraMonitor module-namespace prematurely couples with future SaaS multi-tenancy — mitigated by single-tenant v0 + ESM-bundled per-route modules + multi-tenant boundary deferred to I69 P0 + structural-not-hierarchical chassis sharing) and **R-IH-68-12 NEW** (Argos GitHub App PR-from-fork permission constraint — mitigated by `pull_request_target` + `vetted-by-owner` label pattern documented in P3).
