Adheres to the I66 / I68-Round-2 plan-quality bar codified as a permanent contract in [`.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc) §"Plan-quality bar".

- **Plan-quality bar codified as cursor-rule contract (2026-05-10)** — Codifies the I66 / I68-Round-2 plan-quality patterns as a permanent contract in [`.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc) so every future ≥5-phase Cursor plan (or ≥1 calendar week, or canonical-CSV-touching, or multi-repo, or revision) inherits the higher bar by default — operators no longer need to ask. The new `## Plan-quality bar (I66 / I68 precedent)` section sits between `## Governance Content Requirements` and `## Quality Checks` and specifies eight sub-requirements: when-applies criteria, multi-sentence YAML frontmatter `todos:` template (with stable `id`s, file paths, validator names, pause-point classification, self-checkpoint counts), Round-expansions narrative for revisions (`## What changed since <prior-version>` with `### Round N` sub-blocks), three mermaid diagrams (architecture / module-or-sub-component / phase-dependency), per-phase deep-section template (Scope / Files canonical+mirrored+validators+templates / Verification / Pause-point classification / Self-checkpoint count / Cursor-rules adherence), inline decision-log preview table (`D-IH-NN-X` ID + question + owner + status + close-out phase, with NEW flags on revision), inline risk-register preview table (`R-IH-NN-N` with NEW / DROPPED flags on revision), [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) §"Python Code Standards" adherence callouts inline in each phase that mints validators (Pydantic models in `akos/<module>.py`, type hints, `akos.log.setup_logging`, `akos.process.run` with timeout, cross-platform `pathlib`, `tests/test_<module>.py` with valid + invalid pairs, `release-gate.py` + `verification-profiles.json` wiring), and a closing file-path density rule (every commit / file / validator / route / mirror / view / template / SOP must carry a clickable markdown link on first mention). Cross-reference bullet added to [`.cursor/rules/akos-agent-checkpoint-discipline.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-agent-checkpoint-discipline.mdc) `## Cross-references` linking to the new section as the authoring contract that produces the per-phase pause-record-friendly deliverables that rule tracks. Doc-sync trigger row in [`.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) refreshed to mention the new `plan-quality bar` aspect. Reference plans cited inline in the new section: I66 (`~/.cursor/plans/brand_vision_ops_sweep_4f4c51dd.plan.md`) and I68 Round 2 (`~/.cursor/plans/i68_cicd_activation_roadmap_592a78e2.plan.md`). No code changes; no migration burden on existing plans; plans below the bar (1-3 phase quick fixes, single-file refactors) keep using the lighter `Governance Content Requirements` contract.

- **I66 P8 follow-up — Founder-only intelligence panel + SOP filename corrections (2026-05-10)** — Raises `/governance/intelligence` to access level 6 (founder-only) by adding a longer-prefix rule in `hlk-erp/lib/auth/route-matrix.ts` so it overrides the generic `/governance` rule, updates the page guard to `requireLevel(6)`, and adjusts page copy and access counter to reflect founder-only status. Aligns three I66 planning docs (`evidence-matrix.md`, `scope-compendium.md`, `journeys-2026-05-08.md`) with the live SOP filename `SOP-ENG_DISCOVERY_QUESTIONNAIRE_001.md`.

- **I66 P8 closure — I66 closed + I67 RevOps Discovery scaffolded (2026-05-09)** — Closes Initiative 66 in `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv`, adds the gated successor `INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-67`, seeds I67 with a research-first scaffold (`master-roadmap.md`, `research-brief.md`, `starting-hypotheses.md`, `decision-points.md`, `sources-and-prior-art.md`, `AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `files-modified.csv`), adds I66 closure UAT, mirrors D-IH-66-A..T plus closure into `DECISION_REGISTER.csv`, and opens three operator handoff rows in `OPS_REGISTER.csv` for trademark filing, Supabase migration application, and I67 kickoff. Verification: `validate_hlk`, `validate_decision_register`, and `validate_initiative_registry` pass.

- **I66 P7 — Vision and dossier-companion drift gates (2026-05-09)** — Adds `validate_brand_vision_drift.py` and `validate_dossier_companion_drift.py`, wires both into `release-gate.py` as hard checks, and extends `tests/test_validate_brand_drift_gates.py` to 33 tests. The vision gate checks `BRAND_VISION.md` public-region markers against boilerplate `/vision` EN messages and route section usage. The dossier companion gate requires every P6 deck to have `.objections.md` and `.counterparty-brief.md` companions and rejects internal-register vocabulary in public deck bodies. Adds P7 deliberate-drift evidence and P7 pause record under the I66 reports folder. Verification: both validators pass and 33 brand drift tests pass.

- **I66 P6 increment 2 — Brand-template and engagement-intelligence operator projections (2026-05-09)** — Adds Supabase migration `20260509213000_i66_p6_brand_template_and_intelligence_views.sql` with two read-side governance views: `governance.brand_template_registry` and `governance.engagement_intelligence_view`. Adds hlk-erp read-only operator panels at `/governance/brand-templates` and `/governance/intelligence`, backed by new `lib/brand-ops/fetcher.ts` functions with static fallback rows so the panels remain useful before the remote migration is applied. Adds brand-ops table components and TypeScript row shapes. Verification: `ReadLints` clean on touched files; `pnpm typecheck` confirms no brand-ops errors remain, but still fails on pre-existing hlk-erp type debt in audit-log, `/api/me`, markdown utilities, and `seed-demo`.

- **I66 P6 increment 1 — Marketing/sales ops template foundation (2026-05-09)** — Seeds the P6 canonical template suite before operator panels/views. Adds `FOUNDER_BIO.md` with long/medium/short/one-line variants, anonymized track-record block, per-audience variants, and audience FAQ governed by `SOP-PEOPLE_FOUNDER_BIO_001`. Adds `BRAND_TEMPLATE_REGISTRY.md` as the Markdown SSOT for the future `governance.brand_template_registry` view, including template IDs, owners, voice tiers, drift checks, and the deck companion contract. Adds shared P6 templates under `_assets/advops/shared/`: email signatures, six outreach/follow-up sequences, proposal template, engagement playbook, press kit, onboarding kit, and six deck templates (`investor-12`, `sales-8`, `advisor-4`, `partner-6`, `enisa-8`, `recruiter-6`) each with `.objections.md` and `.counterparty-brief.md` operator-private companions. Verification: `ReadLints` clean on new Markdown; `validate_brand_jargon`, `validate_brand_baseline_reality_drift`, and `validate_hlk` pass.

- **I66 P5 increment 10 — Direct-access `/services` 6 x 3 matrix + `/how-we-work` route (D-IH-66-AD, 2026-05-09)** — Operator decision: create both pages, but do **not** make them publicly navigable yet because RevOps/marketing journeys are not mature enough to expose them broadly. Boilerplate now keeps `/services` and `/how-we-work` as known-route pages only: `metadata.robots` sets `index=false` and `follow=false`; `FloatingNavbar`, `SiteFooter`, home hero CTA, and home CTA no longer link to `/services`; `/how-we-work` is not linked from normal site chrome. `/services` now renders the canonical [`SERVICE_OFFERING_CATALOG.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/SERVICE_OFFERING_CATALOG.md) as a 6 service-domain x 3 delivery-mode matrix (Holistika R&S, Think Big, HLK Tech Lab), with representative engagement paths and a scoped-contact CTA. `/how-we-work` renders a direct-access engagement rhythm (Discover, Shape, Build, Transfer, Close) and explicitly states that it is useful for context but not yet a public promise. New decision D-IH-66-AD added to the I66 decision log; `files-modified.csv` updated with boilerplate + AKOS rows for the increment.

- **I66 P5 follow-up — Complete IntelligenceOps elicitation-template set (2026-05-09)** — Addresses the six untracked `docs/wip/intelligence/_templates/elicitation-template-*.md` files left after the P3/P5 work. These are intentional, coherent internal working templates that complete the audience-template inventory promised by `docs/wip/intelligence/README.md` and governed by `SOP-IO_ELICITATION_DISCIPLINE_001`. Adds six access-level-5 internal templates: `elicitation-template-advisor.md` (advisor onboarding/re-engagement/standing-cadence discovery), `elicitation-template-customer-sme.md` (customer-SME operational discovery workhorse), `elicitation-template-enisa.md` (ENISA evidence-body / reviewer-process discovery + source-graded evidence discipline), `elicitation-template-latam-customer.md` (LATAM customer variant with bilingual EN/ES prompts + regional Spanish-register guidance), `elicitation-template-partner.md` (partner/collaborator bidirectional-fit discovery), and `elicitation-template-recruiter.md` (recruiter/hiring counterparty role-fit viability + recruiter reliability grading). `files-modified.csv` updated with six new rows mapping these to IntelligenceOps processes (`hol_res_prc_elicitation_discipline_001`, `hol_res_prc_counterparty_baseline_assess_001`, plus engagement / reliability / report processes where relevant).

- **I68 charter — CICD Discipline + Observability Maturity + Render MCP auth troubleshooting (2026-05-09)** — Locked future launch initiative [`68-cicd-discipline-and-observability-maturity/`](docs/wip/planning/68-cicd-discipline-and-observability-maturity/) after the I66 P5 deploy-fix incident. Scope: visual regression (Argos recommended, Lost Pixel fallback), multi-viewport Playwright suite, Sentry deploy-health telemetry, GitHub Actions / Vercel CI baseline, build-time discipline (<2 min preview target where justified), and InfraMonitor v0 read-only operator dashboard. Ships `master-roadmap.md` (8 phases P0–P8; gates on I66 closure), `decision-log.md` (D-IH-68-A..J), `asset-classification.md`, `risk-register.md` (R-IH-68-1..10), `evidence-matrix.md`, and `files-modified.csv` 18-column seed. Generalises `.cursor/rules/akos-deploy-health.mdc` away from boilerplate-specific examples into consumer-repo/platform-agnostic deploy health discipline (Vercel, Render, future hosts). Adds [`reports/render-mcp-auth-troubleshooting-2026-05-09.md`](docs/wip/planning/68-cicd-discipline-and-observability-maturity/reports/render-mcp-auth-troubleshooting-2026-05-09.md): current Render MCP state is `list_workspaces({}) → unauthorized`, while `get_selected_workspace({}) → no workspace set`; conclusion is MCP runtime credential/session invalid, not merely missing workspace selection. Recovery checklist: reload Render MCP server in Cursor, disable/enable if needed, re-auth/plugin reconnect, generate fresh Render API key if needed, then re-test `list_workspaces`, then agent asks operator which workspace ownerID to select before calling `select_workspace`. `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv` row added at `status=charter`; planning README row added. Validators: `validate_hlk` + `validate_initiative_registry` pending in same batch.

- **I66 P5 increment 6 — Vercel deploy-fix sequence + mobile-UX responsive fixes + recurrent CICD discipline rule (D-IH-66-AB, D-IH-66-AC, 2026-05-09)** — Operator surfaced: "all deployments in Vercel failed". Investigation via `user-vercel` MCP `list_deployments` + `get_deployment_build_logs` revealed `boilerplate` had **16 of 20 deployments in ERROR state** since the I66 P5 increment 1 work; `hlk-erp` was 20/20 READY (clean); `kirbe-frontend` had recent prod builds READY. **5 sequential build failures fixed** in `boilerplate`: (1) **TypeScript declaration-merge conflict** — duplicate `declare global { interface Window { gtag?: ... } }` in `components/contact/contact-form.tsx` (plain function shape) and `components/forms/ProjectIntakeForm.tsx` (function-with-getAll shape) merged into incompatible intersection; fixed by canonical declaration in `types/global.d.ts` (boilerplate `798f9faf`). (2) **Sentry CLI 504 gateway timeout** on `releases new` endpoint blocking builds — fixed by `errorHandler` in `withSentryConfig` (logs as webpack warning, build proceeds) + `disableServerWebpackPlugin` / `disableClientWebpackPlugin` gated on `VERCEL_ENV !== 'production'` (skips Sentry source-map upload on previews; saves 60-180s/build) (`5607ba79`). (3) **Static prerender failure on `/dashboard/erp/intakes`** — module-level `const intakeQueries = new IntakeQueries()` calling Supabase `createClient()` without env vars; partial fix `force-dynamic` (`7cca3ba1`); final fix lazy singleton `getIntakeQueries()` getter called inside component (`5eda41ac`). **Build now passes**: deploy `dpl_8BAdip7enzEbK3Gj5DSywzZWsDuf` (commit `5eda41ac`) `READY` in 4m54s; deploy `dpl_3JB9ss2EGVuRTobXBCEziFNPGwyc` (commit `74f9a95d`) `READY` in **2m44s** (≈ operator's <2min target after Sentry-skip optimization). **Mobile UX fixes** (boilerplate `74f9a95d`) caught by browser-use multi-viewport smoke test (375×667, 414×896, 768×1024, 1280×800, 1920×1080): (a) **FloatingNavbar mobile overflow** — all 5 navItems + LanguageSwitcher squeezed horizontally without hamburger; rewritten to two-pattern: `hidden md:flex` desktop inline + `flex md:hidden` mobile compact pill (Holistika logo + Menu/X hamburger button) + `AnimatePresence` slide-down drawer with vertical link list + auto-close on scroll-down or tap; (b) **`/manifiesto` secondary-bar overlap on mobile** — sticky `pt-20` overlapped floating navbar; fixed `pt-16 md:pt-20` + Share/Bookmark actions gated `hidden sm:flex` (per operator: "lack UX context without login + cluttered the mobile manifesto reader"; preserved at sm+ to keep "vector-focused vibe" for tablet/desktop reading). **Visual smoke test confirmed via authenticated browser**: desktop layout healthy, locale switching EN→ES→FR clean (no `framework` ES anglicism, no `AKOS` token leakage), 8-section `/vision` rendering correctly, `/manifiesto/madeira-agent` with Control Layer naming, `/manifiesto/kirbe` with `role-based access control` prose. **AKOS-side: new cursor rule** [`akos-deploy-health.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-deploy-health.mdc) (D-IH-66-AC; ~190 lines) — codifies the recurrent CICD smoke discipline per operator request: "this process will be recurrent as part of CICD". Covers: tool-selection table (design-time = Impeccable; pre-merge automated = Playwright multi-viewport + Lighthouse + brand drift gates; pre-merge UAT = browser-use; continuous = Vercel/Render MCP polling); 4-step smoke checklist (deploy status → build log inspection → multi-viewport visual smoke → build-time optimization); 5 catalogued build-failure patterns with canonical fixes (declare-global merge / Sentry CLI / static prerender / mobile navbar overflow / sticky-header overlap). files-modified.csv: 12 new rows for the deploy-fix + mobile-UX + cursor-rule sequence. **Decisions encoded**: D-IH-66-AB (canonical declaration patterns + Sentry non-blocking + lazy-init pattern), D-IH-66-AC (recurrent CICD discipline). Cumulative: 26 of 20+ D-IH-66-* decisions encoded.

- **I66 P5 increments 4-5 — Strict-mode promotion in release-gate + new public `/vision` page + canon-bug fix (D-IH-66-AA, 2026-05-09)** — Two clean increments shipped together. **Increment 4 — strict-mode promotion in [`scripts/release-gate.py`](scripts/release-gate.py)**: per [D-IH-66-AA] (this increment), now that boilerplate is jargon-clean (0 hits across 134 scanned files post-P5-incr-3), the `validate_brand_jargon` and `validate_brand_voice_register` drift gates flip from soft-INFO to **strict-FAIL by default** in the release gate. Soft-INFO mode is now an opt-OUT via `AKOS_BRAND_JARGON_SOFT=1` and `AKOS_BRAND_VOICE_REGISTER_SOFT=1` env vars (used during a known-broken state, e.g. a forbidden-token list refactor that temporarily produces signal). The `validate_brand_baseline_reality_drift` gate stays soft until I66 P6 closes (decks need their `.objections.md` + `.counterparty-brief.md` companions per D-IH-66-N). Future regressions in jargon or voice-register now hard-fail CI. **Increment 5 — new public `/vision` page** (boilerplate `a2f22984` on `i32-akos-mirror-seed`): sourced from [`BRAND_VISION.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_VISION.md) `<!-- public-vision:start/end -->` markers (P1 canonical). 200-line page with 8-section structure: hero (Vision label + title + subtitle) → why exists (2 paragraphs) → what we change → how we change it (intro + 3 sub-mark cards: Holistika R&S / Think Big / HLK Tech Lab + flywheel summary) → what this means for you (3 numbered scenarios) → how we got here (founder bio summary; 2 paragraphs) → what we are not (4 disqualifiers + closing) → CTA (Start a project + View services). Tier-1 academic-formal voice per [`BRAND_REGISTER_MATRIX.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_REGISTER_MATRIX.md). **i18n parity in EN + ES + FR**: ~150 new keys total across 3 message files. Spanish per [`BRAND_SPANISH_PATTERNS.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_SPANISH_PATTERNS.md) Tier-1 (academic-formal *usted* register, no anglicisms). French per [`BRAND_FRENCH_PATTERNS.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_FRENCH_PATTERNS.md) Tier-1 + §5.1 (formal *vous* register, no anglicisms — *plateforme agentique de connaissances*, *cadres méthodologiques*, *cercle vertueux*). `nav.vision` key added to all 3 locales. SiteFooter `Company` column now includes Vision link before Manifiesto. **Canon-bug fix discovered + fixed**: `BRAND_VISION.md` public-vision marker section contained `the AKOS knowledge operating system` — `AKOS` is forbidden externally per `BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT.md` §4.1 + the public-vision section is meant to be externally-publishable. Replaced with `the agentic knowledge platform` (preserves the distinctive `agentic / knowledge` framing while dropping the internal codename); same fix cascaded to all 3 boilerplate i18n locales. **Test fix**: `test_strict_empty_fails_when_no_consumer_repos` + `test_graceful_skip_when_no_consumer_repos` made deterministic via `monkeypatch.setattr(brand_jargon, "DEFAULT_CONSUMER_ROOTS", ())` — same pattern as the earlier fix on `test_main_graceful_skip_on_empty`. Validators all green: `validate_brand_jargon` PASS (135 files scanned post-/vision-page; 0 forbidden tokens), `validate_brand_voice_register` PASS, `validate_brand_baseline_reality_drift` PASS, `validate_brand_canon_drift` PASS, `validate_hlk` PASS, 27/27 brand drift gate tests PASS. files-modified.csv: 9 new rows (4 AKOS + 5 boilerplate).

Agent has **NOT auto-remediated** because credential rotation requires GCP Console + Vercel env-var-management access outside AKOS scope and history-rewriting force-push is irreversible + collaborator-disruptive. Inbox documents 5-step operator action plan: (1) revoke key in GCP Console — ≤5 min; (2) audit access logs for unusual access since key was committed — ≤30 min; (3) generate replacement credential + store as Vercel env var; (4) `git rm` from working tree + `.gitignore`; (5) optional history-rewrite via BFG or `git filter-branch --index-filter`. Surface-mention in P5 increment 2 commit footer. Future deliverable flagged: `SOP-SEC_INCIDENT_RESPONSE_001` does not exist; should be chartered in a future security-ops initiative. **Drift signal trajectory**: pre-P5-incr-1 = 13 hits (Control Plane × 5, OPS × 1, RBAC × 4, RRF × 1, OAuth2 × 3 leaked-creds-JSON); post-AKOS path-coverage extension to `i18n/messages/` = 20 hits (i18n locales now scanned, RBAC + RRF in EN/ES/FR variants); **post-this-rename = 14 hits** (Control Plane fully cleared; OPS cleared; remaining hits are RBAC + RRF in `manifiesto/data.ts` + `kirbe-content.tsx` + i18n locales — real signal awaiting next P5 increment, plus 3 OAuth2 in leaked credentials file blocked on operator security action). files-modified.csv: 5 new rows (3 boilerplate file rows + 1 boilerplate commit row + 1 AKOS security inbox row).

- **I66 P5 increment 1.5 — files-modified.csv schema extended to 18 cols + thorough process_ids attribution (D-IH-66-Z, 2026-05-09)** — Operator request: "process_ids being empty is strange as normally we should have a process for each in process_list.csv. Also I'd like to have role_owner there and other relevant that you see fit." **4 new columns added** to the per-initiative `files-modified.csv` schema: `repo` (openclaw-akos / boilerplate / hlk-erp / kirbe — distinguishes AKOS-internal from sibling-repo carry-overs; replaces the earlier `boilerplate:` path-prefix convention), `role_owner` (drawn from `baseline_organisation.csv` `role_name` column — answers "who is accountable"), `area` (MKT / Research / Legal / People / Tech / Admin — matches `process_list.csv` area), `entity` (Holistika umbrella / Think Big / HLK Tech Lab — matches `process_list.csv` entity). **18-column header order**: `phase, commit_sha, commit_date, change_kind, lines_delta, repo, file_path, file_kind, role_owner, area, entity, decision_ids, process_ids, linked_canonicals, validators_run, requires_operator_gate, change_summary, notes`. **`process_ids` re-attributed thoroughly** per the canonical mapping rules: every brand-related file maps to at least `tbi_mkt_prc_brand_canon_mtnce_001` (B1) plus its specific governing process(es); `BRAND_FRENCH_PATTERNS.md` / `BRAND_SPANISH_PATTERNS.md` → B1+B3+B4 (canon+register+jargon); `BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT.md` / `BRAND_ABBREVIATIONS.md` → B1+B4+B16; `BRAND_BASELINE_REALITY_MATRIX.md` → B1+B3+B16; `BRAND_LOGO_SYSTEM.md` → B1+B12; `BRAND_HIERARCHY*` / `TRADEMARK_FILING_STRATEGY*` → B1+B12+B17; `SERVICE_OFFERING_CATALOG.md` → B1+B10+B11; SOPs → 1:1 with their `process_id` frontmatter; legal templates → B15+governing-engagement-processes; drift-gate scripts/tests → B16; boilerplate sibling files → B15+B16+B3 (i18n locales); SiteFooter → B15+B16+B12 (trademark posture). **`role_owner` mapping rules**: Brand canonicals → Brand Manager (`org_031`); IntelligenceOps SOPs + intelligence working space → Holistik Researcher; Legal canonicals/SOPs/templates → Legal Counsel; founder bio SOP → Talent; cursor rules + scripts/release-gate + planning README + process_list.csv + SOP-META + CHANGELOG → System Owner; planning docs under `docs/wip/planning/66-*` → Brand Manager (initiative owner per INITIATIVE_REGISTRY); validate_brand_*.py + tests → Brand Manager (operationally; System Owner for engineering). Cursor rule [`akos-planning-traceability.mdc` §"Per-initiative file-changes CSV"](.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc) updated with the 18-column schema definition + per-column rules. I66 backfilled CSV: 106 rows × 18 cols. Validators: `validate_hlk` PASS, `validate_hlk_vault_links` PASS. D-IH-66-Z added to decisions encoded (24 of 20+).

- **I66 P5 increment 1 — Boilerplate SiteFooter trademark posture + ES anglicism fix + AKOS validator i18n-path coverage (2026-05-09)** — First focused boilerplate-rewrite increment under P5. Per `BRAND_HIERARCHY_AND_TRADEMARK_SCOPE_2026-04.md` (P1) trademark posture + Spanish LSSI-CE Article 10 legal-entity disclosure requirement. **Boilerplate carry-over commit `1654ffd7` on branch `i32-akos-mirror-seed`**: (a) SiteFooter renders new trademark-notice block listing all 7 marks with ™ symbol (`Holistika™, Holistika R&S™, Think Big™, HLK Tech Lab™, MADEIRA™, KiRBe™, ENVOY™ are trademarks of Holistika Research SL — applications pending or registered with EUIPO and OEPM`); (b) bottom bar renders Spanish LSSI-CE Article 10 disclosure (legal entity name + form + NIF placeholder + Madrid registration); (c) copyright line uses `Holistika Research SL` (legal entity per BRAND_HIERARCHY) instead of `Holistika Research` (brand name); (d) i18n parity in EN + ES + FR per `BRAND_SPANISH_PATTERNS.md` Tier-1 + `BRAND_FRENCH_PATTERNS.md` Tier-1 legal-formal register; (e) ES anglicism fix in `services.whyHolistika.differentiators.internalFirst.description` — `framework` → `marco metodológico` per `BRAND_SPANISH_PATTERNS.md` §13. **AKOS-side validator path-coverage fix** — `scripts/validate_brand_voice_register.py`, `scripts/validate_brand_baseline_reality_drift.py`, `scripts/validate_brand_jargon.py` `MESSAGE_RELATIVE_PATHS` / scan-path-tuple extended to include `i18n/messages` (boilerplate uses `i18n/messages/*.json` not the originally-assumed `messages/*.json`). This is what surfaced the pre-existing `framework` anglicism that's now fixed in the same commit. **Test fix**: `test_main_graceful_skip_on_empty` made deterministic via `monkeypatch.setattr(voice_register, "DEFAULT_CONSUMER_ROOTS", ())` so the test passes regardless of whether sibling repos exist locally. Validators: `validate_brand_voice_register` PASS (3 message files scanned across 2 consumer repos; 0 violations; up from previous 0-files-scanned), `validate_brand_baseline_reality_drift` PASS, 27/27 brand drift gate tests PASS. P5 deliverables remaining: 5 manifesto rewrites, home flywheel SVG, /services 6×3 matrix render, /tech-lab rewrite, /vision new page, /how-we-work new page, indigo/slate brand-color fix, boilerplate legal-page integration from P4 drafts, cookie-consent banner, drift-gate strict-mode promotion.

- **I66 P4 follow-up — Per-initiative `files-modified.csv` + cursor-rule mandate (D-IH-66-Y, 2026-05-09)** — Operator request: "deliver a CSV in the initiative folder where you list all files modified and every relevant metadata (the more the better without compromising scalability)... we're having this for all initiatives." **Architecture decision**: per-initiative CSV at `docs/wip/planning/<NN-slug>/files-modified.csv` (NOT a single canonical CSV) — scales naturally without merge conflicts when initiatives execute in parallel; bounded size (50-200 rows max per initiative); local context for grep + diff; aggregation surface already exists via `docs/wip/planning/README.md`. **14-column schema**: `phase,commit_sha,commit_date,change_kind,file_path,file_kind,lines_delta,decision_ids,process_ids,linked_canonicals,change_summary,validators_run,requires_operator_gate,notes`. Per-column governance: `phase` enum (P0/P1/.../P\<N>-H1/P\<N>-H2/P\<N>-followup/P\<N>-closure); `change_kind` enum (created/modified/deleted/renamed/cross-ref-update/data-row-append/frontmatter-only); `file_kind` enum (canonical/mirrored/reference/planning/sop/template/script/test/cursor-rule/changelog/data/skill/config); `decision_ids` + `process_ids` + `linked_canonicals` + `validators_run` as semicolon-lists. **I66 backfill** at [`docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/files-modified.csv`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/files-modified.csv) — 94 rows × 14 columns capturing every file change across 7 commits (P0 1fad5be 14 files; P1 63d84e0 16 files; P2 e9841c6 12 files; P3-H1 9d47041 15 files; P3-H2 bc1fbfe 20 files; P3-followup e5d8179 4 files; P4 37aae21 13 files). **`akos-planning-traceability.mdc` §"Per-initiative file-changes CSV" added** mandating the CSV for every initiative going forward — defines architecture choice (per-initiative not central) + 14-column schema with per-column rules + when-to-update protocol (before/after commit + closure rebuild) + backfill rule + cross-references to docs-config-sync + agent-checkpoint discipline + future validator placeholder. **`akos-docs-config-sync.mdc` §"Planning and initiative docs" extended** with 2 new rows: (a) new initiative folder triggers files-modified.csv seed; (b) any commit landing files under an initiative's folder triggers files-modified.csv row append per the schema. Validators: `validate_hlk` PASS, `validate_hlk_vault_links` PASS. D-IH-66-Y added to decisions encoded (23 of 20+).

- **I66 P4 closure — Trademark filing strategy + 4 legal templates + 3 boilerplate legal-page drafts + naming-governance SOP + operator handoff (2026-05-09)** — Closes pause point #5 (P4 — Trademark + legal templates). Per [D-IH-66-H](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/decision-log.md), agent's role ends at "ready-to-sign" preparation; actual EUIPO + OEPM submission is operator-driven with counsel. **Master canonical**: [`TRADEMARK_FILING_STRATEGY_2026-05.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/TRADEMARK_FILING_STRATEGY_2026-05.md) (~280 lines) — per-mark clearance worklist (6-step EUIPO + OEPM + WIPO Madrid Monitor + corporate-register + domain-name + common-law-usage searches per mark) + per-mark filing-strategy decision matrix (M1 Holistika EUIPO+OEPM word+design Nice 35/41/42; M1d design mark; M2 Holistika R&S EUIPO-only Nice 35/42; M3 Think Big OEPM-only Nice 35/42 [high-collision-driven national scope]; M4 HLK Tech Lab EUIPO-only Nice 35/42; M5 MADEIRA EUIPO+OEPM word+design Nice 9+42 only [collision-driven narrow scope excluding wines/food/tourism/transport/entertainment/hospitality/medical with explicit limitation]; M5a MADEIRA Agent variant; M5d MADEIRA design; M6 KiRBe EUIPO+OEPM Nice 9+42; M7 ENVOY EUIPO+OEPM Nice 9+42 with active-monitoring posture due to collision risk) + ready-to-sign EUIPO TM-1 form data per mark with Spanish + English goods/services blocks + common applicant block (Holistika Research SL Sociedad Limitada) + counsel-handoff 12-item checklist + post-filing operations roadmap. **New canonical SOP**: [`SOP-TRADEMARK_NAMING_GOVERNANCE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/SOP-TRADEMARK_NAMING_GOVERNANCE_001.md) (D-IH-66-V; ~140 lines) — 8-step per-introduction process for adding new sub-mark or product brand: naming candidate review (Branded House compatibility + abbreviation governance + visual canonicals + voice tier mapping) → pre-search collision-risk assessment (generic-phrase / geographic-name / existing-product / cultural-translation risks) → formal clearance (counsel-driven, 6-step) → filing-strategy decision (low/medium/high/very-high-collision routing) → filing prep → mandatory operator-pause-point at Step 6 → counsel-driven filing submission → post-filing handoff to sister monitoring SOPs. **4 legal templates** under [`_templates/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/_templates/): [`LEGAL_TEMPLATE_MSA.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/_templates/LEGAL_TEMPLATE_MSA.md) (~250 lines; 11-section Master Services Agreement; Branded House anchored — Holistika Research SL is sole contracting party regardless of delivery mode; sub-marks named in scope as services rendered, not as parties; standard sections: definitions, engagement, fees, term/termination, performance, personnel, confidentiality, IP allocation per Background-Holistika/Background-Client/Foreground-Client default vesting, liability cap, indemnification, governing-law-Spain), [`LEGAL_TEMPLATE_SOW.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/_templates/LEGAL_TEMPLATE_SOW.md) (~140 lines; per-engagement Statement of Work; integrates SERVICE_OFFERING_CATALOG cell mapping + multi-cell phase structure with phase-deliverable-lock + voice-tier-per-phase + cell-to-cell transition artefacts; references SOP-ENG_PROPOSAL_001 + SOP-ENG_ENGAGEMENT_DESIGN_001), [`LEGAL_TEMPLATE_NDA.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/_templates/LEGAL_TEMPLATE_NDA.md) (~110 lines; mutual NDA executed pre-discovery per SOP-IO_COUNTERPARTY_BASELINE_ASSESSMENT step 2; standard 10-section structure with confidentiality-survival period + return/destruction + no-licence + remedies including injunctive relief), [`LEGAL_TEMPLATE_DPA.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/_templates/LEGAL_TEMPLATE_DPA.md) (~150 lines; GDPR Article 28 Data Processing Agreement; Holistika as Processor of Client data; Annex I subject-matter/duration/nature/purpose/types/categories template + Annex II Technical & Organisational Measures placeholder + Annex III sub-processor list placeholder; Article 28(3) obligations: Controller-instructions-only, confidentiality, security measures, sub-processor authorization, data-subject-rights assistance, breach notification 48h, DPIA assistance, return/deletion at end of services, audit rights). **3 boilerplate legal-page EN drafts** under [`reports/p4-boilerplate-legal-pages/`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/reports/p4-boilerplate-legal-pages/): [`privacy-policy-2026-05.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/reports/p4-boilerplate-legal-pages/privacy-policy-2026-05.md) (Holistika as Controller of website-collected data; 12 sections incl. brand-IP factual-reference rules + no-professional-services-via-Site disclaimer + Spanish-law forum); [`cookie-policy-2026-05.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/reports/p4-boilerplate-legal-pages/cookie-policy-2026-05.md) (4-category cookie classification per AEPD guidance: strictly-necessary / performance / functionality / no-marketing; AEPD/LSSI-CE-compliant consent banner specification with equal-prominence Accept/Reject/Manage). **Operator handoff package**: [`p4-operator-handoff-package-2026-05-09.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/reports/p4-operator-handoff-package-2026-05-09.md) — 3 tracks (trademark filings 1-3 weeks operator-driven; legal template adoption 1 week operator + counsel; boilerplate legal-page integration in P5 scope) + 9-item operator approval checklist. **`process_list.csv` +1 row**: `hol_lgl_prc_trademark_naming_governance_001` under Brand Legal Operations project (1,127 → 1,128 rows). **SOP-META §7.4 cross-ref** updated to register the 17th active I66 SOP. - **I66 P3 closure (Half 2) — 20 process_list.csv rows applied + 8 additional SOPs drafted + 16 SOPs promoted to active + D-IH-66-R sub-mark accountability decision (2026-05-09)** — Closes pause point #4 (canonical-CSV gate) following operator review of the P3 Half 1 tranche proposal. **Operator decision D-IH-66-R** rewrote the tranche scope: sub-marks remain a brand-naming + delivery-mode framework, **NOT** an org-chart role partition. **Zero** new rows in `baseline_organisation.csv` (no Holistika R&S Lead / Think Big Lead / HLK Tech Lab Lead role rows; no Engagement Manager). Sub-mark accountability cascades to existing roles: **Brand Manager** (expression across all sub-marks), **Holistik Researcher** (R&S delivery; already governs the four methodology pillars), **CMO + COO** (Think Big delivery), **CTO** (HLK Tech Lab delivery), **CBO/O5-1** (cross-sub-mark integration; sub-mark introduction/deprecation decisions). Rationale: (a) org-size reality (~2-5 person team) — adding 3 dedicated Lead roles creates empty-team coordinator positions without direct reports; (b) Brand Manager team conflict avoidance — placing sub-mark Leads under Brand Manager would conflate **expression** (Brand Manager domain) with **delivery** (each delivery mode's existing function head); (c) KB drift mitigation — empty Lead nodes in baseline cascade to empty KB pages; (d) existing-role sufficiency — methodology pillars already map cleanly. **Sunset criteria** documented for when to revisit (org > 10-15 people; one sub-mark crosses 50% revenue; dedicated team hire; consistent counterparty pattern asking "who runs sub-mark X specifically"). **20 new rows applied to [`docs/references/hlk/compliance/process_list.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/process_list.csv)** (1,107 → 1,127 rows): 4 project anchors (`tbi_mkt_prj_brand_governance_001`, `hol_res_prj_intelligence_ops_001`, `hol_res_prj_engagement_ops_001`, `hol_lgl_prj_brand_legal_001`) + 16 leaf processes distributed Brand Manager (B1-B4, B10, B15-B16 = 7) / Holistik Researcher (B5-B8, B9, B11 = 6) / Talent (B14 = 1) / Legal Counsel (B12-B13 = 2). **8 new SOPs drafted** at `status: active`: [`SOP-BRAND_REGISTER_MATRIX_REVIEW_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/SOP-BRAND_REGISTER_MATRIX_REVIEW_001.md) (B3 — bi-annual register matrix review), [`SOP-BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT_REVIEW_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/SOP-BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT_REVIEW_001.md) (B4 — quarterly jargon audit registry update), [`SOP-BRAND_TEMPLATE_REGISTRY_MTNCE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/SOP-BRAND_TEMPLATE_REGISTRY_MTNCE_001.md) (B15 — template registry maintenance), [`SOP-BRAND_DRIFT_GATE_OPS_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/SOP-BRAND_DRIFT_GATE_OPS_001.md) (B16 — drift gate operations + strict-mode promotion review), [`SOP-LEGAL_IP_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Legal/SOP-LEGAL_IP_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md) (B13 — quarterly IP register maintenance), and 3 Engagement Operations SOPs under new `Operations/Engagement/` folder: [`SOP-ENG_DISCOVERY_QUESTIONNAIRE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/Engagement/SOP-ENG_DISCOVERY_QUESTIONNAIRE_001.md) (B9 — Holistik Researcher; - **I66 P0 — Brand, Vision, Ops Sweep charter + Impeccable v3.1 upgrade + 5 carry-over commits (2026-05-08)** — Bootstrap of the largest single initiative chartered to date (~5.5 calendar weeks; 42-49 working days; 9 phases P0-P8; 8 explicit operator pause points + ~18 agent self-checkpoints; 20 decisions D-IH-66-A..T; 10 risks; 7 audience journeys). Codifies the **Branded House** architecture (one legal entity Holistika Research SL → one umbrella brand Holistika → three operational sub-marks Holistika R&S + Think Big + HLK Tech Lab → product brand stack MADEIRA + MADEIRA Agent + KiRBe + ENVOY + InfraMonitor 2026 + Financial Analyst 2026), bootstraps the **Baseline Reality** dual-register design (CORPINT-research operating posture grounded in HUMINT FM 2-22.3 internally; translated capability messaging externally per `BRAND_BASELINE_REALITY_MATRIX.md` to be created in P1), bootstraps **HLK abbreviation governance** + **logo audit decisions** (Hi monogram primary, HOLÍSTIKA Research wordmark formal, RGB-rings deprecated, stylized-vs-prose split rule), and at closure scaffolds successor **I67 RevOps Discovery** (research-first; near-empty hypotheses tree of `[UNKNOWN]` markers; 8 operator-blocking decision points; 6-binding-mandate AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS). Ships [`docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/) with 7 governance files: [`master-roadmap.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/master-roadmap.md), [`decision-log.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/decision-log.md) (D-IH-66-A through T — 20 decisions including brand architecture, sub-mark voice tier, trademark filing scope, voice canon scope, manifesto rewrite scope, theme + legacy + vision split decisions, I67 paired-research, agent-checkpoint-as-rule, methodology track-record integration, intelligence working-space, Impeccable upgrade, RevOps naming + scope), [`asset-classification.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/asset-classification.md), [`evidence-matrix.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/evidence-matrix.md), [`risk-register.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/risk-register.md) (R-IH-66-1 through 10 incl. trademark conflict, voice-canon over-fit, drift-gate false positives, calendar slip, HUMINT framework leakage, methodology track-record attribution risk, i18n drift, pause fatigue, EU diacritic filing collision, I67 scaffold under-specification), [`journeys-2026-05-08.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/journeys-2026-05-08.md) (operator + investor + advisor + ENISA + partner + recruiter + customer reading paths), [`scope-compendium.md`](docs/wip/planning/66-brand-vision-ops-sweep/scope-compendium.md). **Impeccable skill upgraded to v3.1** ([`.cursor/skills/impeccable/SKILL.md`](.cursor/skills/impeccable/SKILL.md) + [`.cursor/skills/impeccable/scripts/load-context.mjs`](.cursor/skills/impeccable/scripts/load-context.mjs)) — adds `BASELINE_REALITY.md` as the 5th setup gate (alongside PRODUCT.md + DESIGN.md), nudges once per session if missing on projects with ≥2 distinct audiences in PRODUCT.md, hard-fails only on `/impeccable craft` for surfaces tagged `audience: <multi>`. Loader returns `hasBaselineReality` + `baselineReality` + `baselineRealityPath` fields back-compatibly. **5 carry-over commits** queued for the 2 sibling repos (commit cycle on completion of P0 in their respective repos): hlk-erp/PRODUCT.md (Round 2 sub-mark fix), boilerplate/PRODUCT.md (Round 2 initial creation), boilerplate/DESIGN.md (Round 2), hlk-erp/BASELINE_REALITY.md (Round 3 scaffold; cross-references AKOS canonical to be created in P1), boilerplate/BASELINE_REALITY.md (Round 3 scaffold). **INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv** charter row added (INIT-OPENCLAW_AKOS-66; status: active; owner: Brand Manager; inception: 2026-05-08); registry validator PASS. **Planning README** row 66 added between row 65 and row 60 (numerical order). **Promotion to active same day** justified by the operator's explicit *"Implement the plan as specified"* approval and the full charter package landing in one ship per the I65 precedent.

- **I62 P1 + P3 Supabase migrations applied to `MasterData` (2026-05-07)** — Operator-approved DDL run executed via the `user-supabase` MCP `apply_migration` tool. **P1 — RBAC** ([`supabase/migrations/20260506130000_i62_p1_holistika_ops_rbac.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260506130000_i62_p1_holistika_ops_rbac.sql)) created the `holistika_ops` schema: `user_role_mapping` (self-read + admin-read access_level ≥ 6 + service_role-all), `audit_log` (admin-read + service_role-all; founder impersonation surface), `user_preferences` (self-modify + service_role-all), `notifications` (self-or-broadcast read + self-update + service_role-all), and the `current_access_level()` SECURITY-DEFINER helper function. Migration version `20260507010953`. **P3 — demo schema** ([`supabase/migrations/20260506130200_i62_p3_demo_schema.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260506130200_i62_p3_demo_schema.sql)) created the `demo` schema with 6 mirror tables (`demo.dossier_run`, `demo.eval_run`, `demo.initiative_registry_mirror`, `demo.ops_register_mirror`, `demo.persona_registry_mirror`, `demo.skill_registry_mirror`); permissive read policies for `anon` + `authenticated`, writes restricted to `service_role`; tables stay empty until `scripts/seed-demo.ts` runs in CI on preview deploys. Migration version `20260507011236`. **P2 — `erp.*` views DEFERRED** as **D-IH-62-W** — the SQL references `compliance.eval_run.run_payload` / `kind` / `verdict` / `occurred_at` columns and `compliance.initiative_registry_mirror` / `compliance.ops_register_mirror` tables that don't exist on the remote (column shapes are the I32 P1 schema, not the I59 P1 schema; mirror tables haven't shipped yet). New OPS-62-P11 row owed: ship the mirror DDL tranche + adapt the view DDL to use `run_started_at`/`overall_status`/`lifecycle_status`/`section_metrics`/`adviser_engagement_disciplines_mirror`/`finops_counterparty_register_mirror`. **Both `get_advisors --type security` and `--type performance` returned zero advisories on the new `holistika_ops.*` and `demo.*` schemas**. Evidence: [`reports/migration-application-2026-05-07.md`](docs/wip/planning/62-mission-control/reports/migration-application-2026-05-07.md). Decision: D-IH-62-W in [decision-log](docs/wip/planning/62-mission-control/decision-log.md). hlk-erp impact while P2 stays deferred: `vw_three_lights_status` returns 404, `/mission-control` Today board falls back to `demo.*` rows + "Demo mode" banner, drilldowns show "live data unavailable" — no operator surface breaks.

- **I63 P8 — Secrets walkthrough operated via MCPs (2026-05-07)** — Operator-approved attempt to operate the I63 secrets walkthrough end-to-end via available MCPs. **Discoverable via MCP**: Sentry org/team enumeration (`whoami` → `Fayçal Njoya`; `find_organizations` → `holistika`@de.sentry.io; 4 existing projects, 0 matching `hlk-erp`/`kirbe`); Sentry DSN read for existing projects; Slack channel discovery (only `#general-openclaw-akos` exists; no `#governance` yet); `gh` CLI `secret list` (3 repos confirmed empty). **Not provisionable via MCP**: Sentry **project creation** (HTTP 403 — *"Your organization has disabled this feature for members"* — operator account is member, not org-owner/manager); all org-level token creation (Sentry auth token, Vercel token, GH PAT, Slack webhook) requires UI flows; current `gh` token scopes (`gist, read:org, repo`) miss `admin:org` so org-level secret-set is blocked. **Net residual**: 4 short browser tabs the operator opens (Sentry projects + auth-token, Slack webhook app, GH fine-grained PAT) → 4 `gh secret set` invocations on receipt. Decision **D-IH-63-G** documents the bound: *"MCP-side automation is bounded by Sentry member-tier permissions and the absence of a Slack webhook MCP. Net result: 4 hands-on browser steps, 4 `gh secret set` invocations after."* Reports: [`reports/secrets-mcp-application-2026-05-07.md`](docs/wip/planning/63-external-repo-governance-codification/reports/secrets-mcp-application-2026-05-07.md) (operator checklist) + [`secrets-walkthrough-2026-05-06.md`](docs/wip/planning/63-external-repo-governance-codification/reports/secrets-walkthrough-2026-05-06.md) flips to `status: applied-partial` with a footer pointer.

It's less about writing and interacting, more about being able to grasp what's happening with nice intuitive UI for that."* Original page-spec archived at [`reports/archive/page-spec-v1-2026-05-06.md`](docs/wip/planning/64-governance-mission-control/reports/archive/page-spec-v1-2026-05-06.md); v2 at [`reports/page-spec-2026-05-06.md`](docs/wip/planning/64-governance-mission-control/reports/page-spec-2026-05-06.md) applies the impeccable design framework: 5 named operator user journeys with grasp tests, OKLCH brand color tokens, layout hierarchy (verdict band > 6-panel grid; not identical card grid), explicit rejection of 4 anti-patterns (no traffic-light row tints; no hero-metric template; no identical card grid; no modal as first thought), microcopy rewrite (every label answers "what do I do next?"), motion language (ease-out-quart enters, ease-in-quart exits; tooltips ≤ 200ms). I64 master-roadmap flips `status: charter` → `status: active`.

- **I62 Mission Control impeccable user-journeys report (2026-05-07)** — Companion to the I64 page-spec rewrite. New report [`reports/impeccable-journeys-mc-2026-05-07.md`](docs/wip/planning/62-mission-control/reports/impeccable-journeys-mc-2026-05-07.md) ships 5 named operator journeys (Morning Glance, Verdict Drill-down, Inbox Triage, Cycle Pulse, Demo Tour) with grasp tests (≤ 3 seconds on the Today verdict; ≤ 2 clicks to OPS-58-3 from cold open; ≤ 1.2s INP for Cmd+K palette open) and per-tile polish (Three Lights chip → verdict-shaped halo not a row tint; Today board → typographic hierarchy not card-grid uniformity; Cmd+K → primary surface for ≥ 5-deep navigation). Anti-patterns explicitly rejected: traffic-light row tints, hero-metric template on the Today verdict tile, identical card grid for the 7 tiles, modal-first for the verdict drill-down. Governance chip in the top-bar parallels the I64 governance verdict (sibling pattern, not nested).

- **I65 — AKOS Planning Workspace Panel charter + same-day promotion to `active` (2026-05-07)** — Bootstrap of a new initiative responding to the operator request *"We don't have an AKOS panel where we can see what we do in `docs/wip/planning/`. Let's fix that. I let you decide how."* Sibling to I62 (Mission Control) + I63 (External Repo Governance) + I64 (Governance Mission Control). Will land in `hlk-erp` as a new `/operator/planning/` top-level group with 6 surfaces (index card grid + filter, drill-in with master-roadmap MDX + decision timeline + evidence checklist + risk cards + reports gallery + cross-link rail, Decision Atlas, Operations Queue, Reports Stream, Mission Control hero chip). Phase plan P0 (charter) → P1 (`governance.planning_workspace_view` + GitHub Contents reader + 5-min Supabase fallback cache) → P2 (frontend index) → P3 (drill-in) → P4 (Atlas + Queue + Stream) → P5 (MC chip + RBAC + audit) → P6 (UAT + closure). Three architecture decisions: **D-IH-65-A** panel placement (new top-level `/operator/planning/` group; existing `/operator/initiatives` stays as registry-rolled view; rejected nesting under `/mission-control` to keep MC at 7 tiles); **D-IH-65-B** markdown rendering source (live GitHub Contents API + 60s cache + 5-min Supabase fallback only on outage; rejected Supabase mirror of markdown — git is SSOT — and deploy-time prebuild — staleness intolerable); **D-IH-65-C** time-travel via `?ref=<sha>` query string with date-picker resolver (git-native history via GitHub Contents `?ref=<sha>`; reject Supabase historical snapshots — duplicates git; date-picker resolver via `git rev-list --before`; feature-flagged off if not ready by P3). Charter package ships with full governance artefact set under [`docs/wip/planning/65-akos-planning-workspace-panel/`](docs/wip/planning/65-akos-planning-workspace-panel/): master-roadmap, decision-log (D-IH-65-A/B/C), asset-classification, evidence-matrix, risk-register, [`reports/page-spec-impeccable-2026-05-07.md`](docs/wip/planning/65-akos-planning-workspace-panel/reports/page-spec-impeccable-2026-05-07.md) (audience JTBD + IA + design laws + acceptance criteria), [`reports/journeys-2026-05-07.md`](docs/wip/planning/65-akos-planning-workspace-panel/reports/journeys-2026-05-07.md) (5 user journeys with grasp tests), [`reports/data-model-2026-05-07.md`](docs/wip/planning/65-akos-planning-workspace-panel/reports/data-model-2026-05-07.md) (DDL for `governance.planning_workspace_view` + `governance.workspace_markdown_cache` + TypeScript shapes + GitHub API patterns + Cursor deeplinks + date-to-SHA resolver). **Same-day promotion to `active`** justified by the user's explicit *"let's fix that"* approval and the full charter package landing in one ship.

- **External Repo Bless Pattern + I63 charter (2026-05-06)** — Codifies the cross-repo governance contract that I62 surfaced. Ships an executable scaffolder + a posture/observability loop + a charter for the canonical-CSV/SOP work that follows.
  - **Scaffolder** — new [`scripts/bless_external_repo.py`](scripts/bless_external_repo.py) idempotently writes the AKOS governance + CI/CD + observability kit into a Holistika-tracked external repo: `.cursor/rules/akos-mirror.mdc` (verbatim) + sha256 stamp, `EXTERNAL_REPO_CONTRACT.md` (rendered), root `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`, `.gitattributes` (when missing), CI/CD baseline (`ci.yml`, `dependabot.yml`, `CODEOWNERS`, `branch-protection.md`), issue templates (`bug.yml`, `feature.yml`, `governance.yml`), `LICENSE`. Stack-aware (Node / Python / Supabase). Opt-in via `--with`: `sentry`, `slack`, `postman`, `sbom`, `semgrep`, `codecov`, `semantic-release`, `browser-smoke`. `--auto-pr` opens a `gh` PR on drift. Refuses to overwrite hand-edited files unless `--force`.
  - **AKOS templates** — 22 new templates under [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Envoy Tech Lab/Repositories/_templates/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Envoy%20Tech%20Lab/Repositories/_templates/): `EXTERNAL_REPO_CONTRACT_TEMPLATE.md` (rendered), `CONTRIBUTING.template.md`, `PR_TEMPLATE.template.md`, `LICENSE.template`, `issue/{bug,feature,governance}.yml.template`, and 14 `ci/*.template` files (CI/CD baseline + Sentry triplet + Slack lib + verdict-flip route + browser-smoke + Postman collection / README / Newman / Husky / lint-staged / commitlint / semantic-release / SAST / SBOM / Codecov / Supabase migration lint / dependabot auto-merge).
  - **Posture + observability** — extended [`scripts/check_external_repo_ci_posture.py`](scripts/check_external_repo_ci_posture.py) with `sentry_liveness`, `slack_webhook_present`, `secret_rotation_freshness` (warn @ 75d / fail @ 90d), `license_present`, plus `--auto-fix` to open a tracking issue when the Sentry env is missing. New [`scripts/check_external_repo_contract.py`](scripts/check_external_repo_contract.py) verifies contract presence + freshness + sha256 alignment via `REPO_HEALTH_SNAPSHOT.csv`. Both are wired into [`scripts/release-gate.py`](scripts/release-gate.py); freshness via `AKOS_EXTERNAL_REPO_FRESHNESS_DAYS`, live mode via `AKOS_EXTERNAL_REPO_CI_LIVE=1`.

[`docs/references/hlk/compliance/REPO_HEALTH_SNAPSHOT.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/REPO_HEALTH_SNAPSHOT.csv) re-rendered.

Sibling to I62.

- **I59 P3-P9 — Governance dimensions seeded + operator surfaces shipped (2026-05-06)** — P3: mass status audit applied the seven-value taxonomy to all 47 master-roadmap frontmatters via `scripts/apply_status_audit.py`; seeded `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv` (47 rows), `OPS_REGISTER.csv` (22 rows), `CYCLE_REGISTER.csv` (3 rows), `DECISION_REGISTER.csv` (48 rows via `scripts/seed_decision_register.py`); validator regex hardened for `D-IH-XX-CLOSURE` and `D-IH-OPS-N` patterns; `validate_hlk.py` OVERALL: PASS. P4: Operator Action Inbox at `docs/wip/planning/OPERATOR_INBOX.md` auto-rendered from `OPS_REGISTER.csv` (22 open operator/mixed rows, RICE-ranked) via new `scripts/render_operator_inbox.py` with `--check-only` determinism gate; release-gate INFO hook; 8 tests. P5: cycle staleness canary `scripts/check_active_initiative_freshness.py` (14-day threshold; 4/5 active initiatives flagged stale); release-gate INFO hook; 6 tests. P6: OPS-58-3 rubric fix — `resolve_persona()` resolves `scenario.persona_id` from `PERSONA_REGISTRY.csv`; `score_response_offline` no longer passes `None` to `_heuristic_persona_fit`; OPS-58-3 row flipped to closed; 14 tests. P7: telemetry promotion routine ran (0 proposals — no telemetry data in this environment); OPS-59-1 row enriched with title + decision link. P8: process_list harmonisation proposal (15 candidate rows across 4 program tranches; `_candidates/i60-process-list-harmonisation.md` + `_candidates/i61-artifact-process-mapping.md` placeholders). P9: USER_GUIDE.md + ARCHITECTURE.md + planning README + SOP.md updates; KM manifests for both new SOPs; CHANGELOG consolidation.

- **I59 P2 — Status taxonomy SSOT wired through dashboard + frontmatter (2026-05-06)** — Wires the seven-value `akos.planning.status_taxonomy.InitiativeStatus` enum (shipped as a module in P1) into the two operator-facing surfaces. **WIP dashboard renderer** ([`scripts/render_wip_dashboard.py`](scripts/render_wip_dashboard.py)) now imports `DASHBOARD_SECTION_ORDER` + `DASHBOARD_SECTION_TITLES` and emits a **section-split** rendering: 8 deterministic sections (Active in execution / Gated on external event / Gated on operator action / Continuous loops / Program lines / Closed / Archived / Unknown). New `_classify_status()` resolves each row's bucket by reading frontmatter `status:` first (taxonomy match → enum bucket; non-taxonomy or missing → Unknown), with the legacy body `**Status:**` line preserved as the display string. New `_render_grouped_table()` replaces `_render_table()` for dashboard output (legacy flat renderer retained for callers that still want it). Empty sections still emit headers + `_(none)_` placeholder so operators see the full taxonomy at a glance — the original "active vs deferred" ambiguity that motivated I59 is now visually impossible. **New frontmatter validator** [`scripts/validate_master_roadmap_frontmatter.py`](scripts/validate_master_roadmap_frontmatter.py) walks every `docs/wip/planning/<NN>-<slug>/master-roadmap.md` and enforces: (a) `status:` field (when present) is one of the seven enum values; (b) required companion fields per `REQUIRED_COMPANION_FIELDS` are present (e.g. `archived` requires `archived_at` + `superseded_by`; `gated_operator` requires `gated_on` + `operator_action`). **Mode discipline**: advisory by default (warnings printed; not failing) for P1–P2; strict (`--strict` flag) becomes the P10 closure gate. Wired into the `validate_hlk.py` dispatcher as the new `MASTER_ROADMAP_FRONTMATTER` entry — confirmed PASS in default mode. **Snapshot of legacy drift** (visible in advisory warnings): 26 master-roadmaps lack frontmatter entirely (I01–I26 era); 17 valid frontmatter but missing `status:` or companion fields; 2 non-taxonomy values needing remap (I55 `active-continuous-loop` → `continuous`; I56 `bootstrapped-pending-first-advisor-reply` → `gated_external`); 2 valid `archived` lacking companions (I05 / I20); 17 `closed` lacking `closed_at` companions. **All 48 warnings close in P3** with a deterministic mass tag operation. Determinism re-verified: `render_wip_dashboard.py --check-only` PASS post-render (sha256 stable: `f7a8756e4cf3c729...`). After P2, both surfaces (dashboard + frontmatter) speak the same enum vocabulary; CSV side already speaks it via `validate_initiative_registry.py` (P1). The frontmatter↔CSV sync gate (also P1) flips from advisory to strict at P10 once P3 has populated `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv`. P2 closure report: [`reports/p2-taxonomy-ssot-2026-05-06.md`](docs/wip/planning/59-hlk-governance-clean-slate/reports/p2-taxonomy-ssot-2026-05-06.md).

- **I59 P1 — HLK governance dimensions landed (2026-05-06)** — Architectural follow-on to the I59 P0 bootstrap. **Five new HLK-governed compliance dimensions ship atomically per D-IH-59-A** (single P1 commit; PRECEDENCE registration + Pydantic schema + validator + sync gate (where applicable) + Supabase migration + mirror-emit helper + `validate_hlk.py` registration in lockstep): `REPOSITORY_REGISTRY.csv` (PK `repo_slug`; promotes the long-standing markdown `REPOSITORIES_REGISTRY.md` to a CSV-backed dimension; **6 rows seeded** from operator-confirmed remotes; FKs to `baseline_organisation.csv` + `TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv`; sync gate `validate_repository_registry_md_csv_sync.py` enforces slug-set agreement); `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv` (PK `initiative_id`; one row per planning initiative; FKs to REPOSITORY + CYCLE + DECISION + nullable `manifests_processes` semicolon-list FK to `process_list.csv` per D-IH-59-G; sync gate `validate_initiative_registry_frontmatter_sync.py` enforces `master-roadmap.md` frontmatter ↔ CSV agreement on status + last_review; **headers only** awaiting P3 bulk seed); `OPS_REGISTER.csv` (PK `ops_action_id`; formalises `OPS-XX-Y` items with RICE + owner_class + status; FKs to originating + forwarded INITIATIVE + DECISION_REGISTER; **headers only** awaiting P3); `CYCLE_REGISTER.csv` (PK `cycle_id`; formalises coordinating cycles I57/I58/I59; covers `coordinating_initiative_id` + `coordinated_initiative_ids` semicolon-list; **headers only**); `DECISION_REGISTER.csv` (PK `decision_id`; every D-IH-XX-Y + closure-form decisions; FKs to linked initiatives + ops items + policies + topics; markdown decision-log files remain canonical for prose per D-IH-59-B; sync gate `validate_decision_register_decision_log_md_sync.py` runs **advisory** during P1–P2 (104 historical D-IH headers in MD have no CSV row yet — bulk seed lands in P3); **headers only**). **Header-only** strategy on 4/5 dimensions per D-IH-59-A circular-FK avoidance (DECISION ↔ INITIATIVE; INITIATIVE ↔ CYCLE; OPS → INITIATIVE → DECISION); P3 seeds all four in lockstep. **Status taxonomy SSOT module** at [`akos/planning/status_taxonomy.py`](akos/planning/status_taxonomy.py) ships the seven-value `InitiativeStatus` `StrEnum` (`closed` / `archived` / `active` / `continuous` / `program_line` / `gated_external` / `gated_operator`) with explicit `REQUIRED_COMPANION_FIELDS` (e.g. `gated_operator` requires at least one back-pointing OPS_REGISTER row); imported by both `validate_initiative_registry.py` and the dashboard renderer (P2). **Five Pydantic schema modules** under `akos/hlk_*_csv.py` expose `<DIMENSION>_FIELDNAMES` header contract tuples + `VALID_*` frozensets (alignment with the existing HLK dimension pattern e.g. `hlk_skill_registry_csv.py`; not per-row Pydantic models). **Five procedural validators** under `scripts/validate_*.py` enforce header contract + PK format/uniqueness + enum values + FK resolution + companion-field rules + ISO date format. **Three sync validators** enforce the two-layer SSOT contract (markdown for prose, CSV for metadata). **Five Supabase migrations** under `supabase/migrations/20260506120{0,1,2,3,4}00_i59_*_mirror.sql` define DDL with `CHECK` constraints matching the `VALID_*` enums, `COMMENT` clauses citing canonical CSV paths, indexes matching expected query patterns (`rice_score DESC NULLS LAST` on ops_register; `decided_at DESC` on decision_register), and ROW LEVEL SECURITY policies (`deny_authenticated`, `deny_anon`, `service_role`) — server-only access posture per the established compliance-mirror convention. **Mirror-emit wiring** in [`scripts/sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py`](scripts/sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py): 5 imports + 5 path constants + 5 `_emit_*_upserts()` helpers (compact pattern matching the I32 P2/P3/P4/P7 dimensions) + 5 argparse `--*-only` flags + 5 entries in mode_flags + new `_i59_mirror_specs` factory block + 5 entries in count-only output and full-bundle conditional emission. Smoke-tested: `--count-only` reports `repository_registry_rows=6`; `--repository-registry-only --no-begin-commit` emits 6 INSERT statements; full bundle is 2.85 MiB and includes the 6 repository_registry inserts. **`validate_hlk.py` dispatcher** registers 8 new entries (5 dimension + 3 sync validators); `OVERALL: PASS` confirmed. **`PRECEDENCE.md`** carries 10 new rows (5 canonical asset rows + 5 mirror rows), each citing canonical CSV path, validator script, sync validator (where applicable), Supabase migration filename, and the relevant I59 decision IDs; the existing `REPOSITORIES_REGISTRY.md` row is updated to flag the two-layer SSOT relationship with `REPOSITORY_REGISTRY.csv`. **Two new PMO SOPs** authored at v3.0 with `status: review` (G-59-D ratifies them in P9): [`SOP-INITIATIVE_GOVERNANCE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/SOP-INITIATIVE_GOVERNANCE_001.md) codifies the 7-status taxonomy + inception → active → cycle membership → closure lifecycle; [`SOP-INITIATIVE_PROCESS_HARMONISATION_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/SOP-INITIATIVE_PROCESS_HARMONISATION_001.md) codifies the two cases (Case A: manifests existing process_list rows; Case B: mints new rows under operator approval per SOP-META; Case C: self-contained no-harmonisation) for linking initiatives to `process_list.csv`. **Markdown SSOT update**: [`REPOSITORIES_REGISTRY.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Envoy%20Tech%20Lab/Repositories/REPOSITORIES_REGISTRY.md) row for the AKOS workspace flips `repo_slug` from `madeira-hlk-runtime` to `openclaw-akos` (the canonical GitHub remote) — eliminates the markdown↔CSV sync drift the new sync validator surfaced on first run; the legacy slug is retained as a parenthetical alias note. **KM manifests deferred** (justified: 5 new dimensions are CSV/text-only governance assets with no visual diagram; existing KM manifest pattern requires raster/svg/mmd; would ship empty visual scaffolds. The 2 new SOP manifests land in P9 per existing pattern for SOP files). **No initiative status flips, no operator approval gates, no process_list mints in P1** — P1 is purely architectural; P3 onward exercises the new schema. P1 closure report: [`reports/p1-hlk-dimensions-2026-05-06.md`](docs/wip/planning/59-hlk-governance-clean-slate/reports/p1-hlk-dimensions-2026-05-06.md). After P1, every CSV change re-runs the 8 new validators automatically via `validate_hlk.py`; mirror-emit ships 22 dimensions cleanly; the planning workspace itself is now a governed HLK dimension stack ready for P2 frontmatter wiring + P3 mass status audit.

- **Initiative 59 — HLK governance promotion + clean slate cycle bootstrap (2026-05-06)** — Coordinating "clean slate" initiative after the I58 closure (engineering AND operator-side closed 2026-05-06). Promotes the planning workspace itself to a governed compliance dimension stack and closes the four open status-ambiguity gaps the operator surfaced after I58: (a) ~50 initiative master-roadmaps with drifted `status:` values; (b) ~16 OPS-XX-Y items scattered across master-roadmap "Forwarded residuals" sections + CHANGELOG entries with no governed schema; (c) 47 D-IH-XX-Y decision references with no queryable index; (d) 4 closeable initiatives (I02 / I07 / I15 / I09) and 2 stale doc-sync rows (F-22a-EMIT-1/2 in I22a; OPS-55-1 P1 in I55) that linger as `Open` in dashboard. **Five new HLK-governed compliance CSV dimensions** ship atomically in P1 per the same-commit pattern: `REPOSITORY_REGISTRY.csv` (promotes existing markdown SSOT to governed CSV; PRIMARY KEY `repo_slug`; FK target for INITIATIVE / COMPONENT_SERVICE_MATRIX / REPO_HEALTH_SNAPSHOT), `INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv` (one row per initiative across all repos; PRIMARY KEY `initiative_id` `INIT-{REPO_SLUG}-{NN}`; FKs to REPOSITORY + CYCLE + DECISION + nullable `manifests_processes` semicolon-list FK to `process_list.csv` per **D-IH-59-G** receiver column), `OPS_REGISTER.csv` (formalize OPS-XX-Y items with RICE / owner_class / status; FKs to originating + forwarded INITIATIVE + GATE_LEDGER + DECISION_REGISTER), `CYCLE_REGISTER.csv` (formalize coordinating cycles I57/I58/I59; covers `coordinating_initiative_id` + `coordinated_initiative_ids` semicolon-list), `DECISION_REGISTER.csv` (every D-IH-XX-Y decision with FKs to linked initiatives + ops items + policies — folded into I59 per **D-IH-59-E** for forward-compat avoidance of ALTER TABLE later). Each new CSV ships its own `validate_*.py` + Pydantic schema + Supabase mirror + RLS policy + KM manifest + PRECEDENCE row in **the same commit** per `.cursor/rules/akos-governance-remediation.mdc`. Three sync validators land too: `validate_repository_registry_md_csv_sync.py` (REPOSITORIES_REGISTRY.md ↔ REPOSITORY_REGISTRY.csv), `validate_initiative_registry_frontmatter_sync.py` (master-roadmap.md frontmatter ↔ INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv), `validate_decision_register_decision_log_md_sync.py` (decision-log.md headers ↔ DECISION_REGISTER.csv; advisory). **Two new SOPs** authored at v3.0: `SOP-INITIATIVE_GOVERNANCE_001.md` (initiative lifecycle reference; 7-value taxonomy explained; FK dictionary) + `SOP-INITIATIVE_PROCESS_HARMONISATION_001.md` (process_list integration recipe; tranche rules; mint authority chain; G-60 gate definitions); both at `status: review` until **G-59-D** at P9. **Status taxonomy SSOT** ships in P2 (`akos/planning/status_taxonomy.py` enum module shared by master-roadmap.md frontmatter validator AND INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv schema): seven values (`closed` / `archived` / `active` / `continuous` / `program_line` / `gated_external` / `gated_operator`) per **D-IH-59-D** with companion-field rules (`closed_at` / `archived_at` + `superseded_by` / none / `continuous_rationale` / `cadence` / `gated_on` / `gated_on` + `operator_action`); `render_wip_dashboard.py` extended with section split (Active / Gated-external / Gated-operator / Continuous / Program-line / Closed / Archived). **P3 mass status audit** applies the new taxonomy to ~50 initiative master-roadmaps + seeds INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv (one row per initiative) + seeds OPS_REGISTER.csv (~16 rows) + seeds CYCLE_REGISTER.csv (4 rows: CYC-57/58/59 + CYC-XX retroactive) + seeds DECISION_REGISTER.csv (~47 rows from active + recent-closed initiatives) + closes **I02 / I07 / I15** with closure UATs + archives **I09** (superseded by I10) + doc-sync flips for **F-22a-EMIT-1/2** (I22a; already implemented in P5/P7 commits) + **OPS-55-1 P1** (I55; already implemented in I58 D.x). **P4 Operator Action Inbox SSOT** — new `docs/wip/planning/OPERATOR_INBOX.md` auto-rendered from `OPS_REGISTER.csv` (status=open AND owner_class IN (operator, mixed) ORDER BY rice_score DESC) via new `scripts/render_operator_inbox.py` with determinism gate (`--check-only`); release-gate informational hook. **P5 cycle staleness canary** — new `scripts/check_active_initiative_freshness.py` reads INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv where `status='active' AND now() - last_review > 14 days`; soft warning footer in release-gate. **P6 OPS-58-3 rubric calibration fix** (engineering-side; RICE 149) — `_heuristic_persona_fit` resolves persona context from `scenario.persona_id` → PERSONA_REGISTRY.csv → full persona dict passed to `JudgeRoster.score()`; re-run A.1+A.2 burns from I58 expected to lift cross-persona alignment from 0% / 5.7% to ≥80%; **OPS-58-3 row in OPS_REGISTER.csv flipped to closed**. **P7 telemetry promotion routine in operator's stead** per **D-IH-59-J** — `scripts/promote_telemetry_to_scenario.py --since-days 30` runs proposal-only (auto-merge forbidden per `artifacts/telemetry-proposals/README.md` design); triage report ranks top 3 clusters; **OPS-59-1** row minted in OPS_REGISTER.csv pointing operator at the merge step. **P8 process_list harmonisation proposal** per **D-IH-59-F** — author `reports/p8-process-list-harmonisation-proposal-2026-05-06.md` mapping every initiative to candidate `thi_*_dtp_*` parent rows; recommend ~17 candidate `process_list.csv` rows across 6 program tranches; draft `SOP-INITIATIVE_PROCESS_HARMONISATION_001.md` at `status: review` with full minting recipe; identify program tranches needing first-batch operator approval; surface as **I60 candidate** scope (operator-approval-gated; G-60-A through G-60-F per-tranche). **NO process_list mints in I59** — `.cursor/rules/akos-governance-remediation.mdc` is unambiguous (operator approval per tranche; `validate_hlk.py` PASS; `docs/USER_GUIDE.md` HLK Operator Model sync if role/process counts change). **P9 documentation pass** — USER_GUIDE.md taxonomy + governance + decision-register + process harmonisation subsections; ARCHITECTURE.md state-model subsection; planning README top blurb update; CHANGELOG.md I59 closure entry; SOP.md pointer to OPERATOR_INBOX.md; KM manifests for both new SOPs; **G-59-D** ratifies both new SOPs flipping from `status: review` to `status: active`. **P10 closure UAT** — 22-check verification matrix (release-gate 8/8 + 5 new validators + 3 sync gates + `compliance_mirror_emit` on 5 new mirrors + dashboard / inbox determinism + freshness canary + KM manifests for both new SOPs); flip I59 master-roadmap to `closed` AND its INITIATIVE_REGISTRY.csv row + CYC-59 row + planning README row 59. **P0 ships** six governance artefacts under [`docs/wip/planning/59-hlk-governance-clean-slate/`](docs/wip/planning/59-hlk-governance-clean-slate/): master-roadmap with full P0–P10 phase plan + 11-phase dependency mermaid + verification matrix + 4 operator approval gates G-59-A/B/C/D; decision-log seeded D-IH-59-A..N (14 decisions); asset-classification per [`PRECEDENCE.md`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/PRECEDENCE.md) covering 5 new canonical CSVs + 5 mirror tables + 2 new canonical SOPs + ~50 modified canonical (initiative master-roadmaps re-statused) + ~3 new canonical (closure UATs for I02/I07/I15) + 1 modified canonical (I09 archive flip); evidence-matrix E1–E17; risk-register R-59-1..13 + 3 cycle-3-specific (R-59-cycle3-A first atomic 5-dimension ship; R-59-cycle3-B first "in stead" telemetry routine; R-59-cycle3-C first mass status flip with new taxonomy enum) + 7 out-of-scope; reports/p0-bootstrap-2026-05-06.md. Planning README row 59 added between I58 and `99-proposals` with **I60 / I61 candidate placeholders**. **Closure model** — engineering closes when P0–P10 all land regardless of operator funding; operator-content forks into **I60 candidate** (process_list mints with G-60-N tranche gates) and stays in OPERATOR_INBOX (OPS-58-2 OpenAI rotation; OPS-58-4 GraphRAG live wiring; OPS-14-1; OPS-55-1; OPS-56-1; OPS-59-1 telemetry merge). After I59 closes, future cycles add new initiatives by picking one of seven taxonomy values; status drift is a CI failure (sync validators); operator's 14-day attention budget is auditable (freshness canary). [`reports/p0-bootstrap-2026-05-06.md`](docs/wip/planning/59-hlk-governance-clean-slate/reports/p0-bootstrap-2026-05-06.md).

- **OPS-58-1 — Initiative 58 Phase A live cycle FIRED, FULLY CLOSED (2026-05-06)** — Post-E.0 operator-funded execution of Phase A under operator's "go all out / dev environment" authorization. **G-58-1 GREEN at 11/11 prerequisites** after operator updated [`~/.openclaw/.env`](C:\Users\Shadow\.openclaw\.env) with real Anthropic / Supabase service-role / Neo4j Aura / Kalavai shadow URL credentials and the agent set Phase A flags in shell (per `D-IH-58-F` "export not persist": `AKOS_RECORD_LIVE`, `AKOS_GRAPHRAG_POC_LIVE`, `AKOS_JUDGE_LIVE_API`, `AKOS_JUDGE_ROSTER`, `MAX_DOSSIER_USD=50`, `MAX_TIER_B_USD=10`). **Pre-A.1 gap-fill landed in-cycle as `D-IH-58-I`** — the live multi-judge API path that I52 P3 had deliberately left as a `NotImplementedError` placeholder is now wired: `akos.eval_harness.judge._call_member_via_api` performs real provider dispatch (Anthropic Messages API + OpenAI Chat Completions API), reads [`prompts/judge/JUDGE_PROMPT_V1.md`](prompts/judge/JUDGE_PROMPT_V1.md) as the system prompt (front-matter stripped), parses the strict JSON output schema (axis ∈ [1,5]; tolerates ` ```json ` fences though prompt forbids them), tracks per-call cost via the new `_JUDGE_PRICING_USD_PER_MTOK` table (current `claude-*-4-5` family + legacy 3.5 + `gpt-4o` / `gpt-4.1` rows; unknown models raise so an operator never silently overspends a roster expansion), tracks latency, and on parse / transport failure raises so `_default_member_scorer`'s exception fallback attributes `fallback-offline-api-error` to the result. Test seam: module-level `_anthropic_client_factory` / `_openai_client_factory` callables that tests monkey-patch with stub clients (no real network in CI). New [`tests/test_judge_live_api.py`](tests/test_judge_live_api.py) ships **17 tests** covering provider dispatch (anthropic + openai + unsupported), payload shape (mandatory + optional persona context), front-matter stripping, strict + fence-tolerant JSON parsing, schema-violation rejection, cost computation (known + unknown models), and the new `JudgeRoster.score()` notes channel that surfaces distinct fallback reasons (`no-api-key` / `no-live-api-flag` / `api-error`) so the calibration burn banner can discriminate them. Companion fix in [`scripts/judge_calibration_burn.py`](scripts/judge_calibration_burn.py): banner now reads "Reasons observed: …" instead of the generic "no API credentials present or AKOS_JUDGE_LIVE_API unset" (which mis-attributed `NotImplementedError` runs); JSON sidecar adds `live_total_cost_usd` so the operator can verify per-burn spend without parsing per-scenario logs; entry-point auto-bootstraps `~/.openclaw/.env`. New `anthropic>=0.40.0` + `openai>=1.50.0` rows in [`requirements.txt`](requirements.txt). One pre-existing test ([`test_eval_judge_multi.py::test_live_api_call_raises_when_wired_path_attempted`](tests/test_eval_judge_multi.py)) updated to inject a stub `_RaisingClient` instead of relying on the old `NotImplementedError` body — preserves all 32 multi-judge tests. Targeted regression: **154 / 154 passed** across `test_judge_live_api.py` + `test_eval_judge.py` + `test_eval_judge_multi.py` + `test_i52_p7_tier_b_multi_judge_endpoint.py` + `test_dossier_judge_axes_endpoint.py` + `test_preflight_g58_1.py` + `test_runpod_provider.py`. **Roster pivot (D-IH-58-I) under operator's "go all out" directive** — model probe revealed Anthropic `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022` returns 404 (deprecated snapshot) and the existing OpenAI key returns 401 (rotated / placeholder); pivoted to `anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-5,anthropic:claude-haiku-4-5` (flagship + cheap-tier; same family) which is strictly stronger than the original cross-family `sonnet+gpt-4o` pair for `D-IH-52-B` cost-aware tiered escalation evidence. **A.1 OPERATOR n=50** burn fired live in 339 seconds (real Anthropic; 100 calls; `fallback_only: false` / `fallback_reasons: []`); per-axis alignment vs offline rubric: brand_voice 100 % / citation 96 % / persona_fit 0 % / overall 65.3 %. **A.2 PERSONA-INVESTOR-COLD n=35** burn in 241 seconds (70 calls); 100 % / 100 % / 5.7 % / 68.6 %. The uniform 5-vs-3 misalignment pattern across both personas (judges score 5, offline rubric scores 3) is consistent with the offline `_heuristic_persona_fit` defaulting to the conservative "3 = neutral but plausible" branch when `persona=None` is passed by the burn harness — i.e. a **rubric calibration gap, not a judge disagreement** — exactly the cross-persona evidence I52 P3 was designed to surface. **A.3 graphrag_poc** stays scaffold-only NO-GO per `D-IH-58-C` (live wiring needs `pip install neo4j-graphrag-python` + Neo4j vault re-index + a working embedding provider; out of single-sitting scope; `D-IH-46-E` non-additive bar enforced — NO-SHIP is a closure event, not a failure). **A.4 dossier `--filter madeira --mode live`** in 9.5 seconds: 12 sections, MADEIRA three-light all GREEN, `madeira_ship_go=true`, `dossier.md sha256: 363de24651a6911b0124f5f27275bb5c4523aeba3c7ea92fc2b20f093215e8ef`, `git_sha: d519f9d93fa4604dc1c49c8fe3d5af30310d7c99`. **A.5 auto-skipped** per A.3 NO-GO (conditional gate; expected). **Cost roll-up**: ≤ $0.50 total for 170 live API calls (well under the $50 hard ceiling / $40 abort threshold). RunPod resources untouched (zero pods / endpoints — judges run on Anthropic; SUT for the dossier reuses already-up Kalavai `VLLM_SHADOW_URL`). **Three cleanly-scoped residuals forwarded** with their own decision frames: **OPS-58-2** (operator; rotate OpenAI key — `D-IH-58-K`; RICE 144), **OPS-58-3** (engineering; persona-aware offline `_heuristic_persona_fit` to close the `persona=None` rubric gap — `D-IH-58-J`; RICE 149), **OPS-58-4** (engineering + operator; GraphRAG live wiring per `D-IH-46-E` non-additive bar; RICE 15). None block I58 closure or production posture. **I58 master-roadmap flips from "engineering closed only" to "engineering AND operator-side closed"**; Phase A "OPS-58-1 forwarded" entries replaced with "Closed 2026-05-06" rows; new closure report at [`reports/ops-58-1-2026-05-06.md`](docs/wip/planning/58-cycle-2-multi-track-forward/reports/ops-58-1-2026-05-06.md); E.0 closure UAT carries a post-closure addendum extending the verdict without invalidating the 2026-05-05 sign-off. After this cycle, Phase A live windows continue under the same envelope discipline; the rubric fix (OPS-58-3) is the highest-RICE engineering follow-up. [I58](docs/wip/planning/58-cycle-2-multi-track-forward/master-roadmap.md), [decision-log](docs/wip/planning/58-cycle-2-multi-track-forward/decision-log.md) (D-IH-58-I/J/K), [OPS-58-1 closure report](docs/wip/planning/58-cycle-2-multi-track-forward/reports/ops-58-1-2026-05-06.md).
