- **Initiative 31 — Holistik Ops Discovery: 5-axis operating system for human interactions (2026-04-30)** — Closes the operator-flagged "we're already operating multilingually across multiple channels and stakeholder types but the system doesn't reflect it" gap. Discovers and codifies the **5-axis Holistik Ops operating system** (Persona × Channel × **Distance N1/N2/N3/N4** × Language × Artifact-class), each axis a governed registry. New initiative folder [`docs/wip/planning/31-holistik-ops-discovery/`](docs/wip/planning/31-holistik-ops-discovery/) with master-roadmap + decision-log (D-IH-31-A "Canonical default = audience of the artifact" / D-IH-31-B "Persona archetype vs GOI/POI named individual" / D-IH-31-C "Channel touchpoint registry distinct from CHANNEL_STRATEGY" / D-IH-31-D "Brand voice rules per locale; FR stub" / D-IH-31-E "MADEIRA + GOVERNANCE_MOAT stay Spanish per audience-canonical exception" / D-IH-31-F "Outbound brief = Markdown with required sections" / **D-IH-31-G "Distance is N1-N4 ordinal + bridge_via FK + assessed_date"** / **D-IH-31-H "Distance variants in touchpoint kit live as in-file sections, not separate files"**) + asset-classification + evidence-matrix + risk-register + discovery-taxonomy. **P1 — Localisation foundation**: new canonical [`SOP-HLK_LOCALISATION_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Tech/System%20Owner/SOP-HLK_LOCALISATION_001.md) (3-axis policy: canonical default = audience of the artifact; audience-canonical exception for strategy artifacts whose deck-bound block feeds a single-locale external surface; locale-derived siblings for multi-locale external deliverables) + new [`scripts/validate_hlk_language_frontmatter.py`](scripts/validate_hlk_language_frontmatter.py) (every canonical MD declares `language: en|es|fr`; `derived_from:` and `derived_locales:` graph integrity) + **migration of ~120 canonical Markdown files to declare `language: en` frontmatter** (the 2 I30 audience-canonical Spanish artifacts MADEIRA_PLATFORM + GOVERNANCE_MOAT preserved as `language: es` per D-IH-31-E) + new [`BRAND_FRENCH_PATTERNS.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_FRENCH_PATTERNS.md) (`status: stub` until first FR external deliverable lands). **P2 — People axes**: new canonical [`PERSONA_REGISTRY.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/PERSONA_REGISTRY.csv) (16 archetype rows: investor cold/warm, advisor referral/cold, talent inbound, vendor outbound/inbound, press, customer KiRBe/service prospect, partner joint-equity/subcontract, idea proposer, random inbound, existing customer/partner; each with direction + intent + value_band + typical_languages + typical_channels + **typical_distance_band** + qualification_gate + intro_artifact_path + handoff_role) + akos field contract + validator + mirror DDL (`compliance.persona_registry_mirror`) + sync flag (`--persona-registry-only`) + topic registry row. **GOI/POI distance extension** (the highest-risk part of the regression): [`GOI_POI_REGISTER.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/GOI_POI_REGISTER.csv) gains 3 columns (`distance_band` enum N1-N4 + `bridge_via` FK + `distance_assessed_date` ISO date); 6 existing rows backfilled to N1; [`akos/hlk_goipoi_csv.py`](akos/hlk_goipoi_csv.py) extended; [`scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py`](scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py) gains 4 invariants (enum, FK self-resolution, non-null when N!=N1, no self-bridge, no cycles in bridge graph); [`SOP-HLK_GOIPOI_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/SOP-HLK_GOIPOI_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md) gains §4.9 distance-assessment subsection (assessment decision tree + quarterly re-assessment cadence aligned with §6.1 service_role rotation); [`scripts/render_pmo_hub.py`](scripts/render_pmo_hub.py) extended to render `distance` + `bridge_via` columns in the auto-generated stakeholder index. **P3 — Channel touchpoint registry**: new canonical [`CHANNEL_TOUCHPOINT_REGISTRY.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/CHANNEL_TOUCHPOINT_REGISTRY.csv) (10 touchpoint rows: LinkedIn DM/post-response, email inbound, web form, Cal scheduling, ad campaign, search organic, direct DM, partner referral, event meeting; each with direction + supported_languages + typical_personas + **typical_distance_band_inbound** + triage_rule + response_sla_band + response_owner_role) + akos contract + validator + mirror DDL + sync flag + topic registry row. **P4 — Touchpoint kit**: new folder [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/touchpoint-kit/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/touchpoint-kit/) keyed by `<persona_id>/<channel_id>/<artifact_class>_<lang>.md`; **15 templates shipped** for 8 highest-leverage cells × locales (PERSONA-INVESTOR-COLD × CHAN-LINKEDIN-DM in EN+ES; PERSONA-INVESTOR-WARM × CHAN-EMAIL-INBOUND in EN+ES; PERSONA-ADVISOR-REFERRAL × CHAN-EMAIL-INBOUND in ES; PERSONA-PARTNER-JOINT-EQUITY × CHAN-EMAIL-INBOUND in ES+EN; PERSONA-TALENT-INBOUND × CHAN-WEB-FORM in EN+ES; PERSONA-VENDOR-OUTBOUND × CHAN-DIRECT-DM in EN+ES; PERSONA-CUSTOMER-KIRBE-PROSPECT × CHAN-WEB-FORM in EN+ES; PERSONA-IDEA-PROPOSER × CHAN-DIRECT-DM in EN+ES). **Each template carries 2-3 in-file distance variants** (`## Variant — N1`, `## Variant — N2`, `## Variant — N3 / N4`) per D-IH-31-H — keeps file count proportional (15 files vs ~96 if split per N-band). **P5 — Outbound brief + sourcing register**: new [`TEMPLATE_OUTBOUND_BRIEF_en.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/sourcing-briefs/TEMPLATE_OUTBOUND_BRIEF_en.md) (canonical) + ES + FR variants (the FR variant is the **first real exercise of the locale-derivation pipeline** from P1 — proves the SOP). New canonical [`SOURCING_REGISTER.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/SOURCING_REGISTER.csv) (with `distance_band_at_first_contact` + `current_distance_band` fields capturing relationship migration from N4 cold to N1 trusted) + akos contract + validator + mirror DDL + sync flag + topic registry row. **P6 — Holistik Ops Discovery doc**: new canonical meta-methodology document [`HOLISTIK_OPS_DISCOVERY.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/HOLISTIK_OPS_DISCOVERY.md) (5-axis operating system explained; cross-axis routing flow diagram; decomposability property; descale-without-impact corollary; **reach-map property** that turns founder social capital into a queryable governed asset; future hooks for MADEIRA-as-SaaS productization, role #2 hire, FR canonical promotion, Neo4j graph projection trigger) + topic registry row (`topic_holistik_ops_discovery`). **P7 — Tests + closure**: 5 new test suites — [`tests/test_persona_registry.py`](tests/test_persona_registry.py) (9 tests), [`tests/test_channel_touchpoint_registry.py`](tests/test_channel_touchpoint_registry.py) (8 tests), [`tests/test_sourcing_register.py`](tests/test_sourcing_register.py) (5 tests), [`tests/test_localisation_frontmatter.py`](tests/test_localisation_frontmatter.py) (7 tests including audience-canonical exception assertions for MADEIRA + GOVERNANCE_MOAT), [`tests/test_goipoi_distance_extension.py`](tests/test_goipoi_distance_extension.py) (10 tests covering all distance invariants + render_pmo_hub distance column). [`tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py`](tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py) extended (new row counts: persona_registry_rows=16 + channel_touchpoint_registry_rows=10 + sourcing_register_rows=1; topic_registry_rows grows from 19 to 23). [`tests/test_company_deck.py`](tests/test_company_deck.py) governance-metric assertion widened from `\b1\d temas\b` to `\b\d{1,3} temas\b` to absorb growth. New mirror migration at [`supabase/migrations/20260430210000_i31_goipoi_distance_extension_and_persona_registry.sql`](supabase/migrations/20260430210000_i31_goipoi_distance_extension_and_persona_registry.sql) (ALTER TABLE for GOI/POI distance + 3 new mirror tables for persona/channel/sourcing); operator applies via `npx supabase db push`. Mirror reseed SQL staged at `artifacts/sql/i31_persona_channel_sourcing_topic_upsert.sql`. Verification: `validate_hlk` PASS at **23 topics / 16 personas / 10 channels / 6 GOI-POI rows with distance / 1 sourcing vendor / 150 MD files with language frontmatter / 12 programs / 1.093 processes / 65 roles**; `validate_hlk_vault_links` PASS; `validate_hlk_km_manifests` PASS (11/11); `pytest tests/` 757 passed, 5 skipped, 6 warnings (the 2 pre-existing `validate_configs.py::TestOpenclawConfig` AKOS sandbox-config failures stay out of scope). The GOVERNANCE_MOAT.md drift detector caught the topic count growth from 17 → 23 mid-migration and was updated automatically (slide 11 + deck story mirror updated). Closure trail at [`reports/uat-i31-holistik-ops-discovery-2026-04-30.md`](docs/wip/planning/31-holistik-ops-discovery/reports/uat-i31-holistik-ops-discovery-2026-04-30.md) with full verification matrix + new operator follow-up queue.

- **Initiative 30 — Deck moat surgery: MADEIRA, joint-equity, governance metrics (2026-04-30)** — Closes the operator-flagged "deck still feels services-biased" gap by projecting three missing halves of the knowledge base onto the company dossier deck. New initiative folder [`docs/wip/planning/30-deck-moat-surgery/`](docs/wip/planning/30-deck-moat-surgery/) with master-roadmap + decision-log (D-IH-30-A "Name MADEIRA externally" / D-IH-30-B "Joint-equity equity-band stays operator-decided" / D-IH-30-C "Demote card-web + card-erp into one card-internal" / D-IH-30-D "Replace moat pillar 1 with quantified governance" / D-IH-30-E "Figma backport DEFERRED" / D-IH-30-F "Drift detector enforced via test") + asset-classification + evidence-matrix + risk-register + reports. **P1 — MADEIRA Platform**: new canonical [`MADEIRA_PLATFORM.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/MADEIRA_PLATFORM.md) (`intellectual_kind: product_thesis`, `deck_bound: true`, slides_consumed: 04, 05, 06, 11, 12; sections: what MADEIRA is — Copilot-class agentic framework with governed sourcing + standardized skills + standardized behaviour; why now; internal evidence pointing at SOP-MADEIRA-* SOPs + KIR onboarding + Think Big section + platform repo; commercial path today/bridge/tomorrow; differentiation matrix vs generic Copilot/custom solutions; ENISA fit; 1 `TODO[OPERATOR-madeira-saas-window]` for productization commit window). New `topic_madeira_platform` row in [`TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv). **P2 — Joint-equity channel**: [`CHANNEL_STRATEGY.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/CHANNEL_STRATEGY.md) extended with Channel 6 "Partner joint-equity SaaS pilot" — hypothesis (partners with customer base ask Holistika to build full SaaS for equity + revenue share), ICP × channel match, leading indicator (1 inbound conversation per quarter, 2 currently in pipeline), CAC band (effectively €0, high opportunity cost), 1 `TODO[OPERATOR-channel6-equity-band]` for the equity / revenue-share band, decision criteria (3-of-3 gate), boundary with corporate structure (Holistika retains IP; partner becomes shareholder of the new SaaS entity, not co-owner of Holistika — preserves "Empresa Emergente" eligibility). Slide 09 ICP 2 renamed "Partner B2B + equidad conjunta" with the joint-equity offer surfaced. **P3 — Governance moat**: new canonical [`GOVERNANCE_MOAT.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/GOVERNANCE_MOAT.md) (`intellectual_kind: moat_thesis`, `deck_bound: true`, slides_consumed: 11). Quantifies the moat with 4 hero metrics — **19 governed topics, 1.093 governed processes, 65 governed roles, 11 KM Output 1 manifests** — plus three layers of validators (artifact / FK / mirror-drift) + four quality gates (jargon, manifest, FK, citation) + deterministic build pipeline. The differentiator: descale-without-impact (a program teardown is a 4-step auditable operation; competitors can't replicate that). New `topic_governance_moat` row in `TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv` (registry now at 19 rows). **P4 — Deck restructure** (7 slides edited in [`deck_slides.yaml`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_company_dossier/deck_slides.yaml) and mirrored in [`deck_story_es.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_company_dossier/deck_story_es.md)): slide 04 third line renamed "Software propio (KiRBe + MADEIRA)"; slide 05 method third column surfaces MADEIRA as agentic layer; **slide 06 capability grid restructured** — `card-kirbe` + new `card-madeira` as hero pair, `card-shopify` + `card-rushly` as demand-signal cards (B2B + joint-equity), former `card-web` + `card-erp` folded into a single `card-internal` "Validación interna" card; `stat_grid` swapped to "2 productos propios / 2 partners en demanda activa / 1.093 procesos / 2023" so the proof grid now reads as 2 products + 2 demand signals + 1 internal-validation, not 5 services; slide 09 ICP 2 surfaces joint-equity; slide 10 tomorrow-tier names KiRBe + MADEIRA as the dual product axis; **slide 11 moat pillar 1** replaced with "Operación gobernada y medible" quoting the 4 GOVERNANCE_MOAT metrics; slide 12 12-24m roadmap re-anchored on KiRBe + MADEIRA's first external customer milestone. **P5 — Rebuild + verify**: HTML deck rebuilt clean (14 slides, sha256 `5b1d4030…`); PDF re-exported (14 pages, 69.5 KB, sha256 `3f4d99f3…`); `sync_deck_from_strategy.py --check-only` reports 12 strategy files / 10 deck-bound (was 8) / 9 TODO[OPERATOR-*] tokens in deck-bound blocks (the 2 new I30 TODO markers live in artifact bodies, not in deck-bound blocks — operator follow-ups, not deck blockers); mirror reseed SQL staged at `artifacts/sql/i30_topic_registry_business_strategy_upsert.sql` (27.5 KB); validate_hlk PASS at 19 topics. **P6 — Tests + closure**: 4 new tests in [`tests/test_business_strategy.py`](tests/test_business_strategy.py) (MADEIRA + GOVERNANCE_MOAT artifacts present, topic FK resolution, joint-equity channel + TODO marker present, expected_children grown to 13); 6 new tests in [`tests/test_company_deck.py`](tests/test_company_deck.py) (MADEIRA names on YAML + story, card-madeira list-item present, slide 11 quotes governance metrics, slide 09 surfaces joint-equity, slide 10 tomorrow names MADEIRA, slide 12 12-24m anchors on MADEIRA, HTML deck contains MADEIRA after rebuild); new test suite [`tests/test_governance_moat_metrics.py`](tests/test_governance_moat_metrics.py) (5 tests — drift detector enforcing parity between GOVERNANCE_MOAT.md hero metrics and live `validate_hlk` / `sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv --count-only` / `validate_hlk_km_manifests.py` output, with tolerance bands per D-IH-30-F: +0/-0 topics + roles, ±5% processes, ±1 KM manifests). Updated [`tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py`](tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py) `topic_registry_rows=17` → `topic_registry_rows=19`. Verification: `validate_hlk` PASS (12 programs / 1093 processes / 65 roles / 19 topics); `validate_hlk_vault_links` PASS; `validate_hlk_km_manifests` PASS (11/11); `pytest tests/` 717 passed, 5 skipped, 6 warnings (the 2 pre-existing `validate_configs.py::TestOpenclawConfig` AKOS sandbox-config failures stay out of scope). Drift-handling rule per D-IH-30-E: Figma file at `https://www.figma.com/design/yiPav7BLxUulNFrrsoKJqW` left intentionally on its I29 P1 frame copy; YAML/Markdown SSOT carries I30 edits; Figma backport queued for the next operator-scheduled session. Closure trail at [`reports/uat-i30-deck-moat-surgery-2026-04-30.md`](docs/wip/planning/30-deck-moat-surgery/reports/uat-i30-deck-moat-surgery-2026-04-30.md) with verification matrix + the 11 founder TODO[OPERATOR-*] markers itemised (9 inherited from I29 P4 + 2 new from I30: `madeira-saas-window` + `channel6-equity-band`).

- **Initiative 29 — Multi-phase consolidation: Figma fixes, design governance, Impeccable Style, Business Strategy SSOT, deck wiring (2026-04-30)** — Closes the operator-flagged "deck visually broken in Figma + dossier still says nothing of substance" gap by shipping six layered improvements in one initiative. **P1 — Figma fix pass**: 14 frames at `https://www.figma.com/design/yiPav7BLxUulNFrrsoKJqW` rebuilt programmatically via `use_figma` Plugin API to fix systematic auto-layout breakage (frames collapsing to 100×100 default, text clipping in tag pills, slide-13 use-of-funds rendering as 1×4 instead of 2×2). Fix pattern: explicit `layoutSizingHorizontal: 'FILL'` for horizontal expansion, `layoutSizingVertical: 'HUG'` for content-fitting, `textAutoResize: 'WIDTH_AND_HEIGHT'` on tag-pill text nodes, `layoutWrap: 'WRAP'` on multi-row grids; recorded in [`figma-link.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_company_dossier/figma-link.md) §"Visual fix pass" with the 5 reusable layout primitives now established (cover hero, capability card, stat block, ask signature, roadmap window). Componentization deferred to a future initiative with persistent trigger contract documented in the same file. **P2 — Governance scaffolds**: [`SOP-HLK_TOOLING_STANDARDS_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Tech/System%20Owner/SOP-HLK_TOOLING_STANDARDS_001.md) extended with §3.6 (Figma governance — every governed Figma file has a row in [`FIGMA_FILES_REGISTRY.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Envoy%20Tech%20Lab/Repositories/FIGMA_FILES_REGISTRY.md) with file_slug / figma_url / team_key / class / topic_ids / linked_yaml_ssot; YAML/MD wins for content, Figma wins for visual layout, any Figma copy edit must be backported to YAML before close) and §3.7 (External design skill bundles — installed under `.cursor/skills/<bundle>/` from the canonical GitHub source; AKOS rule precedence non-negotiable; thin bridge files at the repo root redirect to brand SSOT instead of duplicating). New canonical [`FIGMA_FILES_REGISTRY.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Envoy%20Tech%20Lab/Repositories/FIGMA_FILES_REGISTRY.md) seeds the company-dossier file plus a reserved slot for the future `holistika-design-system` Figma library. KM Output 1 manifests gain `paths.figma_url` + `paths.deck_yaml` slots ([`HLK_KM_TOPIC_FACT_SOURCE.md`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/HLK_KM_TOPIC_FACT_SOURCE.md)). [`akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) gains 3 sync triggers (Figma registry, SOP §3.6/§3.7, KM manifest schema). **P3 — Impeccable Style integration**: external design skill bundle installed under `.cursor/skills/impeccable/` (source: GitHub `cursor-public/impeccable`); thin bridge files at the repo root — [`PRODUCT.md`](PRODUCT.md) and [`DESIGN.md`](DESIGN.md) — redirect Impeccable to the canonical brand SSOT (`BRAND_VOICE_FOUNDATION.md`, `BRAND_VISUAL_PATTERNS.md`, `BRAND_DO_DONT.md`, `BRAND_SPANISH_PATTERNS.md`, `BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT.md`, `deck-visual-system.md`) instead of duplicating content; both bridges carry the AKOS-precedence rule (Impeccable yields when its suggestion conflicts with `.cursor/rules/akos-*.mdc`). First simulated critique + audit report shipped at [`reports/impeccable-critique-2026-04-30.md`](docs/wip/planning/29-multi-phase-consolidation/reports/impeccable-critique-2026-04-30.md) with heuristic scores, cognitive-load assessment, and priority issue queue. **P4 — Business Strategy SSOT (the "says nothing" fix)**: 9 canonical strategy artifacts under [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/) — [`README.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/README.md), [`STRATEGY_DECISION_LOG.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/STRATEGY_DECISION_LOG.md), [`MARKET_THESIS.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/MARKET_THESIS.md), [`COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md), [`PRICING_MODEL.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/PRICING_MODEL.md), [`CHANNEL_STRATEGY.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/CHANNEL_STRATEGY.md), [`SALES_MOTION.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/SALES_MOTION.md), [`UNIT_ECONOMICS.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/UNIT_ECONOMICS.md), [`BOOTSTRAPPING_PLAN.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/BOOTSTRAPPING_PLAN.md), [`INVESTMENT_THESIS.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/business-strategy/INVESTMENT_THESIS.md). Each artifact follows the same shape: brand-voice frontmatter, `## What this answers` (the question the founder needs to answer for an external reader), one or more research-grounded body sections with concrete recommendations (per-channel CAC bands, 2026 LTV:CAC SaaS benchmarks, three-scenario runway model, EU SaaS comparable rounds), an explicit `TODO[OPERATOR-x]` marker for every founder decision still pending, and a `## Deck-bound facts` block (when `deck_bound: true`) carrying the values the deck quotes. New canonical [`POC_TO_COMMERCIAL_MAP.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/POC_TO_COMMERCIAL_MAP.csv) — 5-row register mapping each shipped Holistika delivery (Holistika web, HLK ERP, KiRBe, Use-case-1 Shopify, Use-case-2 Rushly) to delivery_name / partner_or_buyer_ref / domain / engagement_type / revenue_band / recurring / case_study_status; replaces the deck's qualitative "5 capability cards" with a queryable register the deck can quote. [`TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv) gains 12 new rows (parent `topic_business_strategy` + 9 child topics + `topic_poc_commercial_map` + `topic_brand_visual_identity`); registry now at 17 topics; UPSERT SQL staged at [`artifacts/sql/i29_topic_registry_business_strategy_upsert.sql`](artifacts/sql/i29_topic_registry_business_strategy_upsert.sql) (operator applies via `npx supabase db push` or MCP `execute_sql`). **P5 — Deck-from-strategy wiring**: new [`scripts/sync_deck_from_strategy.py`](scripts/sync_deck_from_strategy.py) reads each strategy file's frontmatter, extracts the `## Deck-bound facts` block, and validates the wiring contract (`--check-only` default — informational; `--apply` — refuses with `rc=2` when any `TODO[OPERATOR-*]` token still gates a deck-bound block). The script ships the wiring layer first; once the founder narrows TODO bands to single values, `--apply` will merge the values into the deck. HTML deck rebuilt clean (14 slides, sha256 `a7b7dc325c27c97e…`); 14-page PDF re-exported (66.7 KB, sha256 `9ad30d03552de19a…`). **P6 — Tests + closure**: 3 new test suites — [`tests/test_business_strategy.py`](tests/test_business_strategy.py) (15 tests covering folder shape, all expected artifacts present, frontmatter contract, topic-registry FK resolution, deck-bound facts block presence, POC CSV schema + topic FK, sync-script smoke), [`tests/test_impeccable_bridge.py`](tests/test_impeccable_bridge.py) (8 tests covering bridge file presence, canonical brand-file references, AKOS-precedence rule presence, no-hex-color guard, skill-bundle install), [`tests/test_figma_files_registry.py`](tests/test_figma_files_registry.py) (6 tests covering registry presence, schema columns, figma_url format, class enum, topic-FK resolution, linked_yaml_ssot path resolution); [`tests/test_company_deck.py`](tests/test_company_deck.py) extended with 3 new I29 P5 tests for the deck<-strategy wiring. Verification: `validate_hlk` PASS (12 programs / 1093 processes / 65 roles / 17 topics); `validate_hlk_vault_links` PASS; `validate_hlk_km_manifests` PASS (11/11); `pytest tests/` 701 passed, 5 skipped (the 2 pre-existing `validate_configs.py::TestOpenclawConfig` failures relate to a separate AKOS sandbox-config drift unrelated to I29). Closure trail: dated [`reports/uat-i29-multi-phase-consolidation-2026-04-30.md`](docs/wip/planning/29-multi-phase-consolidation/reports/uat-i29-multi-phase-consolidation-2026-04-30.md) with full verification matrix + the 9 founder TODO[OPERATOR-*] markers itemised so the operator knows exactly what to fill before `--apply`.

- **Initiative 28 — Investor-style company dossier (Figma + HTML + PDF) (2026-04-30)** — Replaces the founder evidence dossier as the **primary** external send for the ENISA / company-dossier audience. Operator review of Initiative 27's output flagged that even the brand-aligned, jargon-free founder dossier is still a workbench — it explains the founding *program* and walks through open adviser questions, which is correct internal hygiene but the wrong artifact for an ENISA adviser, certifier, or investor-style reader. The new artifact is a 12-14 page **deck-style dossier** that sells the company story, not the program. New initiative folder [`docs/wip/planning/28-investor-style-company-dossier/`](docs/wip/planning/28-investor-style-company-dossier/) with master-roadmap (P0-P6 + accepted decisions D-IH-28-1..8 + asset classification + drift-handling rule), [`deck-brief.md`](docs/wip/planning/28-investor-style-company-dossier/deck-brief.md) (audience × promise × proof × objections × ask), and [`evidence-triage.md`](docs/wip/planning/28-investor-style-company-dossier/evidence-triage.md) (auditable mapping of every existing dossier block → main deck / appendix / discard). The existing `dossier_es.md` is **role-tagged** as adviser evidence appendix; its content is unchanged. New canonical narrative SSOT: [`deck_slides.yaml`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_company_dossier/deck_slides.yaml) (14 slides — cover, problem, insight, solution, method, proof grid + 5 capability cards, KiRBe spotlight, why-now, market-ICP, business-model today/bridge/tomorrow, moat 4 pillars, roadmap 0-24m, ENISA fit + use of funds, ask + signature) + [`deck_story_es.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_company_dossier/deck_story_es.md) (Spanish prose mirror) + [`deck-visual-system.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_company_dossier/deck-visual-system.md) (Figma-ready layout primitives, brand-token mapping, anti-patterns). Visual SSOT: a new Figma file at <https://www.figma.com/design/yiPav7BLxUulNFrrsoKJqW> with 14 frames at 1440×810 px built programmatically via the Figma MCP `use_figma` Plugin API (auto-layout, brand tokens applied directly, no captures). HTML preview deck: new [`scripts/build_company_deck.py`](scripts/build_company_deck.py) reads the YAML and emits a self-contained `docs/presentations/holistika-company-dossier/index.html` using a separate [`styles.css`](docs/presentations/holistika-company-dossier/styles.css) inlined at build time — zero npm dependency, single-file portable, Chrome `Save as PDF` works out of the box. Build script enforces a jargon-audit gate (refuses to write the HTML if any forbidden token reaches slide copy) and a slide-count guard (12-16). PDF deck export: new [`scripts/export_company_deck_pdf.py`](scripts/export_company_deck_pdf.py) renders the HTML preview through WeasyPrint at 1440×810 px page size with print-mode overrides → 14-page `holistika-company-dossier-enisa-2026-04-30.pdf` (66.7 KB; `sha256: C5DBAB04FEE04B2B0B1E0017A48CC50855487D3CAFBA1A8D73AF8431918493EF`). New [`cover_email_company_dossier_es.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_company_dossier/cover_email_company_dossier_es.md) — Spanish cover-email body that points at the deck **first** and offers the founder evidence appendix only on request, plus alt-tone variants for investor-style and certifier-style audiences in the internal-notes block. New closure trail [`reports/uat-company-dossier-send-2026-04-30.md`](docs/wip/planning/28-investor-style-company-dossier/reports/uat-company-dossier-send-2026-04-30.md) supersedes the I27 UAT for the **primary** send — the I27 UAT remains canonical for the appendix-only adviser send if the operator picks that path. Tests: new [`tests/test_company_deck.py`](tests/test_company_deck.py) (13 tests) covering deck YAML schema, slide-count window, layout completeness, jargon-free guarantee on YAML + narrative + cover email, build-script `--check-only` smoke, HTML deck primitive presence, zero `TODO[OPERATOR]` leaks, and Spanish opener/closer compliance. Verification: `validate_hlk` PASS (12 programs / 1093 processes / 65 roles / 5 topics); `validate_hlk_vault_links` PASS; `validate_hlk_km_manifests` PASS (11/11); `pytest tests/test_company_deck.py tests/test_render_dossier.py tests/test_compose_adviser_message.py tests/test_wave2_backfill.py tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py` 71 passed, 1 skipped. Drift-handling rule: Markdown/YAML SSOT wins for content; Figma wins for visual layout; HTML preview is fast iteration; PDF is disposable; any Figma copy edit must be backported to YAML before initiative close.

- **Initiative 27 follow-up — Brand jargon audit + visual upgrade for external dossiers (2026-04-29)** — Closes the operator-flagged "lots of jargon in our external docs / PDF is not appealing at all" gap. Two failure modes corrected at once: (1) the dossier was leaking internal codenames (`AKOS Strict`, `topic_*`, `plane`, `GOI/POI`, `RBAC`, `RLS`, `pgvector`, `Logfire`, `Cohere`, `RRF`, `BullMQ`, `Cloudflare R2`, `holistika_ops.*`, `kirbe.*`, `repo_slug`, `TODO[OPERATOR]` etc.) into prose meant for an external Spanish legal adviser; (2) the rendered PDF read like a markdown dump rather than a brand-aligned dossier. New canonical hand-authored companion [`BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_JARGON_AUDIT.md) codifies the **plain-words-externally** rule (operationalises pillar 3 of `BRAND_VOICE_FOUNDATION.md`): forbidden tokens by category (4.1 internal codenames / 4.2 stack jargon / 4.3 methodology shorthand / 4.4 operator-side process tokens), translation gallery (before/after for capability descriptions, operating model, open-question callouts), and a 5-question audit checklist for every external-bound deliverable. Brand voice pillar 3 in [`operator-answers-wave2.yaml`](docs/wip/planning/22a-i22-post-closure-followups/operator-answers-wave2.yaml) Section 2 strengthened to *"Plain words externally; jargon stays internal (we do the heavy lifting)"*; `voice_is_not` gains a fourth row "Jargon-leaking (internal codenames externally)" with a concrete anti-example; auto-emitted MDs ([`BRAND_VOICE_FOUNDATION.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_VOICE_FOUNDATION.md), [`BRAND_DO_DONT.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_DO_DONT.md)) regenerated from the YAML. **Visual upgrade** of [`akos/hlk_pdf_render.py`](akos/hlk_pdf_render.py) `render_pdf_branded` — inspired by modern proposal/dossier patterns (oversized cover title with eyebrow + bottom-strip metadata; numbered section openers via CSS counter `01`/`02`/`03`/`04`; `.stat-grid` callouts with big number + small-caps label; `.pull-quote` margin emphasis; `.capability-card` with charcoal-on-white head + tag-pill row + outcome body + footer; airier tables with bottom-only borders; uppercase letter-spaced section labels; better whitespace + line-height). New HTML post-processor [`_friendly_callout_labels_html`](akos/hlk_pdf_render.py) rewrites every `<blockquote>` whose first paragraph leads with `<strong>TODO[OPERATOR]</strong>` into a brand-aligned amber callout titled "Pregunta abierta para tu confirmación" (Spanish) / "Open question for your confirmation" (English); source markdown still carries the marker for operator grep/audit, only the rendered HTML is transformed. **Dossier rewrite**: [`dossier_es.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_evidence/dossier_es.md) fully rewritten body to plain Spanish, citing every claim by Q-row id only inside the structured Q-tracker appendix (where it's a checklist) and avoiding internal codenames everywhere else. Five capability cards in Apéndice C now use **outcome statements** instead of stack dumps (e.g. KiRBe goes from *"FastAPI + pgvector + 20+ routers + RRF + Cohere + Logfire + Cloud Run + Neo4j"* to *"búsqueda en lenguaje natural con re-clasificación semántica de resultados; aislamiento por organización con auditoría completa; facturación por uso integrada; despliegue cloud productivo con observabilidad continua"*), and each card gets a header eyebrow, tag-pill row, prose body, and footer with public-visibility status. Tests: [`tests/test_render_dossier.py`](tests/test_render_dossier.py) gains 6 new tests — `test_dossier_es_body_is_jargon_free` (PR-time guard against ~30 forbidden tokens leaking into the prose body), `test_dossier_es_uses_visual_primitives` (asserts `.lead`, `.stat-grid`, `.capability-card`, `.card-eyebrow`, `.tag` actually appear in the markdown source), `test_friendly_callout_transform_strips_todo_label` + English variant (verifies the HTML post-processor strips `TODO[OPERATOR]` and inserts the friendly label), `test_brand_pdf_css_includes_new_visual_classes` (verifies all new CSS hooks ship). Verified end-to-end render: 17-page PDF, 238 KB; **zero `TODO[OPERATOR]` strings** in rendered text; all 5 operator-decision callouts now display as amber "Pregunta abierta para tu confirmación" cards. UAT report [`uat-adviser-email-sent-2026-04-29.md`](docs/wip/planning/24-hlk-communication-methodology/reports/uat-adviser-email-sent-2026-04-29.md) §3 updated with Pass 1 (deprecated) + Pass 2 (final) sha256 manifest. Verification: `validate_hlk` PASS (12 programs / 1093 processes / 65 roles / 5 topics); `validate_hlk_vault_links` PASS; `validate_hlk_km_manifests` PASS (11/11); `pytest` 58 passed, 1 skipped across dossier + composer + wave2-backfill + sync-mirrors suites.

- **Initiative 27 follow-up — Tooling Standards SOP (canonical `npx supabase` + GitHub repo references) (2026-04-29)** — Closes the operator-flagged "harmonize the supabase invocation" gap surfaced after the I27 dossier render. New canonical [`SOP-HLK_TOOLING_STANDARDS_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Tech/System%20Owner/SOP-HLK_TOOLING_STANDARDS_001.md) codifies (§3.1) the **`npx supabase ...`** invocation contract — the Supabase CLI is pinned as a `devDependency` (`"supabase": "^2.93.1"` in `package.json`); `npx supabase` resolves the local pinned binary so every operator and CI runner picks up the same version automatically. Bare `supabase ...` is forbidden because it silently uses whatever globally-installed CLI exists (drift). §3.2 codifies external-repo citation as `https://github.com/FraysaXII/<repo>` via [`REPOSITORIES_REGISTRY.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Envoy%20Tech%20Lab/Repositories/REPOSITORIES_REGISTRY.md); local clone paths (`c:\Users\Shadow\cd_shadow\root_cd\<repo>`) are operator-workstation hints only. §3.3-§3.5 cover Mermaid CLI, pypandoc, and Python entrypoints. §4.1 includes a `rg`-friendly drift-detection regex. Sweep across canonical surfaces: [`supabase/README.md`](supabase/README.md) (operator quick card now `npx supabase login` / `npx supabase link --project-ref <REF>` / `npx supabase migration new` / `npx supabase db push`), [`supabase/migrations/README.md`](supabase/migrations/README.md) (pre-`db push` checklist + reconciliation steps), [`docs/DEVELOPER_CHECKLIST.md`](docs/DEVELOPER_CHECKLIST.md) (CLI essentials + ledger parity rows), [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) §"Supabase migration plane", [`docs/guides/understanding_verification.md`](docs/guides/understanding_verification.md), [`docs/guides/DEV_VERIFICATION_REFERENCE.md`](docs/guides/DEV_VERIFICATION_REFERENCE.md). [`BRAND_VISUAL_PATTERNS.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Marketing/Brand/BRAND_VISUAL_PATTERNS.md) and [`dossier_es.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_evidence/dossier_es.md) Apéndice C upgraded to cite `https://github.com/FraysaXII/<repo>` URLs (with explicit local-clone hints) for all five capability cards (boilerplate, hlk-erp, kirbe-platform, holistika-websitz-shopify-bundles, rushly-saas-scaffold). [`akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) gains two new sync-trigger rows for the SOP and the brand visual patterns file so future doc edits keep both in sync. UAT report [`uat-adviser-email-sent-2026-04-29.md`](docs/wip/planning/24-hlk-communication-methodology/reports/uat-adviser-email-sent-2026-04-29.md) §3 updated with the actually-rendered sha256 hashes captured by the operator (`6081B3B6...` for dossier PDF, `49BD66C0...` for appendix handoff PDF, `EFBDEBBF...` for cover email DOCX). Historical planning reports under `docs/wip/planning/14-*/`, `22-*/`, `22a-*/` keep their prior `supabase ...` text intact (they are dated audit records of what was actually run). Verification: `pytest tests/test_render_dossier.py tests/test_compose_adviser_message.py tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py` 33 passed, 1 skipped; `validate_hlk` PASS; `validate_hlk_vault_links` PASS; `validate_hlk_km_manifests` PASS.

New `BRAND_TOKENS_LIGHT` / `BRAND_TOKENS_DARK` constant tables mirror the pattern doc as the single source of truth. Five `TODO[OPERATOR]` callouts (objeto social text + CNAE pick `7219+6202`, capital amount, EUR figures, PESTEL most-material factors, workforce-in-Spain plan). **Apéndice C "Capacidades demostradas"** lifts five concrete production deliveries from external repos as evidence: (1) Holistika Boilerplate (Next.js 14 + i18n en/es/fr + Sentry + Playwright + GTM + CRM/ERP intake; live brand source); (2) HLK ERP (multi-theme 7 modes + 53 shadcn components + full CI gates); (3) KiRBe SaaS (FastAPI + Supabase pgvector + 20+ routers + RRF hybrid search + Cohere rerank + Stripe metered billing + RBAC + Logfire + Cloud Run + Neo4j sync); (4) Use Case 1 Holistika × Websitz Shopify cart-bundle app (Polaris admin + Prisma+Postgres + 91+30 tests + i18n 6 locales + Vercel); (5) Use Case 2 Rushly partner SaaS scaffold (governed monorepo + frozen architecture + multi-job CI). New canonical [`cover_email_es.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_evidence/cover_email_es.md) — hand-authored ~145-word Spanish body per `BRAND_SPANISH_PATTERNS` ("Buenos días, Guillermo," opener, mixed peer register + tú, framing paragraph for dossier attachment naming `Q-LEG-001` + `Q-LEG-002` + `Q-CRT-001` + `Q-CRT-002`, concrete next-step window, "Un saludo," close). New [`scripts/render_dossier.py`](scripts/render_dossier.py) — thin wrapper that reads `dossier_es.md`, strips frontmatter, resolves the cover metadata + monogram, calls `render_pdf_branded`, and emits a JSON manifest with sha256 hashes for the closure trail (`--manifest`, `--smoke`, `--issue-date`). [`scripts/compose_adviser_message.py`](scripts/compose_adviser_message.py) gains `--profile dossier` (branded cover + body styling), `--cover-title` / `--cover-subtitle` (operator-set), and `--body <path>` (inlines hand-authored body into Layer 4 instead of emitting the TODO marker — keeps the composer SOC clean: it composes structure; `BRAND_SPANISH_PATTERNS` authors prose). [`scripts/export_adviser_handoff.py`](scripts/export_adviser_handoff.py) gains `--profile dossier` so the appendix-handoff PDF pairs visually with the dossier (dark hero band cover labelled "Apéndice — Cuestionario asesores"). New [`TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/dimensions/TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv) row `topic_enisa_dossier_es` (`parent_topic=topic_enisa_evidence`, `program_id=PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026`, `plane=advops`, `manifest_path` pointing at the dossier MD); topic graph re-rendered to 5 nodes; UPSERT SQL staged at [`artifacts/sql/i27_topic_registry_dossier_es_upsert.sql`](artifacts/sql/i27_topic_registry_dossier_es_upsert.sql) for operator to apply (Supabase MCP not auth'd in this session; drift probe will detect the gap on next run). Tests: [`tests/test_render_dossier.py`](tests/test_render_dossier.py) (10 tests covering brand-token presence + drift safeguard, branded PDF CSS classes, `render_pdf_branded` signature, smoke success + unknown-language refusal, citation discipline (every active `Q-row` cited), 5 capability cards present, topic registry row, composer body inlining + TODO fallback). [`tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py`](tests/test_sync_compliance_mirrors_from_csv.py) updated for current `process_list_rows=1093` + new `program_registry_rows` / `topic_registry_rows` assertions. Smoke renders confirmed: dossier PDF 245 KB via WeasyPrint with full brand cover; cover-email PDF + DOCX via pypandoc + WeasyPrint; appendix-handoff PDF branded.

- **Initiative 25 P3 + P4 + P5 + P6 + P7 + P8 + P9 — Topic graph render, wikilinks, PMO hub auto-gen, ENISA backfill, .mmd-first rule, batch render, closure (2026-04-29)** — **Initiative 25 closed (P0–P9).** Topics are now first-class governed entities. P0+P1+P2+Pgraph landed earlier in [PR #19](https://github.com/FraysaXII/openclaw-akos/pull/19); this PR ships the remaining phases. **P3** — [`scripts/render_topic_graph.py`](scripts/render_topic_graph.py) reads `dimensions/TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv` and emits `_assets/_meta/topic_graph.{mmd,png,svg}` (4 topics clustered into 3 subgraphs by `program_id`; 2 cross-cluster edges); reuses `render_km_diagrams.py` for the PNG/SVG pipeline; idempotent. **P4** — Obsidian wikilink secondary nav adopted in topic companion `.md` (proof in `topic_enisa_evidence.md`); explicitly out-of-scope for `validate_hlk_vault_links.py` per D-IH-12. **P5** — [`scripts/render_pmo_hub.py`](scripts/render_pmo_hub.py) emits the stakeholder index between `<!-- BEGIN_AUTOGEN section_id=pmo_stakeholder_index_v1 ... -->` / `<!-- END_AUTOGEN ... sha256=<hex> -->` markers in [`TOPIC_PMO_CLIENT_DELIVERY_HUB.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/TOPIC_PMO_CLIENT_DELIVERY_HUB.md); refuse-to-write on missing markers; sha256 drift detection; idempotent; verify profile `render_pmo_hub_smoke`. **P6** (G-25-2) — ENISA backfill ships `topic_enisa_evidence.{mmd,manifest.md,md,png,svg}` under `_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/enisa_evidence/`; row in `TOPIC_REGISTRY.csv`; topic edge `topic_enisa_evidence depends_on topic_external_adviser_handoff` registered in CSV + projected into Neo4j as `:DEPENDS_ON`. **P7** — `.mmd`-first P0 rule extension to [`akos-planning-traceability.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-planning-traceability.mdc) per D-IH-13: initiatives producing or modifying a Topic must commit the `.mmd` source-of-truth in P0 alongside `master-roadmap.md`; consume-only initiatives exempt. **P8** — [`scripts/render_km_diagrams.py`](scripts/render_km_diagrams.py) gains `--all` (batch render every `.mmd` under `_assets/`) + `--dry-run` (no writes; prints what would render); `_render_one` extracted as helper; `_discover_mmd_sources` walks the assets root and skips `.git/`; verify profile `render_km_diagrams_batch_smoke`. **P9** — Dated [`reports/uat-i25-topic-graph-20260429.md`](docs/wip/planning/25-hlk-topic-graph-and-km-scalability/reports/uat-i25-topic-graph-20260429.md) with verification matrix (validate_hlk PASS / vault links PASS / km manifests PASS / 29/29 pytest PASS / topic graph render PASS / PMO hub auto-gen PASS / batch render PASS / wikilinks adopted / `.mmd`-first rule applied / ENISA backfill complete); master-roadmap closure note covering all 11 phases; cursor-rules-hygiene checkbox CONFIRMED. New tests at [`tests/test_render_topic_graph.py`](tests/test_render_topic_graph.py) (11 tests), [`tests/test_render_km_diagrams_batch.py`](tests/test_render_km_diagrams_batch.py) (10 tests), [`tests/test_render_pmo_hub.py`](tests/test_render_pmo_hub.py) (8 tests). New verify profiles in [`config/verification-profiles.json`](config/verification-profiles.json): `render_topic_graph_smoke`, `render_km_diagrams_batch_smoke`.

- **Initiative 26 P0 + P1 — ops-hardening bootstrap + persistent re-eval-trigger templates + pinned mmdc CI runbook (2026-04-29)** — Bootstraps Initiative 26 with the standard six artifacts at [`docs/wip/planning/26-hlk-ops-hardening/`](docs/wip/planning/26-hlk-ops-hardening/) (master-roadmap, decision-log with D-IH-26-A..E, asset-classification, evidence-matrix, risk-register, reports/). P1 ships the **pinned `mmdc@^11` install runbook** in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md): operator workstation install + Linux CI extras (`libgbm1`, `fonts-liberation`, `fonts-noto-color-emoji`, `--no-sandbox`); rationale documented in D-IH-26-A (non-pinned installs render Mermaid 10 vs 11 differently due to default theme + edge-routing changes) and D-IH-26-B (puppeteer-bundled Chromium silently crashes without the apt extras on Ubuntu CI). P2 (service_role quarterly rotation), P3 (WeasyPrint GTK3 Windows runbook), P4 (conditional `compliance/<plane>/` relocation — DEFERRED per D-IH-26-E), P5 (UAT + closure) follow.

- **Initiative 23 P6 + P7 + P8 — KiRBe onboarding + closure (2026-04-29)** — Closes Initiative 23 (all 9 phases). P6 onboarding ships 6 evidence-based KiRBe role-folder READMEs at `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/{Tech/System Owner,Data/Architecture,Data/Governance,Finance/Business Controller,People,Operations/PMO}/programs/PRJ-HOL-KIR-2026/README.md` (Compliance/Legal/Marketing remain on-demand per the evidence-based table — no KiRBe-specific `process_list.csv` rows for those chains today). One KM topic asset (`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/techops/PRJ-HOL-KIR-2026/topic_kirbe_billing_plane_routing/`) with `.mmd` source-of-truth + manifest + companion + rendered PNG/SVG (sha256 `779e9f8e616b4e41…`) **proves the layout convention works at N=2 programs** (the success criterion for the Initiative 22 forward layout). [`docs/references/hlk/v3.0/index.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/index.md) §"Program-scoped casework" extended to list both `PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026` (3 folders) and `PRJ-HOL-KIR-2026` (6 folders). G-23-2 satisfied by existing `env_tech_prj_2 KiRBe Platform` row in `process_list.csv`; G-23-3 N/A (no new role required). The `consumes_program_ids` edge `PRJ-HOL-KIR-2026 → PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026` is registered in CSV and projected into Neo4j as `:CONSUMES`. **P4 drift probe surfaced AND fixed real drift**: `compliance.process_list_mirror=1083` vs canonical CSV=1091 (8-row delta — Initiative 21 ADVOPS tranche + Initiative 18/19 finance rows that were never re-seeded after row count changed); all 8 rows re-seeded via MCP `execute_sql` with `service_role`; live mirror now reports 1091 = CSV; `compliance_mirror_drift_probe` now PASSes 8/8. P7 doc sync: [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) gains the drift-probe operator runbook section; [`master-roadmap.md`](docs/wip/planning/23-hlk-program-registry-and-program-2/master-roadmap.md) status set to **Closed (2026-04-29)** with closure note covering all 9 phases. P8 UAT: dated [`reports/uat-i23-program-registry-20260429.md`](docs/wip/planning/23-hlk-program-registry-and-program-2/reports/uat-i23-program-registry-20260429.md) with results table, P6 onboarding evidence, operator follow-ups, cursor-rules-hygiene checkbox CONFIRMED. **D-IH-23-B**: product programs use 3-letter `program_code` as the slug (`PRJ-HOL-KIR-2026`, not `PRJ-HOL-KIRBE-2026`); casework programs keep the long form (`PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026`). Verification: `validate_hlk.py` PASS (12 programs / 1091 processes / 65 roles); `validate_hlk_vault_links.py` PASS; `validate_hlk_km_manifests.py` PASS; 37/37 pytest PASS; `compliance_mirror_drift_probe` PASS (8/8 mirrors at parity after the i21/i18 reseed). Initiative 22 stays closed.

- **Initiative 23 P3 + P4 + P5 — cross-asset validator + mirror drift probe + cross-program glossary (2026-04-29)** — Closes the validators / drift / glossary slice of Initiative 23 (Program Registry). New [`scripts/validate_program_id_consistency.py`](scripts/validate_program_id_consistency.py) (P3) scans every `program_id` reference in `GOI_POI_REGISTER.csv` / `ADVISER_OPEN_QUESTIONS.csv` / `FOUNDER_FILED_INSTRUMENTS.csv` / `_assets/<plane>/<program_id>/<topic_id>/` / vault `programs/<program_id>/` folders and asserts each resolves to a row in `dimensions/PROGRAM_REGISTRY.csv`; integrated into [`scripts/validate_hlk.py`](scripts/validate_hlk.py) and SKIPs gracefully when the registry is absent. New [`scripts/probe_compliance_mirror_drift.py`](scripts/probe_compliance_mirror_drift.py) (P4) emits a `UNION ALL` `SELECT` covering all 8 currently-shipped compliance mirrors; operator pastes the JSON result into `artifacts/probes/mirror-drift-<YYYYMMDD>.json`; `--verify` (default) compares live counts against canonical CSV row counts and reports row-by-row PASS/FAIL; SKIPs gracefully when no fresh artifact exists. New verify profile `compliance_mirror_drift_probe` in [`config/verification-profiles.json`](config/verification-profiles.json). New [`docs/reference/glossary-cross-program.md`](docs/reference/glossary-cross-program.md) (P5) — flat lookup for program codes, discipline codes, sensitivity bands, sharing labels, GOI/POI class taxonomy, status enums, voice registers; canonical sources cited per family. [`docs/GLOSSARY.md`](docs/GLOSSARY.md) gains "Program", "Cross-program glossary", and "Drift probe" rows. Tests: [`tests/test_validate_program_id_consistency.py`](tests/test_validate_program_id_consistency.py) (7 tests covering regex, reserved keywords, FK loading, repo PASS/SKIP), [`tests/test_probe_compliance_mirror_drift.py`](tests/test_probe_compliance_mirror_drift.py) (13 tests covering contract coverage, SQL emission, csv_counts, verify PASS/FAIL/SKIP semantics, malformed-payload handling). Source scripts hardened against `relative_to(REPO_ROOT)` failure on tmp paths.

- **Initiative 22a — Post-closure follow-ups + Wave-2 bootstrap (2026-04-29)** — Lands the unblocking artifacts for Wave-2 (Initiatives 23-26) without reopening the closed I22 folder. New folder [`docs/wip/planning/22a-i22-post-closure-followups/`](docs/wip/planning/22a-i22-post-closure-followups/) carries: (a) operator-pasted slots for the `supabase migration list` parity check + pinned `mmdc@^11` install in [`reports/post-closure-followups-20260429.md`](docs/wip/planning/22a-i22-post-closure-followups/reports/post-closure-followups-20260429.md); (b) a documented **DO-NOT-ADD** recommendation for the FINOPS / TECHOPS second-CSV question (re-eval triggers deferred to I26-P0 templates); (c) the **operator-answers YAML** at [`operator-answers-wave2.yaml`](docs/wip/planning/22a-i22-post-closure-followups/operator-answers-wave2.yaml) — single source of all Wave-2 operator decisions across 5 sections (Program Registry × 12 rows pre-filled with derivable values + sentinels for Tier-3 cells; Brand voice foundation; GOI/POI voice profiles × 6; KiRBe duality confirmations; G-24-3 founder sign-off), filled in batched sittings (~5-7h total); (d) the **scaffolder** [`scripts/wave2_backfill.py`](scripts/wave2_backfill.py) with `--check-only` (sentinel scan; always exit 0; informational), `--dry-run` (refuses if target sections carry sentinels), `--section <name>` (process one section), `--allow-pending` (partial pass during operator review); validator-enforced `__OPERATOR_CONFIRM__` sentinels make it structurally impossible to merge a canonical CSV with placeholder cells; (e) verify profile `wave2_backfill_check` in [`config/verification-profiles.json`](config/verification-profiles.json); (f) tests at [`tests/test_wave2_backfill.py`](tests/test_wave2_backfill.py) lock in sentinel detection, section gating, the shipped YAML contract (12 programs, unique `^[A-Z]{3}$` codes), and write-mode refusal semantics. Planning README slot 27 reserved for the deferred `process_list.csv` per-plane re-architecture so Wave-2 (23-26) lands contiguously. Cursor-rules sync row added to [`akos-docs-config-sync.mdc`](.cursor/rules/akos-docs-config-sync.mdc) for the scaffolder + YAML.

- **Initiative 22 — Scalable HLK hierarchy + Initiative 21 closures (2026-04-29)** — Establishes a forward `<plane> × <program_id> × <topic_id>` layout convention so the canonical surfaces stop being coupled to one program. New canonical README at [`docs/references/hlk/compliance/README.md`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/README.md) (deprecation-alias map for legacy flat files); [`PRECEDENCE.md`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/PRECEDENCE.md) gains a "Layout convention (forward)" subsection; [`HLK_KM_TOPIC_FACT_SOURCE.md`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/HLK_KM_TOPIC_FACT_SOURCE.md) gains the `paths.mermaid` slot, source-of-truth rule, and `_assets/<plane>/<program_id>/<topic_id>/` directory convention. KM topic relocated under the new layout: `docs/references/hlk/v3.0/_assets/advops/PRJ-HOL-FOUNDING-2026/adviser_handoff/` now holds `topic_external_adviser_handoff.{manifest.md,md,mmd,png,svg}` (the placeholder PNG is replaced by a real Mermaid-rendered diagram, 295 KB PNG + 56 KB SVG, sha256 `3302eb9d595442c4…`). New deterministic CLI [`scripts/render_km_diagrams.py`](scripts/render_km_diagrams.py) (mmdc preferred, mermaid.ink HTTP fallback) writes both raster and vector and updates manifest sha256 atomically. Vault role-folders gain a **`programs/<program_id>/README.md`** convention under Legal, Compliance, and Operations/PMO; existing `FOUNDER_*` / `ENISA_*` files at the role-folder root receive a back-pointer admonition (no link breakage). [`v3.0/index.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/index.md) now documents "Program-scoped casework" under the promotion ladder. [`scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py`](scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py) extends the `class` enum to `client`, `partner`, `investor`, `regulator`, `vendor`, `media` for multi-program reuse; [`SOP-HLK_GOIPOI_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/SOP-HLK_GOIPOI_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md) gains §4.7 (class taxonomy disambiguation) and §4.8 ("Onboarding a new program"). [`scripts/export_adviser_handoff.py`](scripts/export_adviser_handoff.py) now renders **PDF** via WeasyPrint (best CSS fidelity, requires native Cairo/Pango) → fpdf2 (pure-Python, zero native deps) → pandoc (external) chain; new `requirements-export.txt` opt-in deps; new verification profile `export_adviser_handoff_pdf_smoke` in [`config/verification-profiles.json`](config/verification-profiles.json). Initiative-21 deferred actions closed: live Supabase apply via user-supabase MCP `apply_migration` for the four `compliance.*_mirror` DDL files plus `execute_sql` seed DML (`service_role`); migration filenames renamed to match remote `schema_migrations` ledger (`20260429081{728,734,754,800}_i21_compliance_*.sql`) per the Initiative 18 ledger-parity rule; row counts validated against CSV (6 / 6 / 12 / 1); evidence in [`docs/wip/planning/22-hlk-scalability-and-i21-closures/reports/p7-supabase-apply-evidence.md`](docs/wip/planning/22-hlk-scalability-and-i21-closures/reports/p7-supabase-apply-evidence.md). `git filter-repo` posture re-affirmed as **DEFERRED — trigger not met** with documented re-eval contract in [`docs/wip/planning/22-hlk-scalability-and-i21-closures/reports/re-eval-trigger.md`](docs/wip/planning/22-hlk-scalability-and-i21-closures/reports/re-eval-trigger.md). Initiative folder [`docs/wip/planning/22-hlk-scalability-and-i21-closures/`](docs/wip/planning/22-hlk-scalability-and-i21-closures/) with master roadmap, decision log (D-IH-1..D-IH-7), asset classification, evidence matrix, and reports.

- **Initiative 21 — Adviser Engagement plane + GOI/POI dimension (2026-04-28)** — New ADVOPS plane (External Adviser Engagement) parallel to MKTOPS/FINOPS/OPS/TECHOPS for Legal / Fiscal / IP / Banking / Certification / Notary engagements. Four new canonical CSVs under [`docs/references/hlk/compliance/`](docs/references/hlk/compliance/): **`GOI_POI_REGISTER.csv`** (knowledge dimension for Groups/Persons of Interest with deterministic obfuscation; raw mapping off-repo), **`ADVISER_ENGAGEMENT_DISCIPLINES.csv`** (disciplines lookup), **`ADVISER_OPEN_QUESTIONS.csv`** (graduates `FOUNDER_OPEN_QUESTIONS_EXTERNAL_COUNSEL.md` to SSOT; vault MD becomes derived per-discipline view), **`FOUNDER_FILED_INSTRUMENTS.csv`** (graduates `FOUNDER_FILED_INSTRUMENT_REGISTER.md` to SSOT; vault MD becomes derived per-discipline view). Akos field contracts: `akos/hlk_goipoi_csv.py`, `akos/hlk_adviser_disciplines_csv.py`, `akos/hlk_adviser_questions_csv.py`, `akos/hlk_founder_filed_instruments_csv.py`. Validators: `scripts/validate_goipoi_register.py`, `scripts/validate_adviser_disciplines.py`, `scripts/validate_adviser_questions.py`, `scripts/validate_founder_filed_instruments.py` (all integrated into `scripts/validate_hlk.py`). New plane SOP [`SOP-EXTERNAL_ADVISER_ENGAGEMENT_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/SOP-EXTERNAL_ADVISER_ENGAGEMENT_001.md), router [`EXTERNAL_ADVISER_ROUTER.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/Operations/PMO/EXTERNAL_ADVISER_ROUTER.md), Compliance SOPs [`SOP-HLK_GOIPOI_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/SOP-HLK_GOIPOI_REGISTER_MAINTENANCE_001.md), [`SOP-HLK_TRANSCRIPT_REDACTION_001.md`](docs/references/hlk/v3.0/Admin/O5-1/People/Compliance/SOP-HLK_TRANSCRIPT_REDACTION_001.md). ### Changed

- **Terminology — ShadowGPU vs ShadowPC (2026-04-24)** — First-class docs (`ARCHITECTURE`, `USER_GUIDE` §8.8, `README`, `GLOSSARY`), `gpu-shadow.env.example`, GPU/`openstack_provider` strings, and related planning notes now use **ShadowGPU** for the OpenStack cloud GPU service and **ShadowPC** only for the local machine product. Runtime profile name `gpu-shadow` is unchanged. **`config/openclaw.json.example`:** `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode` is `all` to match the OpenClaw CLI schema (rejects `strict`).

- **ARCHITECTURE** — `tools.exec` / Path B bullet updated for `sandbox.mode: all` (OpenClaw-allowed values).

### Fixed

- **`scripts/adhoc/_apply_branding.py`** — Stops writing `gateway.controlUi` (OpenClaw 2026.4.x rejects unknown nested keys and can block gateway startup).

- **`doctor.py --repair-gateway`** — When upstream recovery does not reach HTTP+RPC health, print probe state and operator hints (`gateway logs`, port 18789, dashboard). When hints are available from `akos.runtime`, also print **recovery_hints** (status + RPC excerpt + keyword-filtered log tail: pricing timeout, WebSocket 1006).

- **Browser smoke Phase 2** — `architect_tools_ui` and `executor_approval_hint` now validate against **control plane** `GET /agents` (JSON SSOT) like `agent_visibility`, instead of brittle OpenClaw Control UI copy on `GATEWAY_URL/agents` (which false-failed under msedge / OpenClaw version drift).

- **Workspace scaffold README** — Documents that `py scripts/bootstrap.py` Phase 4 (`deploy_soul_prompts`) writes all five `SOUL.md` files after `assemble-prompts.py`, alongside `switch-model.py`.

- **Initiative 08 — runtime / operator / graph stack** — Planning SSOT `docs/wip/planning/08-python-runtime-deployment/master-roadmap.md` (indexed from `docs/wip/planning/README.md`).
- **`akos/graph_stack.py` + serve-api supervision** — `scripts/serve-api.py` optionally starts Streamlit `hlk_graph_explorer.py` as a **child process** when `NEO4J_*` is non-placeholder and Bolt is reachable; `--no-graph-explorer`, `AKOS_GRAPH_EXPLORER=0`, `--open`; background **validate-then-sync** for canonical CSV fingerprints (`AKOS_NEO4J_AUTO_SYNC`, `AKOS_NEO4J_SYNC_POLL_SECONDS`, `AKOS_NEO4J_SYNC_WATCH`, `AKOS_NEO4J_SYNC_WITH_DOCUMENTS`); `GET /health` gains `graph_explorer` and `neo4j_mirror`; `neo4j_env_non_placeholder()` in `akos/hlk_neo4j.py`; `reset_neo4j_driver_cache()` after sync; debounced mirror kick from `GET /hlk/graph/summary`.
- **Operator / test / CI** — `scripts/akos_operator.py` thin dispatcher; `scripts/test.py` group `graph` (`pytest -m graph`); `neo4j` pytest marker; `tests/test_graph_stack.py`; optional **`Dockerfile`**; **`.github/workflows/neo4j-graph-integration.yml`** manual job with Neo4j service + sync + `pytest -m neo4j`.
- **`load_model_workflow_ssot()`** — `akos/model_catalog.py` reads `config/model-tiers.json` as the tier × variant overlay SSOT.

### Changed

- **OpenClaw gateway SSOT (`config/openclaw.json.example`)** — **Path B:** `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode: strict` and **`tools.exec.host: sandbox`** with allowlist exec security. **Path C:** removed **`web_search`** / **`web_fetch`** from Orchestrator and Architect `alsoAllow`; Architect keeps coarse **`browser`**. Removed **`gateway.controlUi`**. `akos.models`: `SandboxDefaults`, **`ExecConfig.host`** default **`sandbox`**, **`LangfuseTraceContext`** gains **`research_surface`** and eval metadata fields. `config/environments/dev-local.json` sets **`thinkingDefault: medium`** for DeepSeek R1 dev-local. Prompt bases (`ORCHESTRATOR_BASE`, `ARCHITECT_BASE`) document the research spine; USER_GUIDE §14.3a–b; SECURITY audit summary; `scripts/doctor.py` Windows Docker/WSL WARNs.

- **Capability matrix** — Orchestrator/Architect drop ambient web tools; Architect lists coarse **`browser`** for policy alignment with gateway.

- **Gateway recovery UX** — `recover_gateway_service()` uses a short post-`gateway start` sleep, default **150s** wait window, `fetch_gateway_status` + `probe_gateway_rpc_detail`, and builds **`GatewayRecoveryResult.recovery_hints`** from status output, optional **File log(s):** tail (filtered), and RPC capture (aligned with observability / SOC: no unconstrained log dumps).

- **Python packaging** — Root **`pyproject.toml`** adds `[project]` `requires-python = ">=3.10"` and pytest markers; **`.python-version`** pins **3.13** for dev; **`openstacksdk`** moved to **`requirements-openstack.txt`** (core `requirements.txt` stays lean).
- **HLK role neighbourhood (mirror vs SSOT)** — `GET /hlk/graph/role/{role_name}/neighbourhood` resolves names via the HLK registry, returns **`mirror_sync_hint`** when Neo4j has no `Role` node yet, and Neo4j matching tolerates legacy **`05-1` → `O5-1`**. Streamlit `scripts/hlk_graph_explorer.py` and static `static/hlk_graph_explorer.html` surface the sync hint instead of a raw JSON blob when the API reports `not_found`.
- **HLK graph zoom landmarks** — `static/hlk_graph_explorer.html` and `static/streamlit_components/hlk_vis_network/index.html` show **hub names only when zoomed out** (largest vis `size` first, hysteresis on scale) so operators can orient before zooming in; full labels return when zoomed in. Streamlit `graph_engine` help notes `streamlit-agraph` cannot attach this behaviour.
- **HLK HTML graph explorer** — `static/hlk_graph_explorer.html` now mirrors the Streamlit canvas conventions: **PARENT_OF**/**OWNED_BY**-based **node sizing** (children / degree / balanced), **edge abbrev + tooltips + colours/widths**, **node hue nudge** by stable id, legend swatches, **edge label mode** (auto/always/hover-only), and **sessionStorage** for those prefs (re-render without re-fetch).
- **Python dependencies** — Moved `runpod` from `requirements.txt` to optional `requirements-gpu.txt` so `pip install -r requirements.txt` succeeds on platforms where `cryptography` has no wheel yet (for example Windows with Python 3.14 free-threaded). RunPod flows remain a no-op until `pip install -r requirements-gpu.txt`; README and USER_GUIDE document the two-step install.
- **HLK Graph Explorer (Streamlit) UX** — `scripts/hlk_graph_explorer.py` now covers **force vs tree** hierarchical layout (with `REPORTS_TO` cycle fallback), **semantic node/edge colour + legend**, **edge labels/tooltips/thickness**, **node sizing** (PARENT_OF / OWNED_BY / degree / balanced), **lock layout**, **favicon** (`static/hlk_graph_explorer_favicon.png`), **selection expander**, and sidebar **`graph_engine`** (`streamlit-agraph` vs **`vis_component`** local bundle under `static/streamlit_components/hlk_vis_network/` for drag-end pin / magnetic MVP). Prior explorer notes (bulk discovery, preset v2, shell links, glossary) remain; USER_GUIDE + ARCHITECTURE updated.
- **HLK PMO portfolio SSOT + registry hygiene** — `TOPIC_PMO_CLIENT_DELIVERY_HUB.md` portfolio row links `hol_opera_dtp_310` and in-repo `external/` transcripts. **`baseline_organisation.csv`**: Chief Business Officer `role_name` **`O5-1`** (letter O) and official description. **`process_list.csv`**: new process **`hol_opera_dtp_310`** *PMO project portfolio SSOT* under Program Management. **`scripts/merge_gtm_into_process_list.py`**: removed legacy `05-1` parent mapping. UTF-8 exports under `docs/wip/planning/04-holistika-company-formation/external/` (pre-kicks, founder report export, ENISA kick-off parts) with genericized external individual names. `access_levels.md` / `ARCHITECTURE.md` process count (1,066). `FOUNDER_INCORPORATION_KNOWLEDGE_INDEX.md` anchor for `hol_opera_dtp_310`. USER_GUIDE PMO hub pointer unchanged in substance.

### Added

- **HLK Graph Explorer vis Streamlit component** — Checked-in `static/streamlit_components/hlk_vis_network/index.html` (vis-network CDN + minimal Streamlit `postMessage` bridge) so operators can opt into **dragEnd**-driven **pin** state without forking Streamlit; session-round-trips `selectedId`, `pinnedIds`, and `positions` for the same neighbourhood JSON as `streamlit-agraph`.
- **HLK graph closeout (control plane + DI)** — `GET /hlk/graph/explorer` serves `static/hlk_graph_explorer.html` (vis-network CDN); `OVERLAY_HLK_GRAPH.md` + `config/model-tiers.json` standard/full wiring; compact `OVERLAY_HLK_COMPACT.md` forbids `hlk_graph_*` in prompt ladder; graph bullets removed from `MADEIRA_BASE.md` / `MADEIRA_PROMPT.md` (graph docs via overlay on larger tiers). `scripts/browser-smoke.py` parses worker `JSON_RESULTS` on non-zero exit; exit `2` when no parseable JSON; `release-gate` treats `2` as soft PASS; `tests/test_browser_smoke_parse.py`. USER_GUIDE §9.10 production ops; SOP §9.1a; `.github/workflows/neo4j-graph-integration.yml` stub; evidence `reports/uat-neo4j-graph-evidence-20260415.md`.
- **HLK Neo4j graph projection (mirrored)** — CSV-derived `Role` / `Process` graph in Neo4j via `akos/hlk_graph_model.py`, `akos/hlk_neo4j.py`, and `scripts/sync_hlk_neo4j.py` (`--with-documents` for v3.0 `Document` + `LINKS_TO`). OpenClaw env bootstrap helper `bootstrap_openclaw_process_env()` in `akos/io.py`. REST `GET /hlk/graph/summary`, `/hlk/graph/process/{item_id}/neighbourhood`, `/hlk/graph/role/{role_name}/neighbourhood`; MCP `scripts/hlk_graph_mcp_server.py`; `openclaw-plugins/akos-runtime-tools` bridges; `config/mcporter.json.example` `hlk-graph` server; optional `compose.neo4j.yml` and `scripts/hlk_graph_explorer.py` (Streamlit). Vault link CI: `scripts/validate_hlk_vault_links.py` + `akos/hlk_vault_links.py` wired into `scripts/release-gate.py`. Planning initiative `docs/wip/planning/07-hlk-neo4j-graph-projection/`. `PRECEDENCE.md` mirrored row for Neo4j; `NEO4J_*` placeholders in all `config/environments/*.env.example`.
- **HLK process list parent IDs and program layer** — Canonical `process_list.csv` gains **`item_parent_2_id`** / **`item_parent_1_id`** (dual-written with existing name columns). Shared column order and resolution live in **`akos/hlk_process_csv.py`**; **`scripts/backfill_process_parent_ids.py`** upgrades or repairs rows; **`scripts/migrate_process_list_program_layer.py`** inserts optional **`hlk_prog_*`** program workstreams under MADEIRA Platform and Think Big Operational Excellence. Registry exposes children by stable parent id (**`HlkRegistry.get_process_tree_by_parent_id`**) and **`GET /hlk/processes/id/{item_id}/tree`**. **`scripts/validate_hlk.py`** enforces id–name consistency (strict parent ids for uniquely named parents; project rows must not set parent ids). Semantic reviews: `docs/wip/planning/02-hlk-on-akos-madeira/reports/process-list-program-layer-semantic-review-20260415.md`, `process-list-parent-id-semantic-review-20260415.md`.
- **HLK duplicate `item_name` cleanup** — Added **`scripts/dedupe_ambiguous_process_item_names.py`** (operator-run; **`--report`** lists collisions) to disambiguate legacy display-name collisions so parent ids resolve everywhere; **`akos.hlk_process_csv.item_name_uniqueness_errors`** and **`scripts/validate_hlk.py`** check **Unique item_name**; **`scripts/backfill_process_parent_ids.py`** fails fast on duplicates. USER_GUIDE §24.3.4 documents **`PROCESS_LIST_FIELDNAMES`**; **`PRECEDENCE.md`** process baseline row notes uniqueness and the canonical asset table structure is corrected.

- **HLK founder-governance case layer** — Add canonical case docs for entity-formation decisions, capitalization posture, ENISA evidence, trademark scope, Research-vs-Tech-Lab separation, and the founder-governance lifecycle/promotion ladder under role-owned `v3.0` paths.
- **HLK founder-governance SOPs** — Add canonical `v3.0` drafts for `Founder Entity Formation Readiness`, `Trademark and Naming Governance`, `Founder-to-Company Funding Path`, and `ENISA Readiness and Evidence Pack` under the role-owned Legal, Finance/Taxes, and Compliance paths.
- **HLK founder-governance registry rows** — Register matching workstreams/processes in `docs/references/hlk/compliance/process_list.csv` for founder entity formation, trademark control, founder funding, and ENISA readiness (baseline before the GTM tranche; see Unreleased GTM merge entry for current inventory).
- **Windows gateway port recovery** — `akos.runtime.recover_gateway_service()` now clears stale TCP listeners on port `18789` via `netstat`/`taskkill` after `gateway stop` on Windows, aligning with the `py scripts/doctor.py --repair-gateway` operator path and reducing post-reboot manual rescue.
- **Planning traceability** — `docs/wip/planning/02-hlk-on-akos-madeira/phase-7-plan.md` and `reports/phase-7-report.md` record the Gateway and GPU Recovery Hardening rollout (env contract, Windows supervision, operator runbooks).
- **ShadowPC OpenStack GPU provider** -- `akos/openstack_provider.py` provides full lifecycle management (instance creation, floating IP, security groups, teardown, spot termination detection) for Shadow's OpenStack GPU infrastructure (RTX A4500/RTX 2000 Ada). Config: `gpu-shadow.json` overlay, `gpu-shadow.env.example`, `OpenStackInstanceConfig` Pydantic model in `akos/models.py`.
- **`deploy-shadow` GPU CLI subcommand** -- `scripts/gpu.py deploy-shadow` provisions a vLLM instance on ShadowPC OpenStack with cloud-init bootstrapping, health polling, and `switch-model.py gpu-shadow` integration. Interactive menu updated with ShadowPC as option 3.
- **`vllm-shadow` gateway provider** -- `openclaw.json.example` now declares a `vllm-shadow` provider block with `${VLLM_SHADOW_URL}` env substitution, parallel to `vllm-runpod`.
- **AWQ weight quantization support** -- `PodConfig.build_vllm_command()` now emits `--quantization`, `--enforce-eager`, and `--max-num-batched-tokens` flags when configured via `envVars`. `CatalogEntry` gains a `quantization` field.
- **DeepSeek R1 70B AWQ catalog entry** -- `config/model-catalog.json` adds `casperhansen/deepseek-r1-distill-llama-70b-awq` (70GB VRAM at AWQ vs 140GB at bf16), making 70B inference feasible on 2x A100-80GB with KV cache headroom.
- **HLK branding** -- Gateway control UI title set to "HLK Intelligence Platform" via `gateway.controlUi.title`. Agent identity blocks renamed to "HLK Orchestrator", "HLK Architect", etc. FastAPI title changed to "HLK Operations Platform". Placeholder HLK logo generated at `static/hlk-logo.png`.

[`docs/SOP.md`](docs/SOP.md) §9.1a; initiative [`07` **D6**](docs/wip/planning/07-hlk-neo4j-graph-projection/master-roadmap.md); Browser MCP evidence [`uat-graph-explorer-browser-20260415.md`](docs/wip/planning/07-hlk-neo4j-graph-projection/reports/uat-graph-explorer-browser-20260415.md). - **Neo4j Aura / TLS** — `get_neo4j_driver()` honors optional `NEO4J_TRUST` / `NEO4J_CA_BUNDLE` (`neo4j+s` → `neo4j+ssc` rewrite when trust is relaxed; ASCII-only warning logs for Windows consoles). With **`neo4j+ssc://` / `bolt+ssc://` already in `NEO4J_URI`**, redundant `NEO4J_TRUST=all` no longer emits a misleading warning. USER_GUIDE §9.10 prefers explicit `neo4j+ssc` when needed; `prod-cloud.env.example` notes omitting `NEO4J_TRUST` in that case. `scripts/browser-smoke.py` accepts **`AKOS_BROWSER_SMOKE_API_URL`** when the API runs on a non-default port.
